races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jaijai
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns foal, 155,000gns yearling; Hello Youmzain half-brother to five winners, notably Lone Peak (French/Spanish 7f-1m including 2yo; RPR 93); dam French 1m3f-12.5f scorer (including Listed; 98); market informative.
runners.comment: 155,000gns yearling; Hello Youmzain half-brother to five winners; market informative

runners.name: Trust Yourself
runners.spotlight: 42,000gns foal, 62,000gns yearling; by Showcasing out of 1m2f AW winning (RPR 94) half-sister to Listed scorers in Britain (7f) and Australia (1m4f); others have stronger-looking overall credentials.
runners.comment: 62,000gns yearling; by Showcasing; others have stronger overall credentials

runners.name: Victory Gold
runners.spotlight: Space Blues colt; dam French Listed-placed 1m3f/1m4f winner (RPR 100), half-sister to Ebor/November Handicap winner Litigant; first juvenile runner of the season for a stable that has a very good strike-rate with 2yos, latterly 3-5 with sole individual representative in 2025; much respected.
runners.comment: First juvenile runner of the season for a yard that has a very good strike-rate with 2yos

runners.name: King Of Montenegro
runners.spotlight: Presents a slight dilemma as he may put his Bath experience (5.7f, firm; fifth of seven) to good use but sets only an ordinary standard on that form, progress pending.
runners.comment: May put his Bath experience to good use but sets only an ordinary standard on that form

runners.name: Romanza
runners.spotlight: 160,000gns yearling; by Frankel out of 4.5f-1m winner (including 2yo/Australian Group 1; RPR 110); yard's 2yo runners this term feature a couple of debut scorers (SPs 5-1 and 18-1); reasonably interesting.
runners.comment: 160,000gns yearling; by Frankel; yard has had a couple of 2yo debut scorers this term

runners.name: Alfred Wallace
runners.spotlight: Attractively bred, with his sire Dubawi and dam/granddam Group 1 winners (predominantly over 1m) for his owner; mother's first offspring finished second last month on debut; likely to show major promise.
runners.comment: Attractively bred, with his sire Dubawi and dam/granddam Group 1 winners for his owner

runners.name: Cuban Heels
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling; by Havana Grey out of 1m 2yo winning (RPR 96) half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 scorer Sky Kingdom and daughter of NZ 1m Group 1 winner; major stable; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: 100,000gns yearling; by Havana Grey out of 1m 2yo winner; major stable; one to consider

runners.name: Keep Kicking On
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros foal, 85,000euros yearling; Persian Force half-brother to three winners, notably Threebagsfull (5f/6f including 2yo/Qatar; RPR 92); dam unplaced 6f 2yo (63); check the betting.
runners.comment: 85,000euros yearling; Persian Force half-brother to three winners; check the betting

runners.name: Night In Vegas
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns yearling; Lucky Vega half-brother to five winners, notably Shepherds Way (Listed-placed 5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 95); dam unraced half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Slade Power; market helpful.
runners.comment: 60,000gns yearling; Lucky Vega half-brother to five winners; market helpful

runners.name: Thunder Home
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder half-brother to 6f-8.6f winner Commander Of Life (including 2yo; RPR 86), out of 7f AW 2yo scorer (81); potentially the least forward, being a May foal who is effectively the youngest contender.
runners.comment: Potentially the least forward, being the youngest contender (May foal)

races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% British EBF Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Zgharta
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in 1m2f Newmarket maiden last May; the Sandringham over C&D last June was a brutal start to her handicap career but a strong-finishing fifth of 12 at Glorious Goodwood (1m, good) next time, when blocked in her run, suggested this mark was within reach; had excuses on last two starts and has a big run in her under favourable conditions.
runners.comment: Has won at 1m2f but a stiff 1m can suit and this mark isn't beyond her; can go well

runners.name: Song N Dance
runners.spotlight: Limited action so far but she ended 2025 in fine fettle, finding plenty to win a 7f Leicester novice (good) and was not at all hard pressed to win on handicap debut at Newcastle (1m, AW); useful comeback run when runner-up on Southwell AW in April and should come on for the outing; further improvement likely.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; progressive at 7f and 1m on turf and AW; has more to come; major player

runners.name: Jannas Journey
runners.spotlight: Scottish yard won a Group 1 at Royal Ascot from just two course runners in 2025; this one had a season to dream of last year, winning seven at 6f-1m, but only once at the latter trip; this stiff track will test her and she's 4lb higher than the latest win in September; tall order.
runners.comment: Had a season to dream of in 2025, winning 7; still 4lb higher than the latest in September

runners.name: Deedaydiva
runners.spotlight: Just three runs but her Sandown win (7f, soft) on second start turned out very useful form with the second doing really well after and the next three also successful since; her comeback third in a Nottingham novice (about 1m, good) was perfectly satisfactory; interesting newcomer to handicaps.
runners.comment: Just 3 runs; Sandown 7f win was good form; satisfactory return in April; handicap debut

runners.name: Radiant Beauty
runners.spotlight: Progressive in 2025, coming right away for a 1m Redcar win on yard debut before following up on Kempton AW (7f); races freely and saw too much daylight at Newmarket (1m, good) in October but, hanging on well for third, she lost nothing in defeat; up against it in a Listed AW race at Kempton in March but it was a good return; has another handicap in her.
runners.comment: Progressive in 2025, latterly for this yard; pleasing return in AW Listed race; solid

runners.name: Politely
runners.spotlight: Complete turnaround on two modest turf runs when upped to about 1m on AW, well clear at Lingfield then winning with something to spare on handicap debut at Wolverhampton recently; up 6lb but she can rise further.
runners.comment: Progressive since upped to 1m on AW; up 6lb for ready handicap debut win; can rise further

runners.name: Electrifarhh
runners.spotlight: Won on second and final 2yo start (1m, AW); seen only twice since but had a very tough task in attempting to give weight to a subsequent big improver at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last May and wasn't seen to best advantage over 7f on Kempton AW in March; could well play a part back at 1m.
runners.comment: AW winner at 1m as 2yo; just 2 runs since, tough tasks each time; improve for return to 1m

runners.name: Alobayyah
runners.spotlight: Outclassed rivals on sole 2yo start, over 1m at Yarmouth (soft); had a much stiffer task when returning in a Group 3 last August, where she was struck into, but she was nowhere near justifying short odds on Newcastle AW (1m2f) in October; goes handicapping with potential but queries.
runners.comment: Impressive 2yo debut; two runs in 2025, highly tried then disappointing; handicap debut

runners.name: Bella Perla
runners.spotlight: No impact in sole turf start for the Crisfords, on handicap debut last September, but she fared much better over 1m on AW since, winning two of her last three starts; subsequently sold for 35,000gns; this may be needed.
runners.comment: Better for step up to 1m on AW in 2025, winning two of three; might need this for new yard
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs P Whight And T Clover

runners.name: Quamby
runners.spotlight: Sparingly raced since 2yo debut and not yet off the mark but she put in a spirited late dash in a valuable fillies' handicap on Lingfield AW (1m2f) in December, close behind the winner who was ahead of her mark; acts just as well on turf but has other unexposed types to contend with today.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden; good handicap debut over 1m2f on AW in December; tricky again

races.race_name: Carey Group Victoria Cup (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Storm Free
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has a largely solid record and is 2-4 on turf; gelded prior to respectable effort in big field at Newmarket on seasonal debut, taking form figures since upped to 7f to 216; may still have more to offer; big run looks plausible.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who has a largely solid record and may still have more to offer

runners.name: Great Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, including in first-time blinkers at Glorious Goodwood; close third at Haydock (7f) with reappearance under his belt, paving the way nicely for a crack at this prize; has made the frame in both C&D attempts; possibilities in new headgear (visor).
runners.comment: Gained one of his wins in first-time blinkers; has C&D form; possibilities in new headgear

runners.name: Shafdar
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who is 2-5, all at about 1m on AW/turf, this year for new stable; prevailed at Epsom (good) returned to this sphere most recently, despite taking a good hold; may settle better and show further progress in this big-field scenario over a stiff 7f; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo who is 2-5 for new yard; may show further progress; not ruled out

runners.name: Mudbir
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt whose record of 3-6 overall and 2-3 in handicaps (7f) features a narrow win at Glorious Goodwood; hindered by traffic issues at same venue when last seen (effort best forgiven); cheekpieces worn that day are retained; beat Defence Minister by half a length at Sandown last July; still open to further progress and remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Well bred; record is 3-6 overall and 2-3 in handicaps; still open to further progress

runners.name: Morte Point
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 12110 (turf/AW) since upped to 7f, the most recent effort perhaps excusable as it was his seasonal debut and he raced a lot further back than usual; faces no easy task at this level but may resume his improvement with the reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Record of 3-5 since upped to 7f; reappearance effort is excusable; may do better still

runners.name: Defence Minister
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who was gelded prior to justifying favouritism with 1l success at Kempton (7f) on AW/seasonal debut, opening his handicap account and taking overall record to 3-9; this Wathnan Racing runner may well be capable of further improvement; ties in closely with Mudbir on Sandown running last July; major contender.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to AW success on reappearance; may well improve further; major contender

runners.name: Ten Pounds
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive for previous yard but proved disappointing in this race last year, having raced freely; ran well in the Wokingham at Royal Ascot next time; not particularly solid on his 2026 form for new stable and it remains to be seen whether he settles returned to this C&D.
runners.comment: Disappointing in this race last year; not particularly solid on 2026 form for new yard

runners.name: Khafiz
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who ties in with Great Acclaim on a stable/seasonal debut effort at Haydock that took his form figures over 7f to 1135; may resume progress with that outing under his belt; one to consider, especially if the market signals speak positively.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who may resume progress with Haydock reappearance under his belt

runners.name: So Darn Hot
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 6f, winning four times for previous yard; 2-5 on grass; mixed results on AW for new stable; ran respectably in sole 7f attempt but chance of landing this hot handicap partly depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of landing this hot handicap partly depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Tribal Chief
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper at about 1m, winning five times; did well to finish third in the Lincoln at Doncaster in sole outing this term, having come from a difficult position; each-way possibilities, provided he copes with the drop back to 7f for first time since his early days.
runners.comment: Useful handicapper at about 1m; each-way hopes, provided he copes with the return to 7f

runners.name: Nikovo
runners.spotlight: Registered last three wins on Newcastle AW and ran respectably there on finals day; came up well short in the Spring Cup returned to turf last time and remains on a difficult mark in this sphere; sole handicap success on grass was off 13lb lower.
runners.comment: Came up well short back on turf last time; remains on a difficult mark in this sphere

runners.name: Witch Hunter
runners.spotlight: Very useful but rather needs the cards to drop right and gained his only turf wins, namely in Royal Ascot handicap and Newbury Group 3, in 2023; peak AW form this year when scoring in January; finished seventh (off 4lb higher) in this contest 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Very useful but rather needs the cards to drop right and gained his only turf wins in 2023

runners.name: Popmaster
runners.spotlight: Campaigned on AW since December, proving consistent until coming up a tad short on finals day last time; has loads of Ascot form but three defeats in this race (finishing positions 074) suggest he can be opposed, especially now aged eight.
runners.comment: Has lots of Ascot form but three defeats in this race suggest he can be opposed

runners.name: Humam
runners.spotlight: Gained his final win for previous yard and sole placed effort for current stable over 7f; overall record at this trip is 2-5; gelded prior to never landing a blow in another 6f event on seasonal debut; could go well, with this step back up in distance a plausible plus.
runners.comment: Could go well, with this step back up to 7f a plausible plus; 2-5 over this distance

runners.name: Completely Random
runners.spotlight: Looks well treated on this year's AW form but failed to transfer it back to turf (in Group 3) last time when bidding for a 6f hat-trick, and still has a weaker strike-rate in this sphere; bit to prove back at 7f and doesn't look the percentage call.
runners.comment: Failed to transfer his AW improvement back to turf last time when bidding for 6f hat-trick

runners.name: Serengeti
runners.spotlight: Successful in autumn handicap on final start for Aidan O'Brien; far from solid on his form for three different yards since, most recently posting a duck egg in the Spring Cup on debut for this one; best watched.
runners.comment: Duck egg in the Spring Cup last time (debut for another new yard); best watched

runners.name: The Wizard Of Eye
runners.spotlight: Seen only twice, both times on AW, last term for new yard; made a winning reappearance off current mark in this contest (on good ground) two years ago on first start during a brief spell with Charlie Fellowes; fighting chance in repeat bid, assuming he's in top form.
runners.comment: Won this race off current mark in 2024 for Charlie Fellowes; third start for new yard
runners.owner: R Humphrey O Humphrey A Favell

runners.name: Shiplake
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 62743 on turf, mostly over 6f; impressed at Southwell in last two appearances, taking record for new yard to 2-3, strike-rate over 7f to 2-4 and AW score to 3-5; interesting contender, assuming he can transfer the progress back to this sphere.
runners.comment: Impressed at Southwell (AW) the last twice; interesting assuming he's as good back on turf

runners.name: Jordan Electrics
runners.spotlight: Veteran who had raced only over sprint distances until a resurgent effort (second of 18) upped to 7f at Newmarket last time; goes back up in class and seems unlikely to land a Victoria Cup aged ten; gained last five wins, all in 2024, at Hamilton.
runners.comment: Resurgent effort upped to 7f last time but is unlikely to land a Victoria Cup aged ten
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Jumby
runners.spotlight: Two-time Group winner over 7f but is on a near three-year losing spell and current mark reflects that he's not the force of old (admittedly showed bits of useful form in 2025); needs to step up on reappearance effort; failed to shine in the last two runnings of this contest.
runners.comment: On a long losing spell and has failed to shine in the last two runnings of this contest

runners.name: Royal Zabeel
runners.spotlight: Ties in with Golden Mind on Musselburgh running but the pair of them are facing a stiffer assignment in this major prize; remains useful on turf but a lower mark in this sphere reflects that his peak form, namely dual Listed honours, has been shown on AW.
runners.comment: Useful on turf but a lower mark in this sphere reflects that his peak form is on AW

runners.name: Yorkshire
runners.spotlight: Effective on turf but still has a better strike-rate on AW and ended his rather truncated 2025 campaign with defeats (admittedly not entirely disgraced) in major handicaps over this C&D, finishing sixth then ninth; needs to raise his game on reappearance.
runners.comment: Still has a better stirke-rate on AW than on turf; needs to raise his game on reappearance

runners.name: Aalto
runners.spotlight: Good 7f handicapper but has achieved his peak RPRs in the last two runnings of the Bunbury Cup, winning in 2024 and going very close in 2025; may be more interesting when going for a third attempt at Newmarket in July; his Ascot record isn't convincing.
runners.comment: Achieved his peak RPRs in the last two runnings of the Bunbury Cup; others preferred

runners.name: Golden Mind
runners.spotlight: Good second at Musselburgh (7f) with reappearance under his belt but is by no means certain to go one better off 103 in this deep field; has gained all handicap wins off marks in in the high-90s and still needs to prove he can break the triple-digit barrier.
runners.comment: Good second last time but this is harder; yet to win off a mark as high as current one

runners.name: Hickory
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a win and second-place finish, enhancing his good record at Ascot; two attempts at this race comprise a creditable fourth in 2024 and narrow success last year; impossible to dismiss, despite being an 8yo who is on another new career-high mark.
runners.comment: Two attempts at this prize comprise a creditable fourth in 2024 and narrow success in 2025

runners.name: Star Anthem
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-10, the success in 2yo maiden; has bits of useful form, including at Group level, but his handicap record is rather unconvincing; chance of doubling his tally partly depends on the effects of two physical tweaks (had gelding/wind operations since last run).
runners.comment: Chance partly depends on the effects of two physical tweaks (gelding/wind operations)

runners.name: Nostrum
runners.spotlight: Very promising early in career for his first stable but broadly hasn't lived up to expectations and his form went markedly awry last term for a second yard; big turnaround is required on debut for his third trainer; dismissed unless the betting speaks positively.
runners.comment: Form went markedly awry last term; makes debut for another new stable

runners.name: Pellitory
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-14 for James Owen, reaching a useful standard but proving rather difficult to place after the third success ruined his mark; sold for 52,000gns and gelded since last run; probably still too high in the weights for this level of competition.
runners.comment: Probably still too high in the weights for this level of competition; stable debut

runners.name: Houquetot
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner in France as a 2yo; below best figures in AW handicap and Meydan Group 2 during the winter for David O'Meara; needs to prove he retains full ability and, unless the betting speaks favourably, is best watched on debut for another new yard.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he retains full ability; makes debut for another new stable

races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% Italy Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Valedictory
runners.spotlight: All the rage in the betting but found the line coming just too soon for his strong finish when second (Bulletin close up in third) of 14 to Rathgar (won again since) on 1m2f Newbury handicap debut and reappearance three weeks ago; that first experience of a big-field handicap should aid his development and he left the strong impression 1m4f will suit; leading claims with further improvement highly.
runners.comment: Big eyecatcher on 1m2f Newbury handicap debut; the one to beat up at 1m4f

runners.name: Gordon Grey
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life when winning four in a row for Ben Brookhouse last summer, namely a maiden hurdle followed by three Flat handicaps over 1m2f and 1m4f (twice) on good/good to firm; better effort in Bahrain for George Baker this year when third of seven over 1m2f (good to firm) five weeks ago; returns to Britain on a career-high mark and may prove vulnerable to less-exposed sorts.
runners.comment: In career-best form last year; third in Bahrain latest but up against unexposed sorts now

runners.name: Bulletin
runners.spotlight: Both wins on AW but very good third (Valedictory second) of 14 to Rathgar (won again since) on 1m2f Newbury reappearance proves he's equally as effective on turf; stays 1m4f and likely to be in the shake-up again if in similar form.
runners.comment: Very good second (Valedictory third) on 1m2f Newbury return; stays 1m4f; should go well

runners.name: Classical Allusion
runners.spotlight: Won Southwell maiden (1m4f, AW) on debut last autumn and best of the rest behind superstar hurdler Constitution Hill in Kempton novice (1m4f again) on his return in March; on the back of that he was sent off 11-10 favourite for his 2m Newbury handicap and turf debut three weeks ago but pulled too hard; retains potential back down in trip, but the outside stall has the potential to make life awkward.
runners.comment: Promise over 1m4f on AW first two starts; too keen over 2m since; retains potential

runners.name: Opportunity
runners.spotlight: Edged out St Leger runner-up Rahiebb in Haydock novice (11.6f, good) last spring; badly hampered when well-held fourth to Amiloc in a Listed race at Goodwood (1m3f) next time and not too hard to forgive a heavy defeat behind that same rival in the King Edward VII over C&D at the Royal meeting (good to firm); off since (gelded); a very interesting handicap newcomer, particularly if the betting speaks in his favour.
runners.comment: Unexposed handicap newcomer from top yard; market confidence could prove significant

runners.name: Whatsgoingonmarvin
runners.spotlight: A useful 1m5f Flat winner for Sir Mark Prescott last year; only fair form at best in four hurdle outings for this yard since, including when well-held sixth on Stratford handicap debut in that sphere four weeks ago; returns to the Flat with something to prove; first-time cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Bit disappointing over hurdles for this yard ahead of Flat return in headgear

runners.name: Serenity Blue
runners.spotlight: Pair of 1m2f novice wins on good to firm at the start of last summer; disappointed in handicaps on final two starts last year but got his career back on track when third of nine in 1m4f Kempton handicap on reappearance 33 days ago; subsequently gelded; surprise if he's reached his limit after six starts.
runners.comment: Two 1m2f turf novice wins at 3; solid third in 1m4f AW handicap on return; gelded after

runners.name: Too Soon
runners.spotlight: Largely in good nick last season but winless since 2024 and well held on 1m2f Doncaster reappearance (good to soft) six weeks ago.
runners.comment: No win since 2024 and well beaten on reappearance

runners.name: Hermetic
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground winner (1m4f/2m) in France for Christophe Ferland last autumn; new connections picked him up for 105,000euros in November; no surprise were he to develop into another very useful handicapper for this yard but perhaps best watched this time unless the betting speaks in his favour.
runners.comment: Dual soft-ground winner in France; joined good yard but might be best watched this time

runners.name: In The Breeze
runners.spotlight: Won at Pontefract (1m4f, good to soft) for Mick Appleby last September and bounced back from below-par efforts to end 2025 when making a successful start for the Henrietta Knight stable over 1m4f at Thirsk (good) four weeks ago; did that readily but this looks significantly tougher than that five-runner affair.
runners.comment: Winning start for Henrietta Knight at Thirsk last month but this is a much stronger race

runners.name: Siempre Arturo
runners.spotlight: The winner of eight of his 22 starts, the latest success coming at Kempton (1m4f) off a 2lb lower mark; ended 2025 with a couple of lesser runs and might be best watched on return.
runners.comment: Has healthy 8-22 record but below par at end of 2025 and might be best watched on return

runners.name: Wine Dark Sea
runners.spotlight: Winning handicap debut at Kempton (1m4f, AW) last August; didn't fire in the same way back at Kempton (1m3f) next time but resumed his progression in no uncertain terms when close second in valuable 3yo handicap at Newmarket (1m4f, good) on final start, form which was strongly boosted last weekend by the winner (Yabher) and third (Daiquiri Bay); could have a big say if the betting suggests he's ready to roll after 211 days off.
runners.comment: Form of Newmarket second on final 3yo start was strongly boosted last weekend; player

runners.name: Al Wasl Storm
runners.spotlight: Came up well short at Group level last summer but resumed his progression back in calmer waters with a 1m4f Newbury handicap debut success (good to firm) last August; this low-mileage sort has the potential for better again in 2026.
runners.comment: Resumed progression with 1m4f Newbury handicap win last August; more to come in 2026

runners.name: Boatswain
runners.spotlight: His progress stalled at the end of last season but returns as a still relatively unexposed 4yo who could kick on again in 2026; betting informative.
runners.comment: Returns as a still relatively unexposed 4yo who could kick on again in 2026
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Kirchner
runners.spotlight: Showed useful form across five starts in France for Jean-Claude Rouget but not seen since finishing fourth over 1m4f just under a year ago; changed hands for 72,000euros last July; has joined an excellent stable for his British career but an opening mark of 92 isn't obviously generous; has been gelded and now equipped with first-time cheekpieces; the betting should provide more clues.
runners.comment: Useful French recruit for James Owen but off for nearly a year; the betting should guide

races.race_name: Royal Ascot Local Schools Art Competition Handicap

runners.name: Sintra
runners.spotlight: Has advanced his RPR on all three starts in maiden/novice events, finishing 4l third to the useful Del Maro at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) four weeks ago; another in this line-up who likely has more to offer now moving into a handicap.
runners.comment: Promise in maiden/novice events and likely has more to offer in handicaps

runners.name: Waterford Castle
runners.spotlight: Now 0-6 but he's posted progressive RPRs when runner-up on all three starts since joining George Boughey; the form of his latest 1m2f Newmarket second has been boosted by subsequent wins for the third and fourth (the winner also a creditable third in a Listed race); if he can pull out only a little more for the addition of cheekpieces he could take a bit of stopping.
runners.comment: Progressive when second all three runs for George Boughey; headgear on; leading claims

runners.name: Devil's Peak
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden but he looked unlucky not to collect on his 1m3f Kempton reappearance in April, just failing have been denied a clear run; has looked a little ungainly on occasions but he's talented enough to win races.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden but looked unlucky on 1m3f Kempton reappearance.

runners.name: Enemy Agent
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience but kept on well to collar the useful Generic close home on his 1m Kempton debut in March; only sixth to the same rival at Yarmouth (1m again) 17 days later but retains potential now handicapping; wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Beat useful sort on AW debut; only sixth next time but retains potential; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Sassicaia
runners.spotlight: Had plenty to do turning for home but picked up well to make a winning debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in December; bettered that form when fourth under a penalty in a stronger race at Southwell (1m) four months later; his pedigree suggests he should benefit from this longer trip on handicap and turf debut; likely improver.
runners.comment: Raced on AW; won on debut and better form when fourth since; likely improver in handicaps

races.race_name: Mariner Systems Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Naga
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 firmly on the up with a pair of 1m handicap wins last October and improved again to complete the hat-trick in 1m2f Doncaster handicap (good to firm) on reappearance a fortnight ago; raised 8lb but there should be more to come; leading stable won this with a 4yo in the Strawbridge silks last season.
runners.comment: Completed a hat-trick on 1m2f reappearance and top yard won this race last year

runners.name: Room Fourteen
runners.spotlight: Big step forward when making a winning handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) in a first-time hood (retained) 18 days ago, edging out a next-time-out winner with the pair 3.5l clear; a 6lb rise may not stop her with further progress likely.
runners.comment: Clear with a next-time-out winner on 1m2f handicap debut; definitely more to come

runners.name: Ruby Wedding
runners.spotlight: Isn't going to be up to justifying her Oaks entry but it suggests her top yard thinks a bit of her and she has to be of interest setting out in handicaps from a mark in the mid-70s.
runners.comment: Oaks entry is ambitious but suggests she's well regarded; interesting handicap newcomer

runners.name: Venetia
runners.spotlight: Progressive form in 1m maiden/novice events last year; good chance that trend may continue now handicapping over a longer trip which is bred to suit.
runners.comment: Progressive over 1m last year and may be more to come handicapping up in trip

runners.name: Lady Ridgewood
runners.spotlight: 1m2f AW maiden winner last August on her final start for Peter Chapple-Hyam but always behind in 1m3f Southwell handicap on her recent return for Ian Williams; can only be watched after that.
runners.comment: AW maiden win last year but always behind on recent return for new yard

runners.name: Tryst
runners.spotlight: Winner of an AW novice over the extended 1m at Wolverhampton last autumn; beaten at short odds on both starts since but recent handicap debut second back at Wolverhampton was creditable and she remains one who could have a bigger performance in her; steps up in trip for her return to turf.
runners.comment: AW novice win; beaten at short odds twice since but retains potential now up in trip

runners.name: Bint Kilfrush
runners.spotlight: Justified short-priced favouritism in 1m novice at Southwell on New Year's Day; matched that RPR when fifth under a penalty at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) four weeks ago; the second and third haven't done much for that form since but she still brings unexposed potential to this handicap debut.
runners.comment: AW debut win; similar form when fifth on turf since; handicap debut

runners.name: Moon Angel
runners.spotlight: Second win for Stuart Williams when getting up on the line over 1m6f at Newmarket (good to firm) last August; not seen since, though, and possibly best watched racing short of 1m3f for the first time on her return.
runners.comment: Winner over 1m4f/1m6f last year but might be best watched down in trip on return
runners.owner: Mrs S Goddard And Partner I

runners.name: Nanino Niyati
runners.spotlight: Won a fast-ground Salisbury novice over this trip for Kublers last summer; remote third in a soft-ground handicap and down the field in a Listed race on her final two outings last year; the betting is perhaps the best guide to expectations on return for new trainer (same owner).
runners.comment: Turf novice winner but well held in handicap/Listed race final two starts; with a new yard

races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% Blue Handicap

runners.name: Rage Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Not without his quirks but he has plenty of ability for one of his rating and everything clicked in a Class 6 handicap at Salisbury last June (6f, good to firm); three second placings followed and he should be sharper with last week's return to action under his belt; the start will remain a concern but he is not discounted back at 6f.
runners.comment: Talented but quirky and the starts are an issue; sharper for recent return; not ruled out

runners.name: Dicko The Legend
runners.spotlight: Just missed out at Southwell ten days ago (6f), finishing off his race strongly; 1lb higher mark and stronger opposition to contend with but his proven record in big-field events (first of 21, first of 22 and fourth of 24 on the three occasions he's run in fields of 16+) bodes well.
runners.comment: Near miss on AW ten days ago; seems well suited to big fields and should make a bold bid
runners.owner: Mrs S Hibbert, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: The Bitter Moose
runners.spotlight: Back to his best with a front-running 7f win at Epsom last August; creditable fourth to Arantes Nascimento on his return to action last month; not much room for manoeuvre off this mark and he is yet to win at 6f.
runners.comment: Made all for an easy win at Epsom (7f) last summer; fair return last month; needs more

runners.name: Thunderous Love
runners.spotlight: The winner of five 6f handicaps last year, beating a few reopposing rivals in the process; only midfield at Leicester on last month's return but the stable form is a positive and she could take a step forward.
runners.comment: Five 6f handicap wins last year; yard in form; can leave reappearance behind her

runners.name: Arantes Nascimento
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for Michael Attwater when leading late on in a 7f handicap at Kempton last month; 3lb rise not insurmountable and he has run well over 6f in the past but this is a step up in class.
runners.comment: Off the mark for new yard with good 7f win last month; up in class and down in trip today

runners.name: Neyva's Angel
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a 6f handicap at Southwell last month; finished behind Dicko The Legend when fifth back at Southwell ten days ago; improvement essential but the booking of Oisin Murphy catches the eye.
runners.comment: Finished behind Dicko The Legend at Southwell 10 days ago; Oisin Murphy a positive booking

runners.name: Serenity Dream
runners.spotlight: Chance on this winter's AW best but he failed to fire back on turf at Goodwood last week (5f); return to 6f can help but others are stronger.
runners.comment: Chance on this winter's AW best but he was well beaten back on turf last week

runners.name: Azure Zain
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a handicap and he finished down the field on his seasonal return at Southwell in February; down in the weights but he has more to prove than many of these.
runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap at he was well beaten on his seasonal return in February; not solid

runners.name: Hectic
runners.spotlight: Back to his last winning mark and his first two runs in 2026 were respectable; eligible for weaker races than this and easy enough to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: On a winning mark but usually seen at a lower level; easy enough to look elsewhere

runners.name: True Promise
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in ten starts for current stable but he has been placed in seven of them, including back on turf at Wetherby 13 days ago (5.5f, good to firm); should run his race but this looks a tough race in which to end his losing streak.
runners.comment: Reliable for new yard but losing run stands at 26 and this may not be his day either

runners.name: Daring Legend
runners.spotlight: Ran well on his stable debut at Kempton (6f) in March but he was down the field, albeit in Class 3 company, at Newbury three weeks ago; now tongue tied; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Promising stable debut but well beaten at Newbury last time (Class 3); now tongue tied

runners.name: Tokyo Joe
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt when winning a 6f novice at Lingfield (AW) in December; upped 4lb for that but he is unexposed as a sprinter and he makes more appeal than many.
runners.comment: Two good runs since dropped to 6f; still has potential as a sprinter; shortlisted

runners.name: Lord Roxby
runners.spotlight: Generally performed with great credit last season, winning a 6f handicap at Hamilton last May off 2lb lower and ending his campaign with a second of nine at Newmarket; never in contention at Beverley on his return to action 16 days ago (7.4f, good to firm) and likely best watched for a yard having a quiet year.
runners.comment: Reliable in 2025 but yard having a quiet year and he never featured on his reappearance
runners.owner: N Skinner D Jenkins J Glover

runners.name: Juan Les Pins
runners.spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run at Newcastle in March and added to his tally with a Thirsk win last month (6f, good); better form when a close third in a Class 3 at Newbury three weeks ago and a 1lb rise is manageable; conditions won't be an issue having finished third in a Wokingham in his prime; high on the list.
runners.comment: Two wins this year and showed better form when third at Newbury last time; good chance

runners.name: Waistcoat
runners.spotlight: His two wins for Ross Burdon have come at a lower level; disappointed at Kempton when last seen and although he is relatively unexposed at 6f, he will need a clear personal best to take this after 108 days off.
runners.comment: Relatively unexposed at 6f but below par last time and perhaps vulnerable back up in class

runners.name: Twilight Jet
runners.spotlight: Smart in his prime; not the same force now but the pick of his 6f form on turf and AW in 2025 would give him a good shout; others are safer all the same.
runners.comment: On the downgrade but handicapped accordingly; others bring stronger claims

runners.name: Cajetan
runners.spotlight: Far from straightforward and his best form has come on AW; two good runs and three poor runs since joining Ruth Carr, his latest Newcastle fourth (blinkered first time) falling into the former category; not the most solid option but he is better than this level on his day and it is always worth a second look when Billy Loughnane is booked for the stable (four wins and four seconds from 22 rides).
runners.comment: Best form has been on AW but well handicapped & Billy Loughnane a good booking; e-w shout

runners.name: Cressida Wildes
runners.spotlight: Ran out the ready winner of a 5f handicap at Wolverhampton in February but she failed to back it up at Chelmsford ten days later; she is effective on turf but looks up against it in this field on her return from a 72-day break.
runners.comment: 5f AW win in February but absent since a lesser run ten days later; others more convincing

runners.name: Fantasy Master
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a 6f win at Chelmsford in December; 2lb higher mark and stronger opposition might find him out back from five months off.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a Chelmsford win (6f; Class 5); up in grade and may prove vulnerable

races.race_name: William Hill Epic Boosts Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Twisting Physics
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced, progressive 5yo; made all for narrow win at Newbury (7f) last June and shaped encouragingly upped to 1m2f back there (also good), racing freely but keeping on well from a poor draw on reappearance three weeks ago; retains plenty of potential.
runners.comment: Lightly raced, progressive 5yo who made eye-catching return three weeks ago; big player

runners.name: Double Red
runners.spotlight: Hit the ground running last year, winning twice at Nottingham in April, over 8.5f (good) and 1m2f (good to firm); also scored at Epsom (good to soft) in September and once again made a winning return when picking up an AW handicap here (1m2f) 19 days ago; up 5lb and this is tougher but he's progressive and can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Fourth win when scoring on return; up 5lb and this is tougher but he's progressive

runners.name: Morcar
runners.spotlight: Close third in this in 2024 and he enhanced a good C&D record when winning last year's edition (good to firm) from this mark; placed on his next four starts and had a soft-ground excuse on final start that term; had wind surgery ahead of a quiet comeback at Newbury three weeks ago but no surprise if this day has been ringed in his calendar.
runners.comment: Good C&D record; won this last year; looks sure to be primed for this race again

runners.name: Sweet Reward
runners.spotlight: Registered his seventh win when scoring on reappearance at Newbury (1m2f, good) last year and he attracted support before finishing second to an unexposed sort at Epsom (8.5f, good) on recent comeback this time; might not come on a bundle for that but this trip is more suitable and he's in with an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Might not improve for comeback second (8.5f) but this trip is more suitable; each-way shot

runners.name: Salamanca City
runners.spotlight: 3-12 on turf (all 1m2f), the third of those wins coming when she scraped home at Yarmouth (good) on her reappearance four weeks ago; 3lb nudge puts her on a career-high mark.
runners.comment: 3-12 on turf (all 1m2f); scraped home on return and needs more from career-high mark

runners.name: Warning Sign
runners.spotlight: Won two amateur riders' races last year but not competitive in either start this term; suspicion he'll come back to life returned to those earlier events when owner David Dunsdon is back in the saddle.
runners.comment: Won two amateur riders' races last year; of more interest when returned to that sphere

runners.name: Noble Horizon
runners.spotlight: Consistent for William Haggas and he made all to land Leicester novice (1m2f, good to firm) in July; signed off for that yard with creditable third of 19 at York (10.5f, good; not clear run) off this mark in October; secured for 140,000gns a fortnight later and subsequently gelded; a player on stable debut/reappearance.
runners.comment: Bought for 140,000gns after good 3rd of 19 (10.5f, good; not clear run) in October; player

runners.name: Towerlands
runners.spotlight: Improved once returned to AW and bagged a hat-trick across October and December (1m); on the scoresheet again upped to 9.5f at Wolverhampton (career-best form) in February; form took a slight dip on last two starts, while his earlier turf form isn't comparable so interesting to see how he fares back on grass.
runners.comment: Hat-trick once back on AW; chance depends on what he makes of this return to turf

runners.name: Cristo
runners.spotlight: Went back-to-back over 1m2f on Chelmsford's AW around this time last year; winless since and latest eighth of ten at Ripon (1m2f, good to soft) didn't suggest he's about to capitalise on a declining mark; a handicapping case can be made, though.
runners.comment: Handicapping case can be made but latest effort didn't suggest he's about to capitalise

runners.name: Youthful King
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m2f (good to firm) although that was back in 2022/23 and his last success came over 1m6f at Sandown in July; tailed off on final start last term and two runs (1m4f) this season contained little encouragement.
runners.comment: Tailed off on final start last term and two runs (1m4f) this season weren't encouraging

races.race_name: William Hill Oaks Trial Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Romantic Symphony
runners.spotlight: Sister to winners including Yibir (7f-2m including US Grade 1; RPR 123) and Wild Illusion (1m/1m2f including Group 1, also Oaks second; 116); 10-11 when getting the better of a pretty useful rival at Newmarket (1m, good) last September, preceding another novice win at much shorter odds in good style for six-runner race at Kempton (1m3f, AW) one month ago; lots of potential.
runners.comment: 2-2 in novice events at Newmarket (1m, good) and Kempton (1m3f, AW); lots of potential

runners.name: Amora Queen
runners.spotlight: Won last summer on her first two starts, before failing to beat a rival in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket (33-1); second of six when favourite for a handicap at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) two weeks ago put her back on the right track and she's likely to stay; it's also the best RPR in this line-up but it would still be surprising if she's not vulnerable.
runners.comment: Last in Group 1 Fillies' Mile; 2nd in 1m2f handicap at Doncaster is the best RPR in this

runners.name: Bloom
runners.spotlight: By Wootton Bassett out of a 9.5f Listed winner who's sister to Irish Guineas winner Roderic O'Connor; won a maiden at Cork (1m, yielding) last September and probably improved again when front-running third of seven in Group 3 at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding) two weeks ago; top trainer so often gets these 3yo middle-distance fillies to improve.
runners.comment: Front-running third of seven in Group 3 at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding) two weeks ago
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Prizeland
runners.spotlight: Sister to 2022 Derby second Hoo Ya Mal; made an eye-catching debut (good to soft) before her novice win at Kempton (1m, AW) later in October; 4-7 for another novice here (1m4f, AW) last month and easily dismissed her only three rivals in slowly run race; faces radically different opposition today and returns to turf but she's promising, for sure.
runners.comment: Won AW novice events over 1m and 1m4f; radically different opposition but she's promising

runners.name: Cameo
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett half-sister to a 1m4f Listed winner; won 7f maiden at the Curragh before below-form favourite in a 1m Group 3 later in October; fifth of ten in another 1m Group 3 there on reappearance was her best form and suggested she's a middle-distance filly; those last three runs were in the mud, which is most unlikely here, but another step forward otherwise looks assured; Ryan Moore rides her for the first time.
runners.comment: Reappearance fifth in 1m Curragh Group 3 was her best form; looks a middle-distance filly
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

races.race_name: William Hill Lingfield Derby Trial Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Maho Bay
runners.spotlight: Hot favourite when winning with great authority in five-runner races for a maiden at Kempton (1m3f, AW; led 2f out) in December and novice event at Newmarket (1m2f, good; made all) in April, beating O'Brien and Gosden runners in the latter; Dubawi colt with speed on the dam's side but he's won over 1m3f already and has made a striking start to his career.
runners.comment: He's made a striking 2-2 start to his career with wins in a 1m3f maiden and 1m2f novice

runners.name: Maltese Cross
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut at Ascot (1m, soft) and beat notable rivals in a Newmarket maiden (1m, good) later in September; favourite for a valuable novice event at Newbury (1m2f, good) three weeks ago and battled his way to another narrow win, in a blanket finish but there's a good chance he will come on from that run and be suited by this longer trip (very much supported by pedigree); firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Good chance he will come on from recent Newbury win and be suited by this longer trip

runners.name: Isaac Newton
runners.spotlight: Camelot colt; maiden winner at Glorious Goodwood and best form when staying on from off the pace to take fourth in Group 1 Criterium de Saint-Cloud (1m2f, very soft) in October; shaped as if he needed the race when fourth of seven from the back in Listed event at Newmarket (1m1f, good) on return, and this is a different trip, so it's far too early to write him off.
runners.comment: Fourth in French Group 1 (1m2f, very soft) in October; shaped as if needing race on return

runners.name: Balzac
runners.spotlight: Winning siblings include Claymore, a Group 3 winner at 1m2f for this yard; third to Maltese Cross in a 1m Newmarket maiden on sole 2yo start; evens when making all in a Kempton novice (1m, AW) on return and his rallying third of six in Listed race at Epsom (1m2f, good) suggested that this longer trip would suit; some others have better form but he can improve.
runners.comment: Rallying third of six in 1m2f Listed race at Epsom suggested this longer trip would suit

runners.name: A Taste Of Glory
runners.spotlight: It was a French Group 1 (1m2f, very soft) which saw his progress arrested as a 2yo; his improved form to win a six-runner handicap here (1m2f, AW) on reappearance came by a neck off a mark of just 80 and although the runner-up has won twice since, this race clearly demands big improvement.
runners.comment: 1m2f win here (AW) came by a neck off a mark of just 80 but the second has won twice since

runners.name: Bay Of Brilliance
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to smart Flat and hurdle winner Absurde; odds-on winner of maiden at Goodwood (1m2f, soft) and novice at Redcar (1m1f, good to soft) on last two starts as 2yo, in the latter making all and stretching 8l clear; needs a good deal better in this company but he's clearly promising.
runners.comment: 1m2f/1m1f winner as 2yo, latterly making all and stretching 8l clear in a Redcar novice

races.race_name: William Hill Chartwell Fillies' Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Copacabana Sands
runners.spotlight: Seems tactically versatile; hooded when winning Naas Listed event (good) last May and followed up in Leopardstown Group 3 (also 7f, soft) the following month; left Michael O'Callaghan (same owner) after disappointing on heavy ground in October; Ryan Moore booked and she sets the standard on this stable debut/reappearance; plenty to like.
runners.comment: Won Leopardstown Group 3 in June; sets the standard on first start for Andrew Balding

runners.name: Royal Velvet
runners.spotlight: Progressive mare who scored for the eighth time when beating 18-runner field at Newmarket (7f, good) at last month's Craven meeting; might need some luck in running if the field gravitates to the stands' side but thoroughly deserves this first crack at a Pattern race, with the ceiling of her ability probably yet to be reached.
runners.comment: 8-time winner who aims to progress out of handicaps; ceiling of ability yet to be reached

runners.name: La Brodeuse
runners.spotlight: Ex-French filly who didn't run as 2yo; won at Chantilly (1m, soft) on her debut last May; creditable effort despite not staying 1m2f at Listed level next time and she left Andre Fabre for 125,000euros after midfield finish in Saint-Cloud Listed race (1m, heavy) in November; unraced on good or quicker ground but not fully exposed and interesting to see how she goes in the market on this stable debut and first start at 7f.
runners.comment: Ex-French; unraced at 7f and on good or quicker; unexposed; market revealing on yard debut
runners.owner: Mrs R G Hillen And Partner

runners.name: Fouette
runners.spotlight: All three starts at 7f on good to firm and good as 2yo, winning Yarmouth maiden in July; reappeared from 425-day break to land two 7f AW handicaps in October and career-best form when fifth of 16 in Deauville Listed event (7.5f, AW) in December; beat only one home in a valuable handicap at Newcastle on last month's return, however, and has work to do in this company.
runners.comment: Career best when fifth in Deauville Listed (7.5f, AW) in December; still has work to do

runners.name: Havana Pusey
runners.spotlight: Tough mare who won two handicaps around this time last year, scoring at Windsor (6f, good to firm) and following up at Goodwood (7f, good) 18 days later; registered plenty of other good efforts last term and was back on the scoresheet with narrow win at Yarmouth (7f, good) 11 days ago; something to find on the figures but she's the type to make her presence felt.
runners.comment: Fifth win when landing h'cap (7f, good) last time; bit to do but can make presence felt

runners.name: Sunlit Uplands
runners.spotlight: Progressive novice (6f, AW) and dual handicap winner (7f, good and soft) who signed off for Rae Guest with solid fourth of ten in Listed event on AW here (1m) in November; can likely do better again with just eight starts behind her; stable debut.
runners.comment: Progressive; signed off for Rae Guest with fourth in 1m AW Listed here (1m); watch market

runners.name: Dash Of Azure
runners.spotlight: Made a winning handicap debut at Kempton (AW) on her return last May and scored again at Thirsk (good) in September; fine effort despite not getting the breaks when just denied in Ascot Listed event (also 7f, soft) the following month and midfield finish from a poor draw at Longchamp a fortnight later is easily glossed over; commands respect on this return.
runners.comment: Big run when unlucky second in Ascot Listed (7f, soft) in October; respected on return

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Handicap

runners.name: Dragon Leader
runners.spotlight: Made all in very valuable auction race as a juvenile but winless since taking Redcar's Two-Year-Old Trophy six weeks later; looked to be rediscovering his level when third on AW in March and it was a solid fifth of 18 behind Royal Velvet (declared for preceding Group 3) at Newmarket (7f, good) last time; if the stands' rail proves the place to be this could be the day to revert to forcing tactics; interesting.
runners.comment: No win since 2023 but ran well latest and drawn to bag the near rail; interesting

runners.name: Back In Black
runners.spotlight: Scored on his reappearance at Newbury (7f) last April; string of good efforts followed from August onwards and he posted a career best when fourth of 24 in Newbury's Spring Cup (1m, also good) on his return three weeks ago; stall 1 might not be ideal but otherwise holds strong claims.
runners.comment: Career best when fourth on his return three weeks ago; doesn't know how to run a bad race

runners.name: Foreseen
runners.spotlight: The winner of three of his last six starts (1m, heavy and AW) and ran another solid race when close fourth of ten here (AW) in March; also acts on good ground but this is hotter than the races he usually contests.
runners.comment: The winner of three of his last six starts; this is hotter than his normal patch, though

runners.name: Christian David
runners.spotlight: Novice and handicap (7f) winner on AW here, latterly in December when sporting first-time blinkers (retained); respectable ninth of 21 when 66-1 for the Lincoln in March and it was another solid effort when third in Class 2 handicap (1m, good) at Newmarket's Craven meeting; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Respectable ninth in Lincoln and third at Newmarket's Craven meeting; can't be ruled out

runners.name: The Dragon King
runners.spotlight: Spectacular late run to land valuable auction race (6f, good to soft) in October 2024 is starting to fade from the memory but he won his first race since when taking a Wolverhampton handicap in November; narrow defeat at Newcastle (also 7f, AW) in February was up with his best form and respectable efforts on last two starts; not dismissed back on turf.
runners.comment: Narrow defeat (7f, AW) in February was up with his best form; not dismissed back on turf

runners.name: Mr Swivell
runners.spotlight: Good second of 16 (AW) on his stable debut/reappearance last April and went on to land similar events in June and July; turf no issue and was second of 17 at Ascot (also 1m, soft; acts on good to firm) in September; career-high mark but still respected on this return.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner last year; career-high mark but acts on turf and respected on this return

runners.name: Apotheosis
runners.spotlight: Defied penalty in 3yo novice here (good) in 2024; back from a 145-day break and wind surgery when landing Class 2 at Newcastle (also 7f, AW) in December and beaten a neck at Abu Dhabi (1m, good) in March; career-high mark but still considered.
runners.comment: 7f 3yo winner here; beaten neck at Abu Dhabi latest; career-high mark but still considered

runners.name: Sword
runners.spotlight: Patchy form for David O'Meara last term but won in June (good to firm; first-time hood) and ran a corker when third of 21 in valuable Ascot handicap (good) in July; back in form when slightly unlucky second at Newcastle (also 7f, AW) in October and sold for 40,000gns later that month; hood (absent when disappointing on final start) missing again; tongue tied for stable debut and warrants a market check.
runners.comment: Capable in a hood when on a going day last term; makes yard debut in tongue-tie this time

races.race_name: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap

runners.name: Hardy's Hero
runners.spotlight: Maiden and novice winner (6f, good to firm/good) last summer who was a shade disappointing when fifth of ten on handicap debut at Newbury (good to soft) in September; subsequently gelded and his neck second of 13 at Newmarket (also 7f, good) was much more like it on his comeback last month; mixed messages have come out of that race but still commands plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Underwhelming h'cap debut on good to soft but much better when 2nd on return; big player

runners.name: French Affair
runners.spotlight: Scored on handicap debut at Newbury (6f, good) in August and it was a good return when just failing at Kempton in March; took a big step backwards at Southwell (also 7f, AW) next time, although that was a little out of character so probably worth forgiving.
runners.comment: Backward step last time was a little out of character so probably worth forgiving

runners.name: Assaranca
runners.spotlight: Made all at Nottingham in October under Joe Leavy and she stepped up on her seasonal return when third of six at Epsom (also 8.5f, good) 11 days ago; could be dangerous if she grabs the stands' rail.
runners.comment: Made all in October; fair effort latest; could be dangerous if she grabs the stands' rail

runners.name: Dagger Strike
runners.spotlight: Gelded and well backed when making all on handicap debut at Newcastle in December; runner-up on his next three starts but form dipped at Southwell (also 7f, AW) towards the end of March; having just his second go on turf and could be well drawn to attack.
runners.comment: Made all on handicap debut; form dipped latest but drawn to attack; second turf start

runners.name: Mr Writer
runners.spotlight: Promise in his qualifying runs; pulled hard when disappointing on handicap debut at Doncaster (good to soft) in September and raced freely when fourth in Wolverhampton novice (also 7f, AW) on his reappearance three weeks ago; now makes second handicap start and needs to settle if he's going to fulfill his potential.
runners.comment: Has some potential but needs to settle better if he is to fulfill it

runners.name: Mystic Moment
runners.spotlight: Useful filly for Eve Johnson Houghton, readily made all in Epsom maiden (7f, good; Assaranca 4l second) in July; transferred to Neil Mulholland (same owners) but it was a low-key start when last of six in conditions race at Kempton (1m, AW) on her comeback in March; needs to put that behind her on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Useful for previous yard; will need to put stable debut behind her in this first handicap

runners.name: Oracle Mission
runners.spotlight: Good start when third on AW here (7f) in November and didn't need to improve to beat 11 others returned to that C&D the following month; interesting to see blinkers go on for this handicap/turf debut but still of clear interest on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Both starts here (7f, AW); good start and won next time; blinkers on for turf/h'cap debut

runners.name: Fresh Fade
runners.spotlight: Can lead; Naas maiden (6f, good) winner in August for Michael O'Callaghan who wasn't disgraced in a brief two-race spell on AW for Ken Condon; bought for 18,000gns in January; potentially in the best stall and sole start at 7f was inconclusive so heed the market signals on this stable debut/reappearance.
runners.comment: Can lead; Naas maiden (6f, good) winner in August; could be well drawn on stable debut

races.race_name: William Hill Price Boosts Every Day Handicap

runners.name: Shalaa Asker
runners.spotlight: Nine wins on the AW but just 1-28 on turf; fit from a productive winter campaign but he's drawn on the outside and others are more appealing.
runners.comment: Had a good winter on AW but he is just 1-28 on turf and stall 1 rarely a positive here

runners.name: So Smart
runners.spotlight: Turned in an excellent effort when winning the corresponding race from stall 1 (14 ran) last May, flying out of the stalls and able to get to the stands' rail after one furlong; also won at Ffos Las in July and both victories were achieved off a higher mark than today's; not at best last time but his reappearance had been encouraging; should make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Overcame a poor draw to win this race last year; 1lb lower today; one to take seriously

runners.name: Tuscan Point
runners.spotlight: Bolted up on his stable debut at Southwell in March (6f); dropped to 5f for his last two starts, picked off late at Catterick (good ground) before a ready win on the AW track here nine days ago; another 7lb higher mark to contend with but unlikely he has reached his ceiling just yet.
runners.comment: Two wins on AW for new yard but ran well on turf at Catterick; up in weights but improving

runners.name: My Boy Harry
runners.spotlight: In good form in the depths of winter, winning two 7f handicaps at Chelmsford; edging back down the weights after some below-par runs but returning to 5f, even in a race that is highly likely to be run at a fast pace, is unlikely to revive his fortutnes.
runners.comment: Two 7f AW wins in January; quiet more recently and the return to 5f is an obvious concern

runners.name: Maharajas Express
runners.spotlight: The winner of three 5f handicaps for Ian Williams last season, the latest off just 1lb lower; never a threat on his seasonal return 12 days ago (6f, AW) but he kept on well enough given he took a fierce hold through the early part of the race; better expected back at 5f and with the reappearance run behind him.
runners.comment: Pulled hard and kept on well enough on return (6f, AW); much better expected back at 5f

runners.name: Harry Brown
runners.spotlight: Has form on good to firm but 0-15 on grass as opposed to 5-29 on AW; short of his best on AW of late and others bring more convincing claims.
runners.comment: 0-15 on turf; not at best on AW this spring and others bring more convincing claims
runners.owner: Peter Crate And Gallagher Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Birkenhead
runners.spotlight: Into the veteran stage but he retains plenty of dash, setting a good pace at Catterick 17 days ago before weakening into third (Tuscan Point second but 8lb worse off today); likely to make a beeline for the stands' rail and more minor money could be on the cards.
runners.comment: Retains plenty of dash and should make a bold bid up the stands' rail; place contender

runners.name: Desert Champion
runners.spotlight: Had a BHA mark 26lb higher than today when starting out for this yard last summer but he's struggled to make much of an impact; did finish second at Newcastle in November (well backed) but that form hasn't worked out at all; recent Redcar reappearance is easy to excuse (went off hard and raced in a group of two, finishing sixth and seventh, on the far side); stable looks to hold stronger claims with Birkenhead.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark but it is easy enough to have reservations for now

runners.name: Faustus
runners.spotlight: Front-runner with a C&D win on his record; 6lb lower than when seeing off seven rivals at Newbury last summer (5f, good to firm) but not easily recommended on 2026 evidence and he will find easier races to dominate than this.
runners.comment: Front-runner; 6lb lower than for last summer's Newbury win; recent efforts not his best

races.race_name: Pertemps Network Long Distance Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Al Sayah
runners.spotlight: First four hurdle wins came in the month of May, before he took a four-runner race at Taunton (3m, good) last month; 5lb higher in today's much more competitive affair but that was his first attempt over this far and he might have more to offer as a stayer; first visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won at Taunton (3m; 4 ran) latest; might have more to offer as a stayer with new headgear

runners.name: La Pinsonniere
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort but she's won just once (a maiden last January) from 13 starts; rallied into a remote second here (good) last time, so perhaps a first attempt beyond that 2m5f will help the cause.
runners.comment: Rallied into remote second here last time; perhaps first attempt beyond 2m5f will help

runners.name: Flying Fortune
runners.spotlight: Won four on the trot in July-October 2024 at about 2m4f, culminating in a Grade 2 novice event; long absence before three runs this year, easily best of those last time and that display gave some encouragement for this longer trip, but jumping was sketchy and cheekpieces are now applied.
runners.comment: Bit of a revival last time gave encouragement for longer trip; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Bold Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Back with Nicky Henderson last season and showed he retained a chunk of ability on first two starts, latterly when seventh of 24 in the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham (3m, good); made the running at Aintree (22-1) four weeks ago but ended pulled up; remains well handicapped on old form but there's been no win since January 2023.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Aintree last time; well handicapped on old form but no win since Jan 2023

runners.name: Matty's Getaway
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who comfortably accounted for a subsequent Grade 2 novice winner in maiden at Doncaster (2m5f, good to soft) in November; had wind surgery soon afterwards and came a satisfactory second of four in novice here (2m5f, good) on last month's return; the longer trip is an appealing option for this unexposed handicap debutant.
runners.comment: Second run after wind op; new trip makes appeal for this unexposed handicap debutant

runners.name: Tranquil Sea
runners.spotlight: Had one wind operation last November and another this March; surged back from a lean spell with 5l win at Plumpton (3m1f, good) three weeks ago on a rare run in cheekpieces, which are retained, but today's 7lb higher mark in a warm race demands a career best.
runners.comment: Surged back from lean spell with 5l win at Plumpton three weeks ago; career best needed

runners.name: Harbour Lake
runners.spotlight: Won by a neck in this race (good) last year off 1lb lower, having been third in it the year before; 33-1 sixth at Aintree last month suggested he's working his way back to that level, so has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Won by a neck in this race last year off 1lb lower; has to be considered seriously

runners.name: Moon d'Orange
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent chaser who's saved his best for Cheltenham in 2025 and 2026, including a win (his only handicap success) in January 2025 and third at the Festival this March; both were over an extended 2m4f and he's shown his form only once from seven attempts under rules over 3m+; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Inconsistent chaser; has shown form only once from seven attempts under rules over 3m+

runners.name: Deep Cave
runners.spotlight: 3m hat-trick in 2025 started with a success over hurdles at Aintree (good) in April before two chase wins late in the year; three marked backward steps over fences since but he's well handicapped on best chase efforts.
runners.comment: Backward steps on last three starts over fences; well handicapped on best chase efforts

runners.name: Eagle Fang
runners.spotlight: Back hurdling (from chasing) in a first-time visor when an excellent second of 22 at Aintree (3m, good to soft) four weeks ago, always prominent; back up 2lb but a serious each-way player.
runners.comment: Excellent second at Aintree four weeks ago; back up 2lb but a serious each-way player

runners.name: Serious Chat
runners.spotlight: Regular winner, including of ten-runner C&D race (good) last month when he travelled up smoothly to lead two out before answering some serious questions; raised another 5lb but highly respected after that career best.
runners.comment: Regular winner and raised 5lb after C&D win last month; highly respected

runners.name: Lord Snootie
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent and only 1-18 under rules; placed twice from four starts over C&D, including when third to Serious Chat on latest start, but there appear to be more potent options among his rivals.
runners.comment: Placed twice from four starts over C&D but inconsistent and rules strike-rate is only 1-18

runners.name: Sea The Clouds
runners.spotlight: Hurdles form (handicap winner over 2m4f-3m1f) does not look quite good enough in this higher grade and the form of his winning chase debut at Cartmel last July has been followed by heavy defeats in both starts since (ridden by this 10lb claimer on latest); others preferred.
runners.comment: Hurdles form does not look quite good enough in this higher grade; others preferred

runners.name: Walking On Air
runners.spotlight: Sparked just once in each of the last two seasons, at Doncaster; visored on latest start, after three runs in cheekpieces; often tongue tied as well but goes without any equipment today; his last hurdle race was in February 2024 and other options look a lot more solid.
runners.comment: Last hurdle race was February 2024; sparked rarely of late; others look a lot more solid

runners.name: Gold For Alec
runners.spotlight: A marked step up in trip saw him emerge from the wilderness to win five-runner handicap at Hereford (extended 3m1f, good) last month and he did it comfortably, but he's 10lb higher today (4lb out of the handicap) against far more daunting opposition.
runners.comment: Comfortable Hereford win last month but 10lb higher against tougher opposition today

races.race_name: Pertemps Network Swinton Handicap Hurdle (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tellherthename
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced for his age; caught the eye in no uncertain terms when twelfth of 23 in the County at Cheltenham on first start for current yard, travelling smoothly but short of room in straight and finishing full of running; very best efforts for previous stables came on good ground and he missed the Scottish Champion when the rain came; still unexposed and a fascinating contender in a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Caught eye in County at Cheltenham on first start for current yard; unexposed; big chance

runners.name: Magna Victor
runners.spotlight: Trained in Shropshire but has raced mainly in Scotland; followed two wins at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) over the winter with creditable third of 15 at Ayr (2m, soft) three weeks ago; more needed off unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Creditable third at Ayr three weeks ago but needs a personal best off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Made U Blush
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly who gets weight all round; made it 2-3 over hurdles with a Listed juvenile win at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in January and she improved further when a close second at Sandown (2m, good) last month; open to further progress and she's a second plausible candidate for Dan Skelton.
runners.comment: 4yo filly who gets weight all round; close second at Sandown last time; respected

runners.name: Indemnity
runners.spotlight: 4-6 on good ground over hurdles, notching another personal best when returning from a break to win readily at Plumpton (2m; 15-2) last month; a 10lb rise demands further improvement but he's thriving and should encounter suitable conditions again.
runners.comment: 4-6 on good ground over hurdles; ready winner at Plumpton; up 10lb but he's thriving

runners.name: Star Of Guiting
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in the winter and has had excuses since, making late headway after a slow start when eighth of 21 at Aintree four weeks ago; probably in much better heart than recent form-figures suggest, and he's an interesting each-way proposition.
runners.comment: Won twice at Musselburgh in the winter and has had excuses since; interesting each-way

runners.name: Hamlet's Night
runners.spotlight: 0-14 on Flat turf and a beaten favourite at Musselburgh two weeks ago; his hurdles record (2-7) is better but he had no obvious excuse when fifth to Indemnity at Plumpton (33-1) last month; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Had no obvious excuse when fifth to Indemnity at Plumpton last month; others appeal more

runners.name: Mr McLoughlan
runners.spotlight: Free-goer who was on a roll last autumn, winning three times; well held behind Star Of Guiting at Musselburgh on New Year's Day and couldn't land a blow behind Dance And Glance over C&D last month, but he should get plenty of cover in this big field and could outrun his likely odds.
runners.comment: Again too free here last time; should get cover in a big field and could outrun his odds

runners.name: Helnwein
runners.spotlight: Often gives his running in strong handicaps (second in this event 12 months ago) but losing sequence is up to 11 and he looked held when falling two out in the Plumpton race won by Indemnity last time; cheekpieces left off today; opposable off unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Held when falling in the Plumpton race won by Indemnity; opposable off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Bubble Dubi
runners.spotlight: Has run just four times since 2023; returned from long absence to win at Aintree (2m1f, good to soft) last November but failed to repeat the form in prestigious handicaps at Newbury and Sandown; something to prove.
runners.comment: Returned from long break to win at Aintree but has failed to repeat the form; bit to prove

runners.name: Dance And Glance
runners.spotlight: 5-14 strike-rate and steadily progressive form, gaining his latest success over this C&D (good ground; three of today's rivals behind) in convincing fashion; a 6lb rise does not look harsh, and with further improvement on the cards he has solid claims.
runners.comment: Progressive; had two of these behind when winning over C&D last month; solid claims

runners.name: Frontier Prince
runners.spotlight: Has proved a model of consistency in the last 12 months and he rallied for fourth behind Dance And Glance over C&D last time; however, that run underlined that the handicapper appears to have his measure for now; needs to be boosted by the fitting of cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Another creditable run when fourth over C&D last time but the handicapper seems in charge

runners.name: Bucephalus
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who regularly gives his all and was beaten off only on the run-in when third to Dance And Glance over C&D (good) five weeks ago; should make another bold bid but several of these look less exposed.
runners.comment: Front-runner; third over C&D last time; in the mix but several of these look less exposed

runners.name: No Ordinary Joe
runners.spotlight: Finally took advantage of some leniency from the handicapper when winning at Sandown (2m4f, good) two weeks ago, always in the firing line; still well treated on old form but he's not sure to be so effective back over this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Ended long losing run over 2m4f at Sandown; not sure to be so effective back in trip

runners.name: Laafi
runners.spotlight: Useful on Flat and has quickly reached a similar level over hurdles, posting a career best when first past the post in a big field at Aintree's Grand National meeting (2m, good to soft) only to be disqualified for a whip offence; seeks compensation but a 6lb higher mark will make things tougher.
runners.comment: First past post at Aintree last month only to be disqualified for whip offence; 6lb higher

runners.name: Gibbs Island
runners.spotlight: Easy winner at Chepstow (2m, good) last October and he took a big step back in right direction when seventh of 23 in the County at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft); missed the Scottish Champion Hurdle last month due to heavy ground; still only five and he's 1lb lower than in the County; major player.
runners.comment: Shaped well when seventh of 23 in the County at Cheltenham; still only five; major player

runners.name: Moon Chime
runners.spotlight: In good form over hurdles last spring, winning at Stratford and Hexham (both 2m, good to soft); he's only 2lb higher than for the second of those wins, and a recent run on the Flat after six months off should have blown away the cobwebs; impossible to discount in first-time tongue-strap.
runners.comment: Recent Flat run should have blown away the cobwebs; possibilities on last spring's form
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

races.race_name: Pertemps Network 'Junior' National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)

runners.name: Plan De Stan
runners.spotlight: Built on debut form (had wind surgery afterwards) when 1l second of 11 at Hereford (good ground; 3-1 joint-favourite) five weeks ago, leading from over 2f out until near the finish; that's the best form on offer and he should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Hereford second is the best form on offer here; should be in the thick of things again

runners.name: St Gabriel
runners.spotlight: 27,500euros 3yo; half-brother to French 2m2f hurdle winner Atacama Du Mazet; dam maiden half-sister to winners Askar Tau (2m/2m2f Group 2) and Arango (Group placed 1m2f/1m3f), out of German 1m-1m2f Listed winner; yard does well (19%) in bumpers; one of the more likely newcomers.
runners.comment: 27,500euros 3yo; yard does well (19%) in bumpers; one of the more likely newcomers

runners.name: Aribidia
runners.spotlight: Some promise in a big field on debut and he shaped well when third of 11 at Warwick (good; 3-1) next time, rallying; type to keep progressing and he's high on the list.
runners.comment: Improved when third at Warwick, rallying; type to keep progressing and high on the list

runners.name: Magic Feld
runners.spotlight: French-bred half-brother to 1m4f AQPS Flat and useful 2m1f-3m hurdle winner Hititi; dam 1m4f/1m5f AQPS Flat and 2m1f hurdle winner; a second runner for Tom Weston and he makes some appeal on pedigree; the booking of Harry Skelton catches the eye.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a French bumper winner; booking of Harry Skelton catches the eye

runners.name: Sodeto
runners.spotlight: Cost 1,000gns as a 3yo; Ribchester half-brother to 1m4f winner Kate The Cook, out of a 1m2f/1m6f winner; rare runner in this sphere for mainly Flat yard; fitted with hood for debut.
