runners.comment: Cost 1,000gns as a 3yo; rare runner in this sphere for mainly Flat yard

runners.name: Mister Dam
runners.spotlight: Out of a French 2m4f/2m7f cross-country chase winner; looks likely to be of more interest over obstacles and longer distances; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Out of a cross-country chase winner; looks likely to be of more interest over obstacles

runners.name: You Be Lucky Jean
runners.spotlight: By Maxios; dam dual bumper and 2m4f hurdle winner, out of French 1m2f winner; the only filly in the line-up; makes some appeal on pedigree and worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: The only filly in the line-up; appealing on pedigree and worth monitoring in the market

runners.name: Bold Acclaim
runners.spotlight: 9,000euros foal; by Kew Gardens; dam unraced half-sister to winners Heron Heights (smart 2m3f-3m chase), Witness In Court (useful 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase) and Stellar Magic (useful 2m3f-3m1f hurdle/chase); stable has the odd bumper winner and runs two.
runners.comment: Related to several useful types; stable has the odd bumper winner and runs two

runners.name: Running Happy
runners.spotlight: Clearcut winner on debut at Huntingdon (good ground; 11-2) five weeks ago; will need some improvement to defy a penalty but it's certainly possible and his stable remains in good form.
runners.comment: Clearcut winner on debut at Huntingdon; more required to defy penalty but it's possible

runners.name: Sevenkingsmustdie
runners.spotlight: Originally cost 425,000gns as a yearling; finished well behind stablemate Aribidia when sixth of 11 at Warwick (9-1) six weeks ago, racing freely despite the hood (now left off); needs a big leap forward.
runners.comment: Well behind stablemate Aribidia when sixth at Warwick and needs a big leap forward

races.race_name: Pertemps Newton Handicap

runners.name: Barbury Boy
runners.spotlight: Gelded and upped in distance, showed much improved form to win 12-runner race at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) on reappearance, pulling well clear with a rival who has since gone one better; should be at least as effective over this longer distance; still unexposed and has solid credentials.
runners.comment: Much improved to win at Beverley; that form has been boosted; solid credentials

runners.name: Myrrh
runners.spotlight: Ran close to her 2yo best when third of eight on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) two weeks ago, and could come on for the outing; may stay today's even longer trip (mixed messages from pedigree).
runners.comment: Respectable third at Doncaster on handicap/seasonal debut; up even further in trip

runners.name: Crown Inn To Win
runners.spotlight: Campaigned solely on AW so far; has thrived since stepped up to 1m4f, comfortably winning at Wolverhampton 12 days ago; a 5lb rise does not look harsh and he'll be thereabouts again if proving as effective on turf.
runners.comment: Campaigned solely on AW; comfortable winner at Wolverhampton; in the mix if handling turf

runners.name: Salkadan
runners.spotlight: Unplaced over 6f/7f first three starts but he fared much better upped to 1m4f when short-headed at Kempton six weeks ago; should be just as effective on turf and may still have untapped potential.
runners.comment: Much improved upped to 1m4f when second at Kempton; may still have untapped potential

runners.name: Double Combo
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but has made the frame on his last three starts, leading for most of the way when fourth on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in January; doesn't look thrown in but cheekpieces could eke out some improvement.
runners.comment: In frame last three starts; cheekpieces could eke out some improvement

runners.name: Due To Shine
runners.spotlight: Low-level maiden; looks up against it from 4lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Low-level maiden; looks up against it from 4lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Keep It Classic
runners.spotlight: Bred to be useful but has shown just modest form, fading after leading when fifth of ten on handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m2f, good; 5-2) last time; may yet improve but he's hard to fancy on the figures.
runners.comment: Bred to be useful but has shown just modest form and hard to fancy on the figures

runners.name: Corduroy
runners.spotlight: Up and down in his short career to date, running badly on stable debut at Wolverhampton last month; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran badly on stable debut at Wolverhampton last month and others appeal more

races.race_name: Pertemps Network Handicap

runners.name: Silver State
runners.spotlight: The winner of four of his six handicap runs since fitted with a visor, including two impressive front-running displays since returned to turf (1m; good to soft and good to firm); 5lb higher in a stronger race but he cannot be passed over at all lightly, such has been his progress.
runners.comment: 4-6 since handicapping; turf wins on last two starts; up in class but still a key player

runners.name: Mohaaraj
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on his 2yo debut and justified strong market support to win a Kempton maiden in February, in workmanlike fashion but he was well on top at the finish; moves into handicaps with considerable potential.
runners.comment: Strong finish to win a 1m Kempton maiden in February; brings potential to handicaps
runners.owner: Jastar Capital, K Lawrence & P Merritt

runners.name: Jeddaal
runners.spotlight: Promise as a 2yo; gelded prior to his reappearance in which he won a 7f Catterick maiden in good style; hit with a 6lb rise for that but 1m should suit and further progress seems likely; trainer in good form.
runners.comment: Improved on 2yo efforts when winning 7f Catterick maiden last month; more to come over 1m

runners.name: Delinquent
runners.spotlight: Steady progress in AW handicaps this year, getting off the mark at Southwell (1m) two weeks ago; that effort was backed up by the Topspeed figure but he is in a deeper race today.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Southwell two weeks ago (good Topspeed figure); in a deeper race today

runners.name: Dodge City
runners.spotlight: Promise in Britain as a 2yo before winning a 1m1f conditions event in France last August; well held in a 1m2f conditions event at Chantilly last month (soft ground); not obviously well treated back in a handicap but new cheekpieces could give him a lift.
runners.comment: 1m1f win in France last year; last of 7 (1m2f, soft) on return; tough mark; headgear now

runners.name: Blue To Blue
runners.spotlight: Three wins (one 5f and two 7f, including here) for Karl Burke as a 2yo; 50-1 and never threatened in a Newmarket handicap on his seasonal/stable debut last month (1m, good); gelded since; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Three wins for Karl Burke as 2yo; down the field on stable debut last month; gelded since

runners.name: Applesandpears
runners.spotlight: Progressive in three starts last autumn, finishing runner-up on her Leicester debut (6f, good) before winning AW novices at Chelmsford (7f) and Newcastle (6f); should stay 1m and she brings potential to her handicap debut; market instructive.
runners.comment: Improved with each start as a 2yo, winning two AW novices; market useful now handicapping

runners.name: Aerial Silk
runners.spotlight: Promise in three 7f runs as a 2yo; gelded since last seen; plenty of stamina on the bottom line of his pedigree and he could step forward now upped to 1m for his handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Promise in three 7f runs as a 2yo; more to come upped to 1m for his handicap debut

runners.name: Harlequin Breeze
runners.spotlight: Took a good step forward when second of 11 at Newmarket last November (7f, good to soft), her third start; in at the deep end in the Group 3 Nell Gwyn on her reappearance and came up well short; not obviously well treated and the step up to 1m needs to spark extra.
runners.comment: Stiff task in the Nell Gwyn on return and came up well short; improvement needed for 1m

runners.name: Sea The Power
runners.spotlight: Made all in an Ayr novice (1m, good to soft) in September, his third start, and that form has worked out; fluffed his lines when favourite for a 1m2f nursery at Nottingham one month later; has left Adrian Keatley and makes his stable/seasonal debut in a competitive race; not fully exposed and it will be interesting to see if his backers return.
runners.comment: 1m novice win for Adrian Keatley last September; makes stable debut in a competitive race

races.race_name: Pertemps Network Spring Trophy Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Lake Forest
runners.spotlight: Won the Group 2 Gimcrack as a 2yo and finished second in the Group 1 City Of York (7f, good to firm) last August; made no show in two Group 1s in Australia in the autumn and he's since been gelded; returns in a much lower grade than usual and very dangerous if ready to roll after six months off.
runners.comment: Second in Group 1 last summer; very dangerous if ready to roll after six months off

runners.name: Ten Bob Tony
runners.spotlight: Reappeared last season with victory in the Group 3 John Of Gaunt over C&D and he signed off the campaign with a fine third in the Group 1 Foret at Longchamp; might have needed the run when fourth in Listed event at the Curragh six weeks ago; versatile in terms of the ground; solid chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fine third in French Group 1 last autumn; probably needed reappearance; solid

runners.name: Poet Master
runners.spotlight: 7f specialist, a Group 2 winner in July 2024; below best when finding Myal just too strong in conditions event at Thirsk (7f, good; 15-8 favourite) on return; may build on that but he's 4lb worse off with that rival here.
runners.comment: Found Myal just too strong in conditions event at Thirsk on return; may build on that
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Beauty Destiny
runners.spotlight: Won five times over 6f in Hong Kong between November 2024 and March 2025, but he was only sixth of seven on British debut at Newcastle (AW; 7-2; flashed tail) seven weeks ago; headgear returns today but he still looks up against it.
runners.comment: Five Hong Kong wins; well held on British debut; headgear back but he looks up against it

runners.name: Heathcliff
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW this year, winning a handicap at Lingfield (7f) in February and running creditably when fourth in similar event at Newcastle (6f) on Good Friday; has shown he's up to Listed level but this is still a warm race in which to be making his debut on grass.
runners.comment: In good form on the AW this year but this is a warm race for his turf debut

runners.name: Holguin
runners.spotlight: Won the same Listed event at Chester in 2023 (soft) and 2025 (good), posting a personal best in latter; not seen since but he has plenty of form when fresh and cannot be ruled out.
runners.comment: Last seen posting career best when winning at Chester; has gone well fresh; not ruled out

runners.name: Myal
runners.spotlight: Likeable 5yo with a good (7-18) strike-rate; shaped well in Listed event at Doncaster on reappearance and dug deep to win conditions race at Thirsk (7f, good) next time; may not have reached his ceiling and is high on the list.
runners.comment: Likeable type; dug deep to win at Thirsk; may not have reached his ceiling just yet

runners.name: Palmar Bay
runners.spotlight: 5-19; creditable fourth in Listed races last two starts, not beaten far at Wolverhampton (7f) on latest; should give his running again but quite a few of these have more obvious claims on the figures.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth last two starts but does not have an obvious chance on the figures

runners.name: Release The Storm
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten 4yo; followed debut win at Leicester (7f, good) last summer with ready all-the-way success at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) two weeks later; open to plenty more improvement but this is a far sterner test of his credentials.
runners.comment: 2-2 last summer; unexposed but this is a far sterner test of his credentials

runners.name: Audience
runners.spotlight: Group 1 Lockinge winner in 2024 but on a 12-race losing sequence and he ran badly on his last three starts in Dubai early this year; this is a drop in grade but he's proving increasingly hard to predict.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner in his prime but increasingly hard to predict

runners.name: Balmacara
runners.spotlight: None too consistent in 2025, running well when second in 1m Listed event at Sandown (soft) in September but poorly twice afterwards; returns from a layoff and may find that conditions place the emphasis too much on speed.
runners.comment: Returns from a layoff and may find that conditions place  the emphasis too much on speed

races.race_name: Pertemps Handicap

runners.name: One Night Thunder
runners.spotlight: Down the field on his reappearance at Thirsk three weeks ago but last summer's form brings him right into the reckoning and he could take a step forward today.
runners.comment: Down the field on his Thirsk return but last summer's 7f form brings him right into it

runners.name: Ata Rangi
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Ed Walker last season and positives can be drawn from the fact that one was on his seasonal return and the other was over C&D (good ground); returns from a six-month break on a manageable mark and a promising claimer is booked; contender.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 2lb lower last September; can go well fresh; needs serious consideration

runners.name: City Captain
runners.spotlight: Did well for Harriet Bethell last season and his two runs this year have been encouraging, second of 13 to an unexposed one at Thirsk three weeks ago; mark left unchanged; big player.
runners.comment: Progressive for yard; met unexposed winner at Thirsk latest; mark unchanged; key player

runners.name: Diligent Resdev
runners.spotlight: Won the 2024 Ayr Bronze Cup in fine style (6f, good); 0-5 in 2025 but looked to retain all his ability; conditions won't be an issue and he has run well first time out in each of his seasons of racing; chance.
runners.comment: Failed to win last year but ran some good races, including on his reappearance; chance

runners.name: Glenfinnan
runners.spotlight: He should be winning races off this mark and his down-the-field effort at Doncaster six weeks ago isn't the race to judge him on; better can be expected back up at 7f.
runners.comment: On a good mark and his reappearance isn't the run to judge him on; return to 7f is a plus

runners.name: Bravo Zulu
runners.spotlight: Did well last year but this winter's AW campaign was up and down; returns to turf in a competitive race and may come up short again.
runners.comment: Mixed bag on AW this winter; returns to turf in a competitive event

runners.name: Diamondonthehill
runners.spotlight: He has turned in a string of good runs on the AW since November but such consistency ensures he gets little respite from the handicapper; others may prove better treated today.
runners.comment: String of good AW runs since November; not much wiggle room off current mark though

runners.name: Warm Spell
runners.spotlight: Well treated on last season's best efforts and a recent run at Thirsk should have sharpened him up; trip and ground suit and he is another to consider in an open race.
runners.comment: Good chance on last year's best efforts; sharper for last month's return; each-way shout

runners.name: Son
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress for Richard Hannon but, having dropped in the weights, he went desperately close to making a winning debut for Tim Easterby three weeks ago, staying on strongly at Thirsk (7f, good); 1lb higher today but he attained a BHA rating of 106 in his pomp; contender.
runners.comment: Went desperately close on last month's stable debut at Thirsk (7f, good); solid contender

runners.name: Havana Blue
runners.spotlight: Struggled last summer but underwent wind surgery in September and he ended 2025 with two solid AW runs; returns from a five-month break in a competitive race.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two solid AW runs (after a wind op); returns to turf in a competitive race

races.race_name: #Matchbooklovesracing Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Carry The Flag
runners.spotlight: Pleasing effort on debut at the Curragh (5f, soft), beaten 1.25l into second and the third has since run well in Listed company; pedigree suggests improvement will be forthcoming on quicker ground; leading chance for top yard.
runners.comment: Pleasing debut effort on soft, quicker ground a likely plus, leading chance for top yard

runners.name: Woodlawn Lily
runners.spotlight: Space Blues filly produced something to build on with 2l fourth behind subsequent easy Listed winner Velozee on debut at Cork (5f, soft) when an unconsidered 25-1 shot; big chance if she acts on quicker ground (not certain on breeding).
runners.comment: Form of Cork fourth on debut since boosted by winner, chance if acts on quicker ground

runners.name: Sun Goddess
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly; 120,000GBP yearling; dam unraced half-sister to winners Scream Blue Murder (6f Group 3), Pilgrims Rest (1m2f/1m3f) and Gibraltar Blue (South African 7f/1m Group 2), out of 1m Listed winner; second runner for top yard and looks second-string on rider arrangements.
runners.comment: Sioux Nation filly, dam unraced, top yard but looks second string on rider arrangements

runners.name: Mr Montauk
runners.spotlight: Sent off at just 4-1 over C&D (good) on debut but could only manage an 8.5l fifth of 7; bred to be better than that but will need to improve plenty on that run to make an impact here.
runners.comment: Modest effort on debut over C&D, needs to improve plenty on that to make an impact here

runners.name: Abletai
runners.spotlight: Betrayed inexperience when too keen and fading into last of 8 over 6f here on debut; drop in trip a plus but may need a bit more time.
runners.comment: Too keen when fading over 6f here on debut, drop in trip a plus but may need more time

runners.name: Ocean Club
runners.spotlight: Failed to fire on debut in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, gd-sft) when a 14-1 shot; given a 42-day break since and returns with yard in flying form; by Persian Force so could improve plenty for this faster ground; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Failed to fire on debut, faster ground could spark improvement, yard flying

runners.name: Santorini Storm
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in 5f maidens at the Curragh (sft-hvy) and at Cork (soft); not easy to make a case for on the back of those tame efforts.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in maidens at the Curragh and Cork, hard to make a case for

races.race_name: Danny Trundle Heating & Plumbing Handicap

runners.name: She's A Gift
runners.spotlight: 0-14 and yet to finish in the first three; best effort a 2l fourth of 14 at Dundalk (5f) in January off 40; hiked up 10lb for 6l seventh in Dundalk maiden latest; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-14, yet to finish in first three, hiked up 10lb for maiden seventh latest, hard to fancy

runners.name: Plushy
runners.spotlight: 3yo is 0-9 overall (0-3 on turf) but has run well in defeat; placed on first two starts in 5f maidens last year at Bellewstown (good) and Cork (gd-fm); 1.5l behind Stone Bear at Dundalk in January and weighted to reverse that; sharper for latest run; interesting.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat on turf and AW, weighted to reverse Jan' form with Stone Bear
runners.owner: Derek Murray/Poetry In Motion Syndicate/Patrick McGuinness

runners.name: Arctic Steps
runners.spotlight: Two-time 5f winner last year on good ground at Down Royal and Dundalk; runner up twice at Dundalk since joining this stable; modest fifth at Navan on latest (5f, gd-yld); remains 7lb above last turf win.
runners.comment: Two-time 5f winner last year, modest fifth at Navan latest, remains 7lb above turf win

runners.name: Stone Bear
runners.spotlight: 3yo; minor promise in turf maidens; progressive on AW, winning two of last three, both at 6f; has gone up a total of 15lb for those two wins; 5f should be fine, as should good ground; go well.
runners.comment: Won two of last three over 6f at Dundalk, 5f/good ground should be fine, go well

runners.name: Eichan San
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but has ability and didn't run badly last time at the Curragh when a keeping on 4.5l fourth of 21 (6f, soft); not sure the drop to 5f is ideal, however, but good ground should be fine.
runners.comment: Kept on for fourth at the Curragh last time, ground fine, drop to 5f a query

runners.name: Snowballs
runners.spotlight: Glimmers of ability in turf maidens last year; no impact on handicap debut at Navan or three subsequent runs at Dundalk; mark tumbling but new blinkers need to spark something.
runners.comment: Form has regressed since switched to handicaps, new blinkers need to spark

runners.name: Poweracclaim
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but capable of winning a race and made a pleasing return to action at Navan last month, running on for fourth after losing ground at the start (upset in stalls); thereabouts if gets away better here.
runners.comment: Pleasing return at Navan after missing the break, thereabouts if gets away better here

runners.name: Mint Man
runners.spotlight: Course form figures of 1-2 over 6f, the win coming off 1lb higher last August; has won over 5f on AW; below best in two outings this season but could revive back at this venue.
runners.comment: Course form figures of 1-2 at 6f, below best in two runs this term, could revive back here

runners.name: Verified
runners.spotlight: Maiden is 0-18 on turf; most of best efforts have come at Cork, including a 0.5l second behind Nouvel Espoir last June off 2lb higher; sent off at 13-2 on stable debut latest but never got involved under inexperienced claimer; Keane takes over now.
runners.comment: 0-18 on turf, best runs at Cork, no impact on stable bow latest, Keane takes over now

runners.name: Phil's Dream
runners.spotlight: Back to form the last twice at Wolverhampton over 5f, beaten a neck in a handicap off 46 then winning a class 6 classified stakes; 0-5 on turf though and yet to trouble the judge.
runners.comment: Back to form at Wolves the last twice, 0-5 on turf though and yet to trouble the judge

runners.name: Art Of Unity
runners.spotlight: Veteran is 5-62 on turf; fine run on seasonal return at Bellewstown (5f,soft) but well beaten over 6f at the Curragh latest; 1lb wrong and would probably prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Veteran, 5-62 on turf, good run on penultimate, 1lb wrong here, best recent form on soft

runners.name: Whatswrongnow
runners.spotlight: Maiden is 0-14 on turf but has run well in defeat at times; off since below par run in a 5f maiden at Dundalk in November; 7lb wrong and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Maiden, 0-14 on turf, lacks a recent run and 7lb wrong here, others preferred

runners.name: Nouvel Espoir
runners.spotlight: 3-23, the last win coming off this mark at Cork last June (5f, gd-yld); low key start to this campaign and three previous runs here have not been his best; rider booking a positive.
runners.comment: Last won off this mark at Cork last June, below best this term, previous runs here poor
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Hastily
runners.spotlight: Consistency not a strong suit but capable on her day and has won four times; however, she's 9lb above her last win, she lacks a recent run and might just need this.
runners.comment: Four-time winner, lacks a recent run, 9lb above last success, might just need this

runners.name: Apache Star
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner in Britain at 5.5f-6f but hasn't scored since July 2023; hasn't looked like ending the drought in three runs for this stable; hood worn last time discarded; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: No win since July 2023, modest efforts in three runs for this stable, 2lb wrong

runners.name: Harry Longabaugh
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant hasn't shown a whole lot in three maidens at 6f-7.5f on yielding and soft ground; breeding suggests he could improve for this quicker ground; check the market.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant, modest form in maidens, could improve for quicker ground

runners.name: Polanco
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in 7f-7.5f maidens last year; gelded in the off season; best watched on handicap debut and seasonal return.
runners.comment: No show in maidens at 7f-7.5f, gelded, watch on handicap debut and seasonal return

races.race_name: Matchbook El Clasico £100 Moneyback Special Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Confucius
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt denied by a nose on debut at the Curragh (5f, sft-hvy) when showing signs of greenness; the 3.5l third Edward Thatch has since franked that form and top yard won this with the smart Albert Einstein last season; 6f should be fine and obvious chance.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied when green on debut, form since franked, top yard won this last year

runners.name: Bull Shark
runners.spotlight: Third in both starts at the Curragh, over 5f on debut (heavy) and over 6f on soft; more needed to take this but his full brother won on good ground so he should improve for these quicker conditions; can go well.
runners.comment: Third on soft over 5f and 6f in first two Curragh runs, should improve for quicker ground

runners.name: Luca Brillante
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never filly; half-sister to winners Nation's Call (7.5f/1m inc Australia; RPR 96) and Man Of Worth (Australian 6f/1m); dam 7f/1m winner (inc 2yo/Group 3; 110); appealing pedigree; market can reveal expectations on debut.
runners.comment: No Nay Never filly, dam 7f/1m 2yo winner inc' Group 3, market can reveal expectations

runners.name: Alaskan Bear
runners.spotlight: Kodiac colt; 95,000gns yearling; half-brother to 7f AW winner Scarfo (RPR 57); dam unraced half-sister to 6f winner Miliika out of 5f Group 3 winner from family of champion sprinter Owington;  yard can ready one so check the betting.
runners.comment: Kodiac colt, half brother to 7f AW winner, yard can ready one, check the market

runners.name: Give Me Liberty
runners.spotlight: Home-bred The Irish Rover gelding; dam five-time 5f winner (RPR 76), closely related to 6f 2yo/1m winner Point Made, out of unraced half-sister to 1m2f-2m Flat and 2m1f-3m hurdle winner Mith Hill; likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: The Irish Rover gelding, dam five-time 5f winner (RPR 76), likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Selwyn
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation colt; 87,000gns foal, 150,000gns yearling; brother to 1m/1m1f winner Lakota Seven (inc 2yo/Singapore; RPR 96), closely related to 7f AW winner No Nay Data (77), half-brother to 6f AW winner Hello Goodbye (81); dam unraced; respected source and appeal on paper; market best guide.
runners.comment: Sioux Nation colt, related to 2yo winner, respected source, market can guide on debut

runners.name: Two Brigade
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeat on debut when slowly away and last of 8 at the Curragh (5f, sft-hvy) in the race in which Confucius finished second; could improve plenty for quicker ground but can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Slow away and last of 8 at Curragh, better ground could suit but can only be watched

races.race_name: Coonan Cawley LLP In Support Of McAuley Place Handicap

runners.name: Inishfallen
runners.spotlight: Sole win in June 2023; in good form on the AW off this mark recently, beaten less than 1l the last twice; best turf form on fast ground and on a dangerous mark based on a 2l second at Windsor off 7lb higher last June; could go well.
runners.comment: Beaten less than 1l off this mark last twice on AW, on dangerous turf mark, could go well
runners.owner: F J Carragher/Yorkavenue Syndicate/Park Ave Syndicate

runners.name: Beauty Queen
runners.spotlight: Two novice wins for Kevin Ryan; one of better turf efforts for this yard came over 5f here last year off 4lb higher; below best on AW recently and others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Ran well over 5f here last year off 4lb higher, below best on AW recently

runners.name: Sporting Hero
runners.spotlight: Prolific AW performer gained a first turf success in emphatic fashion at Navan on latest (5f, gd-yld) under this rider, making virtually all for a 2.25l win; this longer trip fine but raised 10lb and this is a tougher race.
runners.comment: Up 10lb for first turf win over 5f at Navan on latest, moves up in trip, this is tougher

runners.name: Mehman
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner, sole turf success came in 5f handicap (good) on this card in 2024 and he's just 1lb higher now; solid effort over 1m at Bellewstown latest (soft); should get a strong pace to aim at here and not discounted.
runners.comment: Won over 5f at this meeting in 2024 off 1lb lower, solid 1m run  on latest, can't discount

runners.name: Ribble Emperor
runners.spotlight: Placed on all three AW starts at 7f-1m; turf form not as good and was well beaten on soft ground at the Curragh (5f) last month; better ground here likely a plus but others more convincing.
runners.comment: Solid AW form, not as good on turf, soundly beaten at the Curragh latest, others preferred

runners.name: Pinar Del Rio
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on turf at 6f-6.5f with all three victories coming on soft or heavy ground; has gone well on good ground, however, including when beaten a neck in this race last year off 11lb higher; below best latest but may have needed that and he could go well at a price.
runners.comment: Runner up in this last year off 11lb higher on good ground, could go well at a price

runners.name: Bint Majestic Roi
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner saw off Reposado by 2.25l at the Curragh (6f, soft) on latest and the runner up ran with credit off 4lb higher back there last weekend; however, she'll need a big career best off this mark and quick ground is a concern.
runners.comment: Up 12lb for first turf win on latest, big career best needed, quick ground a concern

runners.name: I Bid You Ajou
runners.spotlight: 3-20 on turf, all three wins at Fairyhouse last season; almost sprang a 33-1 surprise on seasonal return at Dundalk (6f) last month; 7f too far at Leopardstown on latest; not beaten far off 3lb higher over C&D last July; in the mix.
runners.comment: Three-time Fairyhouse winner, not beaten far off 3lb higher over C&D last July, in the mix

runners.name: Collective Power
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner is 3-33 on turf; hasn't won on grass since a 6f success at Cork (good) in June 2023; went close at Dundalk on latest (6f) and has gone well over this C&D before; place shout.
runners.comment: Three-time winner on turf, went close on AW latest, has gone well over C&D before
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Genesis
runners.spotlight: Two-time AW winner gained a breakthrough turf win at the Curragh (6f, good) on his seasonal return five days ago; 5lb rise for that 0.5l success seems fair and top rider booked; should go well again.
runners.comment: Up 5lb for first turf win at the Curragh latest, top rider booked, should go well again

runners.name: Usuario Amigo
runners.spotlight: 7f Brighton maiden win in September 2024; yet to score for this yard; best effort a 1.75l third in a rated race at Limerick last July (6.5f, good); heavy ground not ideal on seasonal return; conditions more suitable here.
runners.comment: Heavy ground not ideal on seasonal return, conditions more suitable here

runners.name: Bundle Of Interest
runners.spotlight: Won off 4lb lower at Navan last July (6f, good); poor run over 1m when last seen at Dundalk in November and she needed her first run back last season; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won off 4lb lower at Navan last year, off since Nov', needed first run back last season

runners.name: Sommelier
runners.spotlight: Both wins on AW; turf form includes a listed second at York (5f, good) when 1.5l behind Big Evs; both starts for current yard on soft ground; showed a bit more last time and the return to better ground here is a big plus; drops in grade and handicapper giving him a chance; interesting.
runners.comment: Showed a bit more last time, return to faster ground a plus, mark sliding, interesting

races.race_name: Declan Landy Fencing Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Real Encounter
runners.spotlight: Debuted on turf and ran fine, solid AW form since; mark left untouched after ultimately snug maiden win over 5f last month, but she was pushed along at halfway then and stiff 6f here will likely suit better.
runners.comment: Mark left untouched after snug AW maiden win over 5f; fine at this trip too

runners.name: Michael's Well
runners.spotlight: Made 125,000gns at the breeeze-ups; second to Chicago Pope here on debut, went one better at Navan with odds-on Ocean's Breath only fourth; big drifter in market when poor at Cork on comeback, but capable of much better; has only encountered easy ground thus far.
runners.comment: Second to Chicago Pope here on debut, went one better at Navan; poor at Cork on comeback

runners.name: Joyful Tidings
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 5f has been held in handicap company thus far, but showed speed and only gave best inside final furlong when creditable fifth over C&D last time; place claims.
runners.comment: Showed speed and only gave best inside final furlong when creditable fifth over C&D

runners.name: White Smoke
runners.spotlight: Been a bit disappointing since finishing runner-up in pair of Curragh maidens over 6f; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Been a bit disappointing since finishing runner-up in pair of Curragh maidens over 6f

runners.name: Nakamura
runners.spotlight: Hard-knocking juvenile won pair of 6f handicaps and fourth in Birdcatcher over C&D; soft ground wasn't the reason for his poor comeback at Cork and Coen deserts him.
runners.comment: Hard-knocking juvenile won pair of 6f handicaps, fourth in Birdcatcher here; poor comeback

runners.name: Treasured Royal
runners.spotlight: Well held at stakes level after maiden win over this trip at Navan; 10-3 fav for C&D handicap on return, got a bump exiting stalls and ran with the choke out when always behind; hooded now and capable of better if settling.
runners.comment: Well held at stakes level after maiden win; fav for C&D h'cap, too keen and always behind

runners.name: Chicago Pope
runners.spotlight: Beat the debuting Michael's Well in C&D maiden (yielding) in October and second over 5f on testing ground at Cork on return, having led both times.
runners.comment: Beat Michael's Well in C&D maiden and second over 5f on return, having led both times
runners.owner: Anthony Paul Smurfit & Samuel M Mencoff

runners.name: Midnight Dusk
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner over 6f; 1.5l behind runner-up Chicago Pope when third over 5f at Cork (soft) last month, and they meet on the same terms here.
runners.comment: AW winner over 6f; 1.5l off runner-up Chicago Pope when third over 5f at Cork (soft)

runners.name: Ocean's Breath
runners.spotlight: Was still a bit weak physically when twice showing promise at Navan last year; odds-on for the second time when readily beating subsequent winner in AW maiden over 6f last month.
runners.comment: Promise at Navan last year; readily beat subsequent winner in AW maiden on seasonal debut

runners.name: Dawn Romance
runners.spotlight: C&D good-ground maiden winner in August on third start; well beaten in pair of stakes races since, including at Cork last weekend.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner in August on third start; well beaten in pair of stakes races since

runners.name: Tahcawin
runners.spotlight: Went from 70 to 84 after easy 6f nursery win at Fairyhouse in September; shaped well against older rivals over 7f on Gowran comeback, improved to go second a furlong out before hanging right and tiring; has clearly trained on and 6f will be better for her.
runners.comment: Went from 70 to 84 after easy 6f nursery win at Fairyhouse; shaped well over 7f on return

runners.name: Gavoo
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 6f at two, got up on the line when touching off Sovereign Cry (same terms today) at Dundalk in November; gelded since.
runners.comment: Raised 3lb for touching off Sovereign Cry over 6f at Dundalk in November; gelded since

runners.name: Jp's Express
runners.spotlight: Just okay runs in 7f maidens last year and well held over same trip on return; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Just okay runs in 7f maidens last year and well held over same trip on return; h'cap debut

runners.name: Sovereign Cry
runners.spotlight: Made 58,000 at the breeze-ups; progressive at Dundalk in early winter; too keen when unplaced favourite on debut, caught late by more experienced Gavoo over 6f (they meet on same terms here) and then got up close home to beat 82-rated filly over 5f; expected to be a stronger model this year and champion jockey aboard for handicap debut; good claims if not too fizzy after five months off.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW at Dundalk in early winter and champion jockey aboard for handicap debut
runners.owner: John B O'Hagan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Pass Me If You Can
runners.spotlight: Three AW runs at Dundalk over the winter; won well over 5f last time, and trainer never underestimated, but opening mark looks stiff.
runners.comment: Three AW runs at Dundalk over the winter; won well over 5f last time but mark looks stiff

races.race_name: Gresham House Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Cromac Quay
runners.spotlight: Third foal; closely related to Japanese 6f turf winner Irifi (inc 2yo); dam 1m 2yo winner and won 1m2f Listed contest here at three (RPR 105), closely related to 1m Listed winner Mooharib, out of maiden half-sister to 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winners Rewilding and Dar Re Mi; 33-1 for Leopardstown debut over this trip (good to yielding), bit green but got the message late on when promising third behind two smart types.
runners.comment: 33-1 for Leopardstown debut over this trip, got the message late on when promising third

runners.name: Feel Happy
runners.spotlight: Sister to 6f 2yo winner Jewelry (RPR 86), half-sister to winners Sir Lamorak (10.6f-1m3f inc AW/Australia; 108) and A New Dawn (Group-placed 6f 2yo; 104); dam 5f 2yo winner (95), half-sister to 1m4f Group 1 winner Bracelet; yard is strong this term in 3yo fillies' category.
runners.comment: Well-related debutante by Wootton Bassett is one to consider for in-form yard
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Flower Forest
runners.spotlight: Siyouni filly; half-sister to Canadian 7f turf winner War Strategy; dam 7f 2yo Group 3 winner (RPR 106), sister to Group 1 winners Gleneagles (7f/1m), Marvellous (1m), Happily (7f/1m) and Joan Of Arc (10.5f), out of 6f 2yo Group 2 winner; 10-1 stable second-string when fourth over 7f at Cork on testing ground (odds-on stablemate was second and won easily next time).
runners.comment: Was 10-1 stable second-string when fourth over 7f at Cork on testing ground
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Six Wings
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 77), sister to 7f winner Navarra Pace, half-sister to useful 1m4f winner Poetic Sound, out of 1m4f winning sister to German 1m4f Group 1 winner Yavana's Pace; tongue-tie is fitted for debut.
runners.comment: Third foal of 6f 2yo winner (RPR 77), family of German 1m4f Group 1 winner Yavana's Pace

runners.name: Closely
runners.spotlight: Daughter of No Nay Never has been beaten 6l over 6f last twice and 71-rated filly needs to improve plenty for going farther; taken out of a recent 1m handicap due to soft ground.
runners.comment: Beaten 6l over 6f last twice and 71-rated filly needs to improve plenty for going farther
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Haroussa
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; closely related to 11.5f/1m4f Flat (RPR 100) and 2m5f hurdle winner Hamsiyann, half-sister to winners Harannda (7f; 86) and Haroya (French 1m1f); dam 7f 2yo winner (inc Group 3; 109), half-sister to Derby winner Harzand.
runners.comment: Half-sister to winners Harannda (7f; 86) and Haroya (French 1m1f) from family of Harzand
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Hydream
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners inc Vadream (5f/6f inc 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 111), Humam (6f/7f inc 2yo/AW; 99) and Midream (6f; 94); dam maiden (69) half-sister to Australian 1m5f Group 2 winner Swashbuckling; bought in for E150,000 as a foal and again for E240,000 as a yearling.
runners.comment: Homebred half-sister to five winners inc Vadream (5f/6f inc 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 111)

runners.name: Monamie
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; dam 1m4f winner (RPR 91), closely related to useful 2m1f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Glenstal Abbey, half-sister to Group-placed 7f/1m winner Margay, out of half-sister to US 1m Grade 1 winner Buckhar.
runners.comment: Eighth foal out of 1m4f winner (RPR 91) whose progeny have achieved little to date

runners.name: Moonlit Sun
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to winners Blue For You (7f/1m inc 2yo; RPR 109), Soaring Sun (1m4f AW; 78) and Forgetmenotblue (2m; 64); dam 8.4f/1m1f winner (82), closely related to HK 1m2f Group 1 winner Designs On Rome; had a spin in a barrier trial last summer; second-string for Harrington yard.
runners.comment: Nice pedigree (family of Designs On Rome); had a spin in a barrier trial last summer

runners.name: Motta Alta
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Aubergine (RPR 74); dam Italian 5f/6f winner (inc 2yo), half-sister to winners Thunderbear (5f Group 3) and Dancing Feet (useful 6f/7f), out of maiden half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winner Move Up.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Aubergine (RPR 74); dam Italian 5f/6f winner

runners.name: Sparan Nua
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to 2m3f hurdle winner Churchwarden; dam 10.6f AW winner (RPR 74), half-sister to winners Admodum (1m-10.4f) and Jack Duggan (7.5f 2yo), out of unraced half-sister to 6f/7f 2yo Group 1 winner Saoirse Abu.
runners.comment: Half-sister to hurdles winner; dam 10.6f AW winner, family of Group 1 winner Saoirse Abu

runners.name: Chosen One
runners.spotlight: Second foal of 1m 2yo/1m2f Listed winner (RPR 112), sister to Irish Derby winner Latrobe, half-sister to winners Diamond Fields (6f 2yo/7f Group 3) and Entangling (useful 1m-1m4f); 12s, started slowly and never a factor on sole 2yo start at Dundalk.
runners.comment: Well-bred type started slowly and never a factor on sole start last year
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Hinder
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to six winners inc Babouche (6f inc 2yo Group 1; RPR 117), Zarinsk (6f-1m inc 2yo/Group 2), Derevo (1m2f/12.4f inc AW/KSA; 106) and Society Lion (6f-1m inc 2yo/AW; 102); dam 7f and 1m (Listed) winner (103); 5-2 for AW debut over 6f last month, underwhelming when beaten nearly 6l.
runners.comment: From strong Juddmonte family but underwhelming on 6f AW debut when beaten nearly 6l

runners.name: Serendipia
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal of trainer's Matron Stakes winner Fiesolana (116); half-sister to winners Up Helly Aa (8.5f-1m7f inc Listed; RPR 107), Earendel (French 10.5f; 91) and Innervisions (French 7f/7.5f); sent off 18-1, travelled nicely to the straight before getting tired when beaten 11l over 1m2f at Leopardstown; shorter trip may suit better and should take a nice step forward.
runners.comment: Travelled nicely to the straight before getting tired when beaten 11l over 1m2f on debut

runners.name: Rodeo Blues
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well held so far; turned out again promptly to get a mark.
runners.comment: Big prices and well held so far; turned out again promptly to get a mark
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited

races.race_name: Darley Irish EBF Naas Oaks Trial Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Eretria
runners.spotlight: Purchased for 420,000gns as a yearling; beaten 0.75l by stablemate Emmeleia (runner-up in Group 3 next time) over this trip at Dundalk, then bolted up over 1m2f at Leopardstown; holds entries for Oaks/Irish Oaks.
runners.comment: Bolted up in 1m2f maiden at Leopardstown; holds entries for Oaks/Irish Oaks.

runners.name: Caught U Sleeping
runners.spotlight: Winner and Listed-placed over 7f at two; hooded when disappointing in Guineas trial over that trip on comeback; despite it being her sixth run, still looked raw when 50-1 for 1m Group 3 at the Curragh on Monday, off the bridle at halfway before making some late gains; 1m2f should suit, but not sure she'll be quick enough to cope with some of these.
runners.comment: 50-1 for 1m Group 3 at the Curragh, off the bridle halfway before making some late gains

runners.name: Mixed Feelings
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly was only a length off Ice Dancer in sole start at two; 11-10 fav when hosing up in 1m Leopardstown maiden last month, but what did she beat is the question? Still, it was impressive and connections think she'll improve for better ground; there are stayers further back in her pedigree.
runners.comment: 11-10 fav when hosing up in 1m Leopardstown maiden, but what did she beat is the question?

runners.name: River Ara
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when making all in back-end 1m nursery at Leopardstown (yielding to soft); unable to land a blow in 7f Guineas Trial back there last month, but finished in close proximity to some useful types and she's bred to stay this far.
runners.comment: Made all in back-end 1m nursery, unable to land a blow in 7f Guineas Trial; should stay
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: La Fogata
runners.spotlight: Last three juvenile starts were all over 1m; beaten 5l in six-runner Curragh Group 3, strong at the line when landing Punchestown maiden, then weak in the market when last of eight in another Curragh Group 3 (heavy ground a possible excuse); with Ger Lyons at two, has changed hands and now starts off for Eretria's trainer; entered in both Oaks and dam was Stakes-class at up to 1m6f.
runners.comment: Some good 2yo form over 1m for Ger Lyons and steps up in trip for stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Ice Dancer
runners.spotlight: Winner and Group 3-placed over 1m on heavy in the autumn; forget about her run at Navan a fortnight ago (found to be showing signs of coming into season), but what was significant was that Moore picked her over 10lb higher-rated stablemate (who, admittedly, also ran poorly); pedigree implies she can improve at this trip and entered in both Oaks.
runners.comment: Winner and Group 3-placed over 1m on heavy; readily excused her poor run at Navan
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Pollenca
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Curragh maiden on heavy; 16s for 1m Group 3 back there on Moday, ran green and was blocked in her run when starting to stay on from rear in the last quarter-mile; quick turnaround but could go well.
runners.comment: 16s for 1m Group 3 at the Curragh, blocked in her run when starting to stay on

races.race_name: TRM Race, Replace, Rehydrate Rated Race

runners.name: Perisher
runners.spotlight: Consistent in defeat over 7f/1m before narrowly getting the better of a debuting stablemate over 1m at Gowran, finding plenty off the bridle for McDonogh who said post-race that the gelding might like 10 furlongs.
runners.comment: Consistent in defeat before beating stablemate narrowly over 1m at Gowran, finding plenty

runners.name: Pierre Grosse
runners.spotlight: Quietly fancied and seemed to improve for this trip when placed in Leopardstown handicap on his return, and that can be marked up because he was on the slower ground up the straight; most of the Harrington string are coming on for their first runs, too, so lots to like.
runners.comment: Quietly fancied and seemed to improve for this trip when placed in Leopardstown handicap

runners.name: Galileo Noir
runners.spotlight: 1m1f maiden winner on soft to heavy at the Curragh and placed horses both won next time out; Keane thought he could go back in trip, trainer thinks he'll get this far.
runners.comment: 1m1f winner on soft to heavy at the Curragh and placed horses both won next time

runners.name: Marjorie Daw
runners.spotlight: Landed 1m Gowran maiden on heavy in October, her yard's last winner but a few have run well lately; dead in the market and ran accordingly on return over this trip at Leopardstown, long way behind Pierre Grosse.
runners.comment: 1m winner on heavy at two; dead in the market and ran accordingly on return over this trip
runners.owner: T O'Sullivan & S R Stafford & Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Freedom And Light
runners.spotlight: Better last time (150-1) when stepping up to this trip at Leopardstown, beaten 9l by promising type who contests the preceding Oaks Trial today; wasn't far off the placed horses, but more needed all the same.
runners.comment: Better latest (150-1) when stepping up to this trip, beaten 9l by smart type; more needed

runners.name: Golddream
runners.spotlight: Won at 22-1 over 1m on AW; pedigree suggests he's almost certain to improve for this trip, his brother a French 10.5f winner, dam a Listed winner over 1m7f and stayed even farther.
runners.comment: Won at 22-1 over 1m on AW; pedigree suggests he's almost certain to improve for this trip

races.race_name: Killarney Avenue Hotel Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: North Shore
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; 1m4f winner in France on debut (soft); highly tried in 2m juvenile hurdles last season, finishing 11l behind top prospect Narciso Has in a Leopardstown Grade 2 (yielding) and then Grade 1 (heavy); didn't appear to give true running in Triumph; fell three out at Fairyhouse last time; big player dropped to this level.
runners.comment: Highly tried last season and ran with credit; big player in this company

runners.name: Whats New
runners.spotlight: Flat winner has placed a couple of times over hurdles, oftentimes exhibiting quirky behaviour; fourth at Bellewstown last time (2m1f, yielding) when 17.5l behind the winner; fighting it out for a place maybe the best he can hope for.
runners.comment: Flat winner was fourth in a m'den last time and maybe fighting for the places once again

runners.name: Portnahapple
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in both Curragh maiden starts for Andy Oliver at 1m2f/1m4f on yielding/yielding to soft; gelded since and now makes hurdling debut for champion trainer; player.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both Curragh m'dens and now makes hurdling debut for powerful connections

runners.name: Tribal
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Hill Station (1m3f/1m4f AW, RPR 73; 2m hurdle) and Nomadic Rebel (French 12.5f); dam French 1m2f-1m5f winner (inc AW), half-sister to French 1m4f/12.5f winner Pegasus Bridge; unlikely to make immediate impact.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two winners inc' Hill Station (RPR 93); unlikely to make immediate impact

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: Lowly-rated Flat maiden after eight starts; hurdles need to bring mammoth improvement.
runners.comment: Lowly-rated Flat maiden after eight starts; hurdles need to bring mammoth improvement
runners.owner: Mrs M Cahill & Mrs Caroline Hendron & Peter Robin Parkhill

runners.name: Fergy Five
runners.spotlight: El Salvador gelding is a half-brother to the yard's useful 2m5f-3m hurdle winner Sequestered; dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m7f-3m6f chase winner Outlaw Pete; may need further than this in time and best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to yard's useful Sequestered (RPR 144); may need further and best watched

runners.name: Messire Des Bordes
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; 65,000euros 3yo; dam placed in French 2m2f hurdle, half-sister to three winners, including high-class Kopek Des Bordes; tailed off at Bellewstown on hurdling debut and is stable second string once again.
runners.comment: Related to Kopek Des Bordes but tailed off at Bellewstown on debut

runners.name: Osco Flyer
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; 21,000euros foal, 9,500euros 3yo; second foal; dam bumper and 2m2f/2m4f hurdle winner, half-sister to 2m4f hurdle/3m chase winner Hall Lane, out of unraced sister to smart bumper/2m-2m4f hurdle winner Rouble; check market.
runners.comment: Dam bumper and 2m2f/2m4f hurdle winner; check market for debut

runners.name: Straightenyourhand
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Kreb's Cycle (5f-1m inc 2yo/AW; RPR 85) and Highest Rank (6f 2yo; 69); dam French 1m1f/1m2f winner (inc AW), out of German 1m Listed winning half-sister to 2,000 Guineas second Snow Ridge; unlikely.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two Flat winners inc' Kreb's Cycle; unlikely

runners.name: Time For Talking
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to point/2m5f chase winner Hollywood Harmon; dam unraced half-sister to winners Bob Lingo (useful bumper and 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase) and Talk The Lingo (2m2f-3m hurdle/chase), out of half-sister to useful 2m-2m5f chase winner Dev; best watched.
runners.comment: Half-brother to point/2m5f chase winner Hollywood Harmon; best watched

runners.name: Who's Da Goose
runners.spotlight: Brother to point winner More Than A Mule; dam bumper/2m hurdle winner, half-sister to useful winners Cultivator (bumper/2m-2m5f hurdle), Magna Sam (3m-3m7f chase) and Scotsbrook Cloud (2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase); best watched.
runners.comment: Brother to point winner More Than A Mule; best watched

runners.name: Pillartou
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in only point-to-point start; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: PU in only point start; hard to fancy

runners.name: Royal Alliance
runners.spotlight: AW winner for George Boughey in 2024; no positive Flat form since joining this yard; goes hurdling now.
runners.comment: No positive Flat form since joining this yard; goes hurdling now

runners.name: Bravemans Bounce
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; sent off at 200-1 on debut at Navan and ran like it.
runners.comment: Sent off 200-1 on debut and ran like it

runners.name: Breeze Of Tobruk
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in opening two maidens, the latest of which at a whopping 500-1.
runners.comment: Tailed off in opening two maidens, the latest of which at a whopping 500-1

runners.name: Earthwatch
runners.spotlight: Flat winner for William Haggas ahcieved a peak rating of 81 in that sphere; soundly beaten on debut for this yard in a Dundalk claimer and tailed off on hurdling bow at Cork last month; unlikely.
runners.comment: Flat winner was tailed off at Cork on hurdles/stable debut when 50-1; unlikely

races.race_name: Mongey Communications Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Bud Fox
runners.spotlight: Form of his debut bumper win has worked out well but disappointing since; beaten favourite in a Cheltenham November bumper when third and weakened tamely at Leopardstown; hurdles didn't spark a return to form at Down Royal last time; big chance if back to form of bumper win, but that's a big if.
runners.comment: Form of debut bumper win worked out well but disappointing since; chance if bouncing back

runners.name: Tyson Fury
runners.spotlight: Added fourth Flat win in a Dundalk claimer in March; creditable efforts in three starts over hurdles; distant runner-up at Tramore last time (2m, soft); not totally discounted.
runners.comment: Four-time Flat winner has run creditably in three starts over hurdles; not dismissed

runners.name: Pure Logic
runners.spotlight: Consistent placed form in bumpers, and took second on hurdling debut at Ballinrobe last year (2m1f, good); a bit below that when last seen at Taunton in November; should run her race but likely to find a few too good.
runners.comment: Consistent mare should run her race but likely to find a few too good

runners.name: Goodmancon
runners.spotlight: Still winless after nine bumper starts and his hurdling debut last time when third (1.75l in front of Whet The Appetite); others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-9 in bumpers; third on hurdling debut last time but others preferred

runners.name: Minella Mate
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in June 2023 added a Galway Flat maiden later that summer; lightly raced since but ran well on AW when runner-up on penultimate start; disappointed as favourite in sole hurdling start in 2023.
runners.comment: Bumper/Flat winner ran well on AW recently; disappointed in sole hurdles start in 2023

runners.name: Magico
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner for Marco Botti in Britain; purchased for 89,000gns in August; brings a Flat rating of 89 for his hurdling/stable debut; should be suited by this better ground and of obvious interest given trainer/rider combination.
runners.comment: Brings Flat rating of 89 following three British wins; of obvious interest on hurdles bow

runners.name: Strong Contender
runners.spotlight: Has it all to do on basis of opening three maiden hurdle runs.
runners.comment: Has it all to do on basis of opening three maiden hurdle runs

runners.name: Whet The Appetite
runners.spotlight: Good fist of improvement over moderate debut when fourth at Tramore last month at 50-1; needs to keep going the right way.
runners.comment: Improved over moderate debut when fourth at 50-1 last time; needs to keep improving

runners.name: Jerry From Kerry
runners.spotlight: Beaten five times in winners' bumpers since Ballinrobe success on good ground in 2024 for Emmet Mullins; tailed off at Thurles on hurdling bow and in midfield when a faller two out at Naas; makes stable debut now.
runners.comment: 1-7 in bumpers; tailed off on hurdles bow and faller last time; makes stable debut

runners.name: Ellis Brittle
runners.spotlight: Dam well beaten in bumpers, half-sister to winners Smitty Bacall (bumper and 2m4f-3m2f hurdle/chase), Palm Sur (bumper) and Sullivan's Brow (2m hurdle), out of close relative of smart 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Blazing Tempo; best watched.
runners.comment: Dam well beaten in bumpers; best watched

runners.name: Backinbusiness
runners.spotlight: First reserve; pulled up in first point-to-point but took a 6l third of 11 next time; much more needed.
runners.comment: Reserve; PU in first point but took a 6l third of 11 next time; much more needed

runners.name: Calzaghi
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; winner of a Navan Flat handicap in 2024 (1m2f, good to yielding); placed in final three starts for Kevin Coleman on differing ground types before being purchased for 29,000gns; brings a Flat rating of 78 to this hurdles/stable debut.
runners.comment: Flat winner was placed in final three starts for Kevin Coleman; makes hurdles/stable bow

runners.name: Approval
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; progressive 3yo on Flat for William Haggas in 2024, winning twice over 1m2f on good to firm; hasn't reached those heights since, including in latest two starts for Owen Burrows; purchased for 16,000gns in October and goes hurdling now in new headgear.
runners.comment: Progressive 3yo on Flat in 2024, winning twice; hasn't reached those heights since

runners.name: Individualism
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; won twice on the Flat for Charlie Johnston on good ground; rated 83 in that sphere; signed off British career with a tailed off finish at Hamilton in September; makes hurdling/stable debut now in a first-time tongue tie.
runners.comment: 2-19 on Flat for Charlie Johnston; makes hurdling/stable debut now

runners.name: Ancient Myth
runners.spotlight: Bought for 10,000gns after losing his form in two starts following a breakthrough Flat win at Thirsk (1m4f, good to firm) in August for David O'Meara; others more likely.
runners.comment: Purchased for 10,000gns after losing form in two starts after breakthrough Flat win

runners.name: Money Shuffler
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; yet to demonstrate winning potential in two bumpers and four Flat starts; goes hurdling now.
runners.comment: Reserve; yet to demonstrate winning potential in two bumpers and four Flat starts

runners.name: Chou Chou
runners.spotlight: Didn't jump with fluency when running no race in sole hurdles start at Punchestown in 2024 for Noel Meade; bagged a Flat win at Clonmel in September off 48 (1m2f, soft); moderate form in three Flat starts since.
runners.comment: Only hurdles start in 2024 was poor; Flat rating is not promising

runners.name: Screaming Eagle
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 73-rated Flat maiden after eight starts; hasn't made much impact in two maiden hurdle starts so far.
runners.comment: Reserve; 73-rated Flat maiden hasn't made much impact in two m'den hurdles so far

runners.name: Royad
runners.spotlight: Winner of a French AW Flat race in 2024 before being purchaed for 41,000euros; well beaten in sole hurdles start in July (2m, good); 306-day layoff to overcome.
runners.comment: French AW winner was well beaten over hurdles on Irish debut in July; absent since

races.race_name: MD O'Shea's Tourist Attraction Mares Hurdle (Listed Race)

runners.name: Break My Soul
runners.spotlight: Dual winning hurdler for Nicky Henderson and took fourth in Grade 1 company behind Lossiemouth at Aintree in 2025 (2m4f, good to soft); made a winning chase debut for this yard, and ran a valiant race when third in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham (2m, good); pulled up at Fairyhouse when last seen off top weight; top-rated of these reverting to hurdles; new headgear.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler had a positive campaign over fences last season; the top-rated of these

runners.name: Sainte Lucie
runners.spotlight: Back to life when seventh in the Martin Pipe (2m5f, soft) at Cheltenham after not getting the clearest run and built on that to win by 3.25l at Cork (2m3f, yielding) under a claimer; Townend takes over now and she looks like a mare on an upward curve having disappointed earlier in her career.
runners.comment: Built on return to form in Martin Pipe when winning at Cork; appears on an upward curve

runners.name: Royal Hollow
runners.spotlight: Flat winner has landed three hurdle races, the latest of which came at Gowran in October in a conditions event (2m1f, soft); proved her health when winning her chasing debut at Clonmel last month; a small bit to find on ratings but by no means dismissed.
runners.comment: Three-time winning hurdler has a small bit to find on ratings but by no means dismissed

runners.name: Redwood Queen
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since joining this yard last year after unseating with the race at her mercy in a Wexford claimer; decent efforts in opening two runs for this yard, but not at her best recently; needs to bounce back; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Hasn't won since joining this yard and poor below best last twice; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Disco Dancer
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; bumper/dual winning hurdler; returned from almost a year off when well beaten on Flat in February; more promise in 10l fifth over hurdles at Navan (2m, soft); didn't build on that at Bellewstown last time.
runners.comment: Well beaten last time and needs to go beyond her best to win

runners.name: Dunsy Rock
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; built upon the promise of her debut run at Down Royal (2m4f, yielding) in December to win by 6l in a big field at Fairyhouse (2m, heavy) in February; was withdrawn based on unsuitable ground at Perth last month so these conditions a question; this is just her third start, so she could outrun her rating.
runners.comment: 6l winner of second start in Feb'; ground a question but could outrun rating

runners.name: Tir Og
runners.spotlight: Progressed really well in the early part of last season, rising 30lb in ratings; form has levelled off since and was beaten 24l at Fairyhouse last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Progressed well early last season but form has levelled off since; others preferred

runners.name: Bynx
runners.spotlight: Recorded a third hurdles win off a career low mark at Thurles in October (2m4f, good to yielding), beating Tir Og by a neck; decent enough sixth on Flat at Gowran back from a break last month, considering she stumbled at the start; she will have easier opportunities than this.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdles winner will have better winning opportunities than this

runners.name: Queenofthelodge
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicapper was gaining a third win of 2025 when landing a 2m4f Clonmel mares' handicap in October in first-time tongue tie (yielding); her two outings in December against better company were not her best; the break may have done her good, but a bit to find.
runners.comment: Progressive last year, winning three; below best last time but maybe better after break

runners.name: Katherine
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner has been disappointing in Flat/hurdle starts recently and has it all to do on these terms.
runners.comment: Disappointing in most recent Flat/hurdle starts; has it all to do on these terms

races.race_name: Kelly Bros Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Playtime
runners.spotlight: Handicap win over 1m4f (good) at Chester was the highlight of a five-race British Flat career; off the mark hurdling at fourth attempt with an easy Leopardstown victory (2m, soft to heavy); well beaten in a Fairyhouse Listed Leopardstown (2m, yielding to soft) contest last time for handicap debut; this is easier; non-runner 5.20 at Chester on Friday.
runners.comment: Flat/hurdles winner was well beaten on h'cap debut last time; this is easier

runners.name: Wholelotofbusiness
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; in decent form in the second half of last year, placing three times before gaining a C&D novice hurdle win (yielding); another positive effort when placed at Down Royal next time (2m1f, yielding) off 1lb lower; may come on for moderate return at Cork last time (2m3f, yielding).
runners.comment: C&D novice hurdle winner; may improve for fair return latest

runners.name: Ragmans Corner
runners.spotlight: Flat winner got his third hurdles win under this rider at Navan in September (2m, yielding) off 9lb lower; 1l behind Wholelotofbusiness when fourth at Down Royal in November (worse off at weights now); another positive effort when second back at Navan (2m, soft to heavy) when last seen in November.
runners.comment: Three-time winning hurdler was second at Navan when last seen in Nov'

runners.name: Cappa Hill
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back handicaps at Cork (2m1f, good) and Tipperary (2m, good to yielding) in August/October; tailed off in a Punchestown novice handicap next time (2m1f, soft to heavy); not too bad at Cork last month when eighth of 19 (2m3f, yielding); could build on that and this ground is what he wants.
runners.comment: Back-to-back h'cap winner last season; could build on okay run latest and ground suits

runners.name: Becasse
runners.spotlight: Bought for 40,000euros after winning a French hurdle race on very soft in October 2024; returned from a 519-day absence with a positive runner-up finish at Wexford (2m, soft) despite being unfancied in the market (11-1); could step forward now but has never raced on this better ground.
runners.comment: Returned from lengthy absence with positive second at Wexford; could step forward

runners.name: Impero
runners.spotlight: Five hurdle wins came on goodish ground at 2m-2m4; last success came when 10l in front at Kelso last May; not at his best at Stratford next time, nor on the Flat at Galway when last seen; has won after a break before.
runners.comment: Five hurdle wins came on this ground; not at best in last two runs; won after break before

runners.name: Fast Felix
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; C&D maiden winner here last summer (good) and added a second hurdles win at Listowel (2m, good to yielding) next time when vanquishing Paul's Dream into a 3.25l second; form dropped off in three subsequent runs, including here; break needs to have done him good; remains 6lb above last winning mark.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner inc' C&D; form dropped off latterly; break needs to have done him good

runners.name: Paul's Dream
runners.spotlight: Added second hurdles win when victorious at Fairyhouse in October (2m, yielding) off 7lb lower; not done with when departing at the second last at Cheltenham but unseen since tailed off at Navan (2m4f, soft to heavy) in November; ground should suit.
runners.comment: Added second hurdles win at Fairyhouse off 7lb lower but unseen since tailed off in Nov'

runners.name: Icare Desbois
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper and maiden hurdle over 2m for Willie Mullins in an interrupted career; form for current yard over hurdles and Flat suggests he needs to find a fair bit of improvement.
runners.comment: Hurdles/Flat form for current yard suggests he needs to find a fair bit of improvement

runners.name: Walhaan
runners.spotlight: Seven-time Flat winner won a Leopardstown maiden hurdle in March 2023 (2m, good to yielding) but generally moderate form over hurdles since then; the same can be said of his recent outing on the level at Leopardstown last month.
runners.comment: Seven-time Flat winner has shown generally moderate form over timber since winning in 2023

races.race_name: Gerard O'Sullivan Memorial Novice Chase

runners.name: Butch Cassidy
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner in 2024 hasn't really built on that promise; ran a fine race on second chase start at Punchestown in December but not as good since; bled when pulled-up last time at Fairyhouse so questions now.
runners.comment: Ran a fine race on second chase start but not as good since; bled last time so questions

runners.name: Sandor Clegane
runners.spotlight: Useful sort at his best and has run well in defeat in Grade 1 company over hurdles and fences; finally got off the mark over fences at Down Royal last time over 2m4f; this trip will suit better; a big player.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over fences at Down Royal last time; this trip will suit better; player

runners.name: Da Capo Glory
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins and ran a fine race in the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham, beaten only 8l; unluckily unseated at Punchestown last week; his best chase effort of three came at Gowran in February; gets weight from some of his rivals today and has a solid chance.
runners.comment: Three hurdle wins and fine run in the Martin Pipe; best chase effort came at Gowran in Feb

runners.name: Justabitofyours
runners.spotlight: Maiden in all spheres was pulled-up on last two chase starts and can't be fancied in this grade.
runners.comment: Maiden was pulled-up on last two chase starts and can't be fancied in this grade

runners.name: D Art D Art
runners.spotlight: Had been in good form over hurdles in the 2024/25 season but was regressive in the last year in that sphere; however, he  staged a revival to beat the classy Hiddenvalley Lake on chase debut at Wexford two days ago; respected if turning out againx.
runners.comment: Regressive over hurdles in last year but staged a revival to win on chase debut at Wexford

runners.name: The Rebel County
runners.spotlight: Hunter chase winner at Tipperary last year has been soundly beaten over hurdles and fences lately and has lots to find in this company.
runners.comment: Hunter chase winner has been soundly beaten over hurdles and fences lately; lots to find

races.race_name: Donie Sheahan Memorial Irish EBF Mares Beginners Chase

runners.name: Miss Doyenne
runners.spotlight: Was a point and bumper winner last year; seemed to get stuck in heavy ground at Punchestown on her second hurdle start in January; much better when winning at Down Royal last time over 2m4f; should go well on chase debut.
runners.comment: Point and bumper winner won over hurdles at Down Royal last time; go well on chase debut

runners.name: Emily Love
runners.spotlight: One of her five hurdle wins came here over 2m4f last year; ended her campaign with a win in a valuable mares' hurdle at Far Hills in USA and is rated 129 in that sphere; has won when fresh and is a big player if ready after the break.
runners.comment: One of five hurdle wins came here; rated 129 in that sphere; player if ready after break

runners.name: Billie Frechette
runners.spotlight: Flat and hurdle winner is rated 120 in the latter sphere after showing consistent form last season; ran well enough on chase bow at Kilbeggan, albeit beaten 17l; can improve from that and be involved.
runners.comment: Flat and hurdle winner ran well enough on chase bow at Kilbeggan and can be involved

runners.name: Gaoth Chuil
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins and some fine efforts in competitive handicaps; some decent chase efforts, notably behind Kilbarry Saint at Clonmel but was below her best at Kilbeggan last time; a chance if rebounding.
runners.comment: Three hurdle wins and some decent chase efforts but below best at Kilbeggan last time

runners.name: La Note Verte
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in 2024 won twice over hurdles last summer but wasn't at her best on last two 2025 runs; however, she is a quality sort and can go well fresh so is respected on chase bow.
runners.comment: Bumper and dual hurdle winner wasn't at best on last two 2025 runs; respected on chase bow

runners.name: Fortunate Lighting
runners.spotlight: Won a Sligo handicap hurdle last August but was poor twice after; a better effort on return but is rated 111 over hurdles and has a bit to find with some of these on chase debut.
runners.comment: Rated 111 over hurdles and has a bit to find with some of these on chase debut

runners.name: Crescent Moon
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner last year but lost her form in that sphere; has run well at times over fences but pulled-up last two tries and not easy to fancy.
runners.comment: Has run well at times over fences but pulled-up last two tries and not easy to fancy

runners.name: Stormalong
runners.spotlight: Her three hurdle wins includes a Listed hurdle at this meeting last year; hasn't matched that form in five starts over fences yet but is capable of better and returning to this track may help.
runners.comment: Won a Listed hurdle at this meeting last year but hasn't matched that over fences yet

runners.name: Affinity Rock
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m6f maiden hurdle at this meeting last year but was poor in a Galway handicap next time; absent since and needs to improve significantly for this chasing debut.
runners.comment: Won a 2m6f maiden hurdle at this meeting last year but poor in a h'cap next time; absence

runners.name: My Mary Ellen
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in four point starts and looks way out of her depth on rules bow with cheekpieces on.
runners.comment: Failed to win in four point starts and looks out of her depth on rules bow

runners.name: Beauforts Storm
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner is rated 124 over hurdles but has been nowhere near that level in two chase runs last season and has to do way more now.
runners.comment: Rated 124 over hurdles but nowhere near that in two chase runs and has to do way more

runners.name: Seventy Eight Team
runners.spotlight: Maiden in all disciplines is rated just 83 over fences so has a mountain to climb in this company.
runners.comment: Maiden is rated just 83 over fences so has a mountain to climb in this company

races.race_name: Tote Killarney National Handicap Chase

runners.name: O'Moore Park
runners.spotlight: Point and hurdle winner is yet to score over fences but has lots of fine efforts to his name; only beaten 13l in the competitive Topham Chase at Aintree last time; this is a bit easier so has to be on the shortlist even though is unproven over this far.
runners.comment: Only beaten 13l in the Topham Chase at Aintree; this is easier so is on the shortlist

runners.name: Kamikaz Du Plessis
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner is rated 128 in that sphere; hasn't been as good over fences in four starts but best effort came when fourth in a Clonmel novice last time; raced a bit awkwardly then; will need plenty more today.
runners.comment: Fourth in a Clonmel novice last time; was a bit awkward then; will need plenty more today

runners.name: Smallcraftwarning
runners.spotlight: Sole chase win came at this track over 2m7f last year; some decent runs in defeat since but faces a tough task here on return from 8lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Course winner but faces a tough task here on return from 8lb out of the handicap

runners.name: West Cork Wildway
runners.spotlight: Course hurdle winner is 1-12 over fences but that came back in 2021; runner-up over fences at Limerick in March but was disappointing over hurdles at Cork since.
runners.comment: Runner-up over fences at Limerick but was disappointing over hurdles at Cork since

runners.name: The Short Go
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle here in 2023 and is 1-10 over fences; hasn't had much racing in the last two years but ran two fine races in valuable Cheltenham handicaps last winter; not as good after wind surgery in the Ultima handicap last time; player if at best.
runners.comment: Course winner; not at best after wind surgery in the Ultima handicap last time; needs best

runners.name: Happy Jacky
runners.spotlight: Eight hurdle wins including once at this track; got off the mark over fences in a Kilbeggan beginners' last year before beaten 13l into third in this race; 2lb lower today but can go well fresh so could be involved.
runners.comment: A 13l 3rd in this race last year; 2lb lower today; can go well fresh so could be involved

runners.name: Pride Of Place
runners.spotlight: Won over fences at Tipperary and Listowel up to 3m last year; struggled off higher marks in softer ground after but could do better back on a sound surface.
runners.comment: Struggled in softer ground after wins last year; could do better back on a sound surface

runners.name: Caesar Rock
runners.spotlight: A record of 1-29 over fences but has been placed lots of times including at Limerick and Wexford the last twice; needs to do more but shouldn't be far away off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Placed at Limerick and Wexford the last twice; needs to do more but should go well

runners.name: Midnight Our Fred
runners.spotlight: Was third in this race in 2024 when the easy ground went against him; best form since was when runner-up in that year's Paddy Power Chase; hasn't regained his best form since sustaining an injury in the 2025 Fulke Walwyn Chase and needs a revival
runners.comment: Third in this in 2024; not at his best last season and needs a revival; good ground helps
runners.owner: Mrs Florence Lockwood & Emily B Lockwood

runners.name: Shanbally Kid
runners.spotlight: Bumper and hurdle winner won a valuable chase at Punchestown last year over just short of 4m; not as good off higher marks since and unseated there last time; looks the yard's second-string on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: Won at Punchestown last year but not as good since; unseated back there last time

runners.name: Hermino AA
runners.spotlight: Three wins over hurdles when trained in the UK and was Grade 3-placed; below that form in four chase starts for this yard and has to stage a revival.
runners.comment: Below best form in four chase starts for this yard and has to stage a revival

runners.name: Supreme Gift
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; won five times over hurdles and once over fences when trained in UK; three chase starts for this yard with best run coming when fifth at Limerick; needs a fair bit more.
