runners.comment: Three chase starts for this yard; best run when fifth at Limerick; needs a fair bit more

runners.name: The Gradual Slope
runners.spotlight: Won for the second time over fences when taking a Fairyhouse handicap over 2m5f last time despite jumping right late on; raised 5lb for that in a stronger contest so will have to do more from 1lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Won a Fairyhouse handicap over 2m5f last time; up 5lb so will have to do more; 1lb wrong

races.race_name: Hugh Mulryan Memorial (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Mike Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: French-bred posted a solid effort on debut at Leopardstown behind a subsequent Punchestown winner; that was in testing ground but if he handles this terrain as well should get involved with more to come.
runners.comment: Solid debut effort behind a subsequent Punchestown winner; get involved with more to come

runners.name: Fleu Du Glaye
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Batonnier (bumper/useful 2m4f hurdle) and Le Bazin (prolific French 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase); by a useful sire and cost Eur30,000 as a 3yo; likely to improve from this; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: By a useful sire and cost Eur30,000 as a 3yo; likely to improve from this; tongue-tie

runners.name: Alright Kid
runners.spotlight: Twelfth foal; half-brother to winners Master Dancer (1m-2m Flat inc 2yo, RPR 77; useful 2m and 2m5f hurdle) and A Song For You (7.5f/1m; 80); by a Leger winner and cost Eur8,000 as a 3yo; yard won this in 2022 so is worth a market check.
runners.comment: By a Leger winner and cost Eur8,000 as a 3yo; yard won this in 2022; worth a market check

runners.name: Hill Silver
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to winners Ellen Kelly (dual bumper) and Metalor (point); dam unraced half-sister to smart 10.5f-2m winner Litigant; decent pedigree but made only Eur2,000 as a 3yo; likely to improve from this.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree but made only Eur2,000 as a 3yo; likely to improve from this

runners.name: Knocknasuff
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; closely related to 5f 2yo/6f winner Pals Battalion (inc AW; RPR 93); dam French maiden from 6f-11.5f (73), half-sister to 10.6f/1m4f Listed winner Fire Fighting; Flat-bred was a Eur3,000 vendor buyback as a yearling; one to watch on debut.
runners.comment: Flat-bred was a Eur3,000 vendor buyback as a yearling; one to watch on debut

runners.name: Kolirisk De Couely
runners.spotlight: Brother to French 2m2f hurdle/chase winner Koliriska, half-brother to French 2m1f hurdle winner Kerkadova; dam well held only start over hurdles; by a top French sire and cost Eur52,000 as a 3yo; respected yard won this last year and the market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: By a top sire; cost Eur52,000 as a 3yo; respected yard and the market can guide on debut

runners.name: Linford
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to French winners Lady Barbarella (2m2f-2m6f jump) and Erik Le Viking (2m2f chase); dam maiden hurdler (best run at 3m2f), half-sister to 2m7f hurdle winner Dream Deal; by a leading French sire and maximum respect from this yard especially if strong in the market.
runners.comment: By a leading French sire and maximum respect from this yard especially if strong in market

runners.name: Mountain Sky
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam Group-placed 1m2f winner (RPR 96), sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Vancouverite, half-sister to 9.5f Group 2 UAE Derby winner Khawlah (dam of Derby winner Masar), from the family of Sea The Stars and Galileo; sold by Godolphin unraced for 40,000Gns last autumn; fine Flat pedigree and a top rider aboard; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Has a fine Flat pedigree and a top rider aboard; worth a market check

runners.name: Night Chaperone
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to bumper winner Will Wilde; dam fourth over 1m1f/1m3f in France, sister to point winner On Se Calme, half-sister to smart French/US 7.5f-8.5f winner Yorktown; cost Eur27,000 as a yearling; decent pedigree and could run well on debut.
runners.comment: Cost Eur27,000 as a yearling; decent pedigree and could run well on debut

runners.name: Society Gent
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners incl' Minella Awards (smart 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Montys Meadow (useful bumper/2m hurdle/2m4f chase); made Eur8,500 as a foal but only Eur5,000 as a 3yo; likely to improve from this debut run.
runners.comment: Made Eur8,500 as a foal but only Eur5,000 as a 3yo; likely to improve for this
runners.owner: Natalie Gilligan & Mark Watson & Brian J O'Higgins

runners.name: Telegram Sam
runners.spotlight: Pulled-up in a point on debut in testing ground; decent enough pedigree (family of Remittance Man) so may do better.
runners.comment: Pulled-up in a point on debut in testing ground; decent enough pedigree so may do better

runners.name: Lucky Signal
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on debut at Leopardstown on heavy ground, albeit behind a smart winner; debut for a new yard and likely to do better.
runners.comment: Well beaten on debut at Leopardstown on heavy ground; new yard and likely to do better

runners.name: Mischievous Fun
runners.spotlight: Has a useful Flat pedigree but was beaten a long way in an Academy hurdle over Christmas and has to improve hugely; respected yard.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in an Academy hurdle over Christmas and has to improve hugely

runners.name: Robbie Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Did too much from the front when beaten 36l in a Punchestown bumper; has to settle better.
runners.comment: Did too much from the front when beaten 36l in a Punchestown bumper; has to settle

races.race_name: Family Funday 31st May Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bayside
runners.spotlight: Cracksman filly who is out of half-sister to winners Hazzis (1m Group 3) and Whazzat (7f 2yo Listed); made a promising start when third at Bath (good) and she built on that when finishing well to land a Beverley novice (good to soft) ten days later; has a penalty but she looks a useful prospect and is open to more to progress on this step up in trip; key player.
runners.comment: Won at Beverley three weeks ago and she's open to more progress upped in trip; key player
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Civ & B R Millman

runners.name: Sahara Lake
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Inthar (1m2f-1m4f), Arabian Revolution (11.6f Flat/2m hurdle) and Emirates Skywards (1m5f), out of 1m4f Group 2 winner; has fair standard to aim at on debut and she could be a longer-term prospect on pedigree.
runners.comment: Has fair standard to aim at on debut and she could be a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Knock Three Times
runners.spotlight: 95,000gns yearling; tenth foal; half-sister to four winners including Khaadem (5f-6f including 2yo/dual Group 1; RPR 119), Log Out Island (5f/6f 2yo including Listed; 112) and Themainprotagonist (7f; 79); dam 5f 2yo/6f winner (70), half-sister to 6f Listed winner Lady Links; plenty to like on paper and interesting to see how she figures in market.
runners.comment: 95,000gns yearling; plenty to like on paper and she needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Crazy Cubana
runners.spotlight: April foal; 38,000euros foal, 120,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Lancillotto (French 1m4f) and Bibiche (French 2m1f hurdle); dam 1m6f winner (RPR 79), half-sister to US 1m1f/1m4f Grade 2 winner Grandeur, out of half-sister to Doncaster Cup winner Far Cry; has lots of stamina in pedigree but she needs checking in market on debut.
runners.comment: 120,000gns yearling; has lots of stamina in pedigree but she needs checking in market

runners.name: Havana Grey Star
runners.spotlight: February foal; 70,000gns yearling; first foal; dam French 8.5f winner (RPR 73), half-sister to winners Crooked Crown (5f 2yo), Flashy Snapper (1m), Blue Geranium (1m) and Kybosh (1m3f), out of maiden who was third in 1,000 Guineas; yard has had two 2yo winners this season and she needs a close look on debut.
runners.comment: 70,000gns yearling; has plenty of winners on dam's side and she's an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Sweet Devastation
runners.spotlight: April foal; 50,000euros yearling; half-sister to winners Asad Zabeel (Listed-placed 7f/1m1f including 2yo/UAE dirt; RPR 105), Alshimali (5f; 64) and Shibli (Australian/French 7.5f-10.7f inc AW); dam French 10.7f winner, half-sister to US 8.5f Grade 3 winner Shagaf; yard 12% with 2yos in recent years and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: 50,000euros yearling; yard 12% with 2yos in recent years and market should guide on debut
runners.owner: C Wright, D Kilburn & Mrs C Corbett

runners.name: Angel Sense
runners.spotlight: April foal; £38,000 yearling; ran green after a slow start on Kempton debut (6f, AW; 17-2) last week but she got the hang of things in the closing stages and did well to finish runner-up behind the favourite; that was a promising start and she should know more this time; respected.
runners.comment: Promising second on Kempton debut last week and she should improve for the experience

races.race_name: Best Odds Guaranteed At Betano Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lighting Thunder
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder colt who cost 650,000gns as a yearling; went close on Yarmouth debut (1m, good to soft) in September and  he finished runner-up again in a 25-runner maiden at Newbury (1m, good) on his return last month; recorded RPRs in the mid 80s in both those runs and he sets a clear standard here; strong contender.
runners.comment: Recorded RPRs in the mid 80s when a close second in both his runs; strong contender

runners.name: Romanticizing
runners.spotlight: 180,000gns yearling; closely related to 7f/1m winner Firmament (RPR 115) and half-brother to five winners; 11-1 on Kempton debut (1m, AW) last month but he ran well to finish a clear third behind a favourite who dominated; should benefit from that experience and he's a likely improver on his second start.
runners.comment: Has a striking pedigree and was a promising third on Kempton debut last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Qarreeb
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two useful winners at 1m; made a low-key start at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in November but he returned after a break with a front-running third here (1m2f, good) last month; that was a promising effort and he's respected on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Improved form when a front-running third here (1m) on his return; respected back in trip

runners.name: Flag Of Eva
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 9.4f-16.6f winner (RPR 78), sister to 1m4f-2m4f Flat and 2m hurdle winner Timoshenko, half-sister to 1m-9.4f winner Nastenka, out of 1m4f/2m winner; this looks a tough starting point and he could be one for longer trips later on.
runners.comment: This looks a tough starting point and he could be one for longer trips later on

runners.name: Forever Endless
runners.spotlight: 13,500gns foal, 85,000gns yearling, 4,200gns 2yo; brother to 7f-9.7f winner Leopold Bloom (including Italy; RPR 75), half-brother to winners Conservationist (1m 2yo; 92) and Custodian (6f 2yo; 81); dam 5f winner (including Listed; 98) from smart family; has good standard to aim at on debut and is probably best watched.
runners.comment: 4,200gns 2yo; has good standard to aim at on debut and is probably best watched

runners.name: Midsummer Storm
runners.spotlight: 550,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; shaped with promise when fourth on Newcastle debut (1m, AW) in February but he failed to build on that when a remote third there (7f) the following month; still early days but he needs plenty of progress on this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Well held in two runs at Newcastle this year and he needs plenty of progress

races.race_name: Bet 10 Get 30 At Betano Handicap

runners.name: Valkyrie Storm
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when scoring on handicap debut over C&D (good; 10-1) two weeks ago; a 3lb rise for that comfortable success looks fair and this well-bred filly is open to more progress; key player.
runners.comment: Won on handicap debut over C&D two weeks ago and she's a big player again off 3lb higher

runners.name: Sailor Batt
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden but he's been placed in three of his four runs this year including when a 4l third behind two major improvers at Ripon (1m, good to soft) last month; 1lb lower here and he should be in the mix again.
runners.comment: 0-10 but he's been placed in three of his four runs this year; should be in the mix again

runners.name: Quick Turn
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at the seventh attempt when justifying favouritism in a Wolverhampton handicap (8.6f, AW) in March; up 6lb but that was a clearcut success and he's strongly respected back on turf.
runners.comment: Made the breakthrough at Wolverhampton (8.6f); up 6lb but he's respected back on turf

runners.name: Glasgow Kiss
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in AW novice events this spring but she was tailed off in handicap debut at Bath (1m, good to soft; 10-1) three weeks ago; the slow ground may have been an issue but she was always in rear and has something to prove after that laboured effort.
runners.comment: Lightly raced filly but she was tailed off on handicap debut at Bath (1m) last month

runners.name: Gwen's Girl
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start when runner-up at Carlisle (6f, soft) in September but she's struggled in three subsequent runs including on handicap/seasonal debut at Ripon (1m, good to soft) last month; still unexposed but she weakened quickly last time and has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Has struggled in the last three of her four runs including a 1m handicap last month
runners.owner: E Holmes, M Graham And P Day

runners.name: Inferno
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when third in his final two qualifying runs and latest was after a gelding operation in a six-runner novice at Thirsk (1m, good) last month; needs to find more progress on handicap debut but it's still early days and he's a half-brother to a useful winner.
runners.comment: Showed promise in final two qualifying runs and he needs a close look on handicap debut

runners.name: Lillie Margot
runners.spotlight: Well held in three novice/maiden events including when 11-4 favourite at Leicester (1m) on her second start; now goes handicapping but she needs a transformation on her second run after a break; first run was on good ground and last two have been on good to soft.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly but she needs a transformation on handicap debut

runners.name: Empirical
runners.spotlight: His best effort from three runs during the winter was an 8l third of 12 at Wolverhampton (8.6f) on his second start; opening mark looks on the high side but he's been gelded and is a possible improver with headgear added on handicap/turf debut; needs watching in market.
runners.comment: Been gelded and he's possible improver with headgear added on handicap debut; watch market

runners.name: Bosom Pals
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in five 2yo runs but she won a 7f nursery at Thirsk (good to firm; 5-2) on her penultimate run last season; only 2lb higher here and needs checking in market at this new trip on her return.
runners.comment: Won 7f nursery (good to firm) last summer; needs checking in market at new trip on return

races.race_name: Boosted Odds Every Race At Betano Handicap

runners.name: Leadenhall
runners.spotlight: Now 4lb lower than for his win at Haydock (1m, good to firm) last summer and he wasn't beaten far on his reappearance at Pontefract (1m, good) ten days ago; should get a decent tow into this race and has claims if he can build on that last run.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark and he wasn't beaten far on reappearance at Pontefract; not ruled out

runners.name: Degale
runners.spotlight: Won a Ripon novice in final 2yo run and he started last season with a close call in a Haydock handicap (1m, good to firm); looks interesting on that form but he's had only three runs since and has been well held in all of them; still lightly raced but he needs to turn things around back on turf.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo but he needs to get back on track on this return to turf

runners.name: Orangesandlemons
runners.spotlight: Added to her Southwell novice win when landing a C&D handicap (good to soft) in October; well held in final run last season but she reappeared with a fair third at Wolverhampton last month; this is a deeper race but she's not ruled out back at this track.
runners.comment: C&D winner who reappeared with a fair third at Wolverhampton last month; possibilities

runners.name: Intrusively
runners.spotlight: Third in 2yo Group 2 in 2024; ran respectably off a falling mark on a few occasions last season but he's struggled in two 1m handicaps (good to soft/good to firm) this spring; has plenty to prove and overall record is now 1-14.
runners.comment: Sole win was as a 2yo and has struggled in both runs this spring; plenty to prove

runners.name: Winston's Warrior
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has won on AW in two of his last three runs including on Southwell reappearance (7f; first run since gelded) two weeks ago; up another 4lb but this return to 1m is no problem and he's shown clear promise on fast and slow ground on turf; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Has won on AW in two of his last three runs and he's a big player again back on turf

runners.name: Asteverdi
runners.spotlight: Triple winner for Alice Haynes including over C&D; went the wrong way in five runs for Hugo Palmer last season but she reappeared with a creditable third on stable debut at Musseburgh (1m, good) last month; that was off 2lb lower than for her last success and has claims if she can back that up.
runners.comment: Good third on stable debut at Musselburgh (1m) and has claims if he can back that up

runners.name: Principality
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and heavy ground; won off 1lb higher at Goodwood (1m) last July but has mixed record since and was beaten 11l in the Spring Mile on his reappearance in March; this is a big drop back in grade but he's now 1-11 in handicaps and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Acts on most ground but he's hard to predict and is 1-11 in handicaps; others preferred

runners.name: Port Erin
runners.spotlight: Won three 1m handicaps (AW/good) last season but he ended his 5yo campaign with heavy defeats over C&D and at Chelmsford; has bit to prove after six months off and he doesn't have a great record when fresh.
runners.comment: Has bit to prove after six months off and he doesn't have a great record when fresh

runners.name: Barry's Boy
runners.spotlight: Won at Windsor (1m, good) last spring and he doubled his tally when justifying favouritism at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March; that was a narrow success but he's only 3lb higher and is respected after another break.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in March and he's only 3lb higher after another break; respected

runners.name: Youarenotforgiven
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 8yo who has rattled off a hat-trick on AW this spring including a clearcut win at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month; this is tougher off 9lb higher but he remains feasibly treated on his old form and is a five-time winner on turf; key player.
runners.comment: Five-time turf winner who has rattled off a hat-trick on AW this spring; key player

runners.name: Aspull
runners.spotlight: Raced four times in his first season last year, producing a solid novice record (132) on turf prior to a lesser effort in AW handicap; gelded since; remains open to further progress back in this sphere and reappearance may be the time to catch him.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who won on 2yo debut last season; needs close look on his reappearance

runners.name: Helm Rock
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner split between turf and AW; his last success was at Kempton (1m) last month and he backed that up with a close third under a penalty at Lingfield (Polytrack); did well to hang in there from a wide draw last time and he's respected off same mark back on turf.
runners.comment: Ten-time winner who has been in good form on AW this spring; respected back on turf

runners.name: Tiger
runners.spotlight: 1m1f winner in France last March but he's not made an impact in four runs at up to 1m4f for Ian Williams; has dropped down the weights but he needs to raise his back at this trip after 162 days off.
runners.comment: On reduced mark but he needs to raise his game back at this trip after 162 days off

races.race_name: Spin The Betano Lucky Dip Handicap

runners.name: Komorkis
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f novice at Musselburgh (good to soft) for Richard Fahey last autumn; left that yard for 1,400gns but she made an encouraging start for new stable when a close fourth in C&D handicap (good) last month; still has potential after only six starts and she's in the mix with hood added.
runners.comment: Close fourth over C&D on recent stable debut and she's in the mix with hood added

runners.name: Westgate Warrior
runners.spotlight: Just one win from ten starts but that was a dominant display in a 14-runner handicap over C&D (good) in October; mixed results in three subsequent runs but he went close at Wolverhampton in December and may have needed his reappearance run at Southwell in March; still looks on a workable mark and it wouldn't be a surprise if he made a bold bid back at this track.
runners.comment: Sole win was over C&D and it wouldn't be surprise if he made a bold bid back at this track

runners.name: Papa Don't Preach
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who is 0-29 on turf but he ran well to finish third over C&D (good; 100-30 favourite) three weeks ago; rallied well in that race and he should be in the thick of things again off only 1lb higher.
runners.comment: 0-29 on turf but was favourite when a creditable third over C&D last time; shortlisted

runners.name: Herakles
runners.spotlight: Has reached the frame on AW in his last six starts including when a close second at Newcastle (5f; tongue-tie added) last month; record of 1-21 on turf but conditions should be fine back in this sphere and he had a close call off 1lb higher at Beverley last summer; interesting contender for yard that has won this race in the last two years.
runners.comment: In good form on AW this year and his mark hasn't moved much; respected back on turf

runners.name: Dc Cogent
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta) last spring and he's run four solid races on AW this year; now returns to turf but he's still lightly raced in this sphere and has possibilities off a sliding mark.
runners.comment: Four solid efforts on AW this year and he has possibilities back on turf
runners.owner: J R Dwyer And B Smart

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner who scored off 6lb higher at Catterick last May; has been up and down since then but he finished well to go close over C&D (good) on his penultimate run; has possibilities if he can recapture that form and it would be positive if rain arrives.
runners.comment: Nine-time winner who went close over C&D on his penultimate run; could be dangerous
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

runners.name: Mrs Trump
runners.spotlight: Five sprint wins but last three have been over 6f and latest was off 2lb lower last summer; has mixed record when fresh and market should guide back at this trip after 170 days off; stablemate of Herakles.
runners.comment: Her last three wins have been at 6f; one of two for yard and market should guide on return
runners.owner: R Chapman, G J Daly & R J Parks

runners.name: Donald
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in all three 2yo runs, including a close second in a three-runner contest at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) in September; should have more to offer this season and he's an interesting contender off what looks a fair mark on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed ability in all three 2yo runs and he needs a close look on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Marajito
runners.spotlight: Made all when winning a maiden handicap at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last June but she's been disappointing since and ended last season with a heavy defeat at this track; drops back in trip/grade on her return but record is now 1-12 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Just one win from 12 starts and she needs a major revival after 219 days off

races.race_name: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Mademoiselle Belle
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-18 but she got back near her best when a rallying second behind Tickettothestars here (6f, good) two weeks ago; should be unfazed by this drop back in trip and has claims off only 1lb higher than last time.
runners.comment: Just 1-18 but she went close behind Tickettothestars here two weeks ago; in the mix

runners.name: Tickettothestars
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 12th attempt when beating 14 rivals in a 6f handicap here (good; Mademoiselle Belle second) two weeks ago; up 4lb but that was a brave win under a positive ride and she's respected on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Off the mark with a brave win over 6f here (good) two weeks ago; respected back in trip

runners.name: Contorno
runners.spotlight: Won two 5f handicaps at Newcastle (AW) this year but he's not finished closer than fifth in six starts on turf; got badly hampered at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago but he was the outsider in that race others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but he's not finished closer than fifth in six turf runs; down the list

runners.name: Data Fata Secutus
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but she ran her best race so far when a close second in a Musselburgh handicap (5f, good) ten days ago; didn't get much luck in that race and she's an interesting contender off only 1lb higher here.
runners.comment: 0-7 but she didn't get much luck when close second at Musselburgh last time; interesting

runners.name: Dragon Spin
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but she has form form figures of 4223 in handicaps and that latest third was on her reappearance at Bath (5f, good) last month; 1lb lower here and she should be in mix again.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden but she has form figures of 4223 in handicaps; respected

runners.name: Go Lockers Go
runners.spotlight: Has improved since being gelded and he made it 2-3 in handicaps when completing a double at Yarmouth (5f, good) 11 days ago; up another 5lb but he beat a clear second with a bit in hand last time and he's open to more progress; big player.
runners.comment: Improver who made it 2-3 in handicaps when scoring at Yarmouth; big player again up 5lb
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing D Turner B Roberts

runners.name: O Fortuna
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a promising fourth on handicap debut at Catterick (5f, good) last month but he's finished out the back in both subsequent runs; a tongue-tie didn't work last time and he needs major revival with cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden who has finished out the back in last two handicaps; cheekpieces now tried

runners.name: Stromness
runners.spotlight: All four runs have been at on AW Newcastle this year; finished down the field in novice/maidens events and same story when 11th of 13 on handicap debut (6f) last month; still early days but he needs a transformation on this drop in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding but he needs a transformation on this drop to 5f

runners.name: Oasis Cover
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in three runs on Tapeta (5f/6f) late last year including a 2l fifth of 13 at Wolverhampton; returns after 147 days off but she needs watching in market on handicap/turf debut.
runners.comment: Showed some ability on AW late last year and needs watching in market on handicap debut
runners.owner: All About York Partnership And Partner

races.race_name: Safer Gambling With Betano Apprentice Handicap (Apprentice Training - Part Of RE Series) (GBBPlus)

runners.name: Gentle Warrior
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on Musselburgh reappearance (1m6f, soft) last month but that was a rare blip and he's feasibly treated on his best form last season including two wins over this trip (good); dangerous if he can bounce back.
runners.comment: Two wins at 1m6f last season and has claims if he can bounce back after a blip last month
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Dino Bellagio
runners.spotlight: Two-time bumper winner (soft/good) who made all in Flat handicaps on Tapeta (1m6f) and turf (2m, heavy) in the second half of last year; ended last season on a low note but he still has scope for further progress and needs watching in market on his return.
runners.comment: Two-time bumper winner who won two Flat handicaps last year; market should guide on return

runners.name: Red Derek
runners.spotlight: Only 2-54 and a long time has passed since that second success; well handicapped on some form over 1m6f last season but he was up and down last year and he doesn't have a great record when fresh; others preferred.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but last win was in 2022 and he doesn't have a great record when fresh

runners.name: Russian Rumour
runners.spotlight: 9yo who won this race (good) on her reappearance in 2024 and she ended last season with a 12l win at Ffos Las (2m, heavy; made all); 7lb higher on her return but she still looks on a workable mark and won't mind what the weather does; key player for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2024 and she ended last season with a 12l win at Ffos Las; key player

races.race_name: Harrison Family Remembering Loved Ones Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tap Tap Shamie
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in November (3m, yielding to soft; second has won since); runner-up on both starts over hurdles, on first occasion beaten a neck at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) by one who was second in a Punchestown handicap last week; beaten at 2-13 at Carlisle last time (2m4f, good to soft); the one to beat despite that reverse.
runners.comment: Point winner; has been 2nd on both hurdle starts, last time at 2-13; the one to beat again

runners.name: Rachel Lopez
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; modest form in points and was well beaten in a bumper; plenty to prove on stable/hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; modest form in three starts pointing and in a bumper; hurdles/stable debut

runners.name: Hashtag Queen
runners.spotlight: Third in a Kelso bumper on debut last May (2m, good) but hasn't shown much since, last twice over hurdles.
runners.comment: Third in a bumper on debut last May; hasn't shown much since, last twice over hurdles

runners.name: Colinski
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat performer, winner of a 11.5f handicap on good at Lingfield for James Owen last summer; fair sixth on AW over 2m on third start for current yard last time; quite interesting now hurdling.
runners.comment: Fair on the flat, winner of a 11.5f h'cap in 2025; quite interesting now going hurdling

runners.name: Palavar
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; maiden on the Flat and a well-beaten 40-1 shot at Perth (2m, good) on hurdles debut last month; improvement needed.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish Flat maiden who was well beaten on hurdles debut last month; improvement needed

runners.name: Mickthesoldier
runners.spotlight: Point winner; well-beaten 50-1 shot over C&D on stable/hurdle debut last week; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Point winner; well beaten over C&D on stable/hurdle debut last week; best watched for now.

races.race_name: Charlie's Halfway To 100 Handicap Chase

runners.name: Zero Tolerance
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden under rules; placed in six of his nine races over fences; fair fourth of five to a subsequent dual winner at Wetherby last time (1m7f, good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: 0-16; placed on 6 of his 9 starts over fences; decent fourth last time; each-way chance

runners.name: Natural Ability
runners.spotlight: 1-15 over fences; clear of the rest when beaten 1.75l second at Sedgefield last time (2m, good); chance on that form.
runners.comment: 1-15; good second at Sedgefield last time and has a chance if reproducing that form

runners.name: Conquer The Breeze
runners.spotlight: C&D win for Maurice Barnes last June; good second at Perth next time; hasn't fared quite as well since but has a good chance on debut for new yard.
runners.comment: C&D winner for Maurice Barnes last June; back after a break for new yard; good chance

runners.name: We Got Your Back
runners.spotlight: Has won three races over fences, the latest off this mark over C&D in October (good); has not been in top form last few starts; could bounce back.
runners.comment: Three wins over fences, latest over C&D; bit below par in 2026 but could bounce back

runners.name: Beat The Edge
runners.spotlight: Good third at Market Rasen two runs back (2m1f, good to soft); hard to win with, but an each-way chance; new combination of headgear tried.
runners.comment: Capable of going well (good third two runs back) but hard to win with; each-way chance

runners.name: Ghasham
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner who was bought out of Dan Skelton's yard for 4,400gns last month; had been in fair form over hurdles, but perhaps best watched on chase/stable debut.
runners.comment: Flat/hurdle winner; bought 4,400gns out of the Skelton yard last month; chase/stable debut

runners.name: Sao Carlos
runners.spotlight: Ex-Olly Murphy; well handicapped on his hurdles form, but hasn't shown much in three runs over fences; pulled up over hurdles on first run back after wind surgery last time; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Ex-Olly Murphy; well treated on his hurdles form; modest efforts chasing; hard to fancy
runners.owner: A Balmer And Bolton Mill Racing

runners.name: V Twelve
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/Flat winner; wasn't at his best on the Flat following a break at Catterick last month but should come on for the run; conditions fine; quite interesting on chase debut.
runners.comment: Hurdle/Flat winner; should come on for latest run after a break (Flat); chase debut

runners.name: Baptism Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Chase/hurdle winner for Henry de Bromhead; has pulled up on five of his six starts for present connections (well beaten over 3m on the other); best watched for now.
runners.comment: Chase/hurdle winner for Henry de Bromhead; not shown much for new yard; best watched

races.race_name: Paxtons Now Stocking Stihl Equipment Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jet Legs
runners.spotlight: Has won three times over fences, the latest over 2m4f here in March last year (good to soft; visored first time); hasn't done quite as well since; stays 3m and on a good mark now.
runners.comment: Three chase wins, latest over 2m4f here in March 2025; not run as well since; stays 3m.
runners.owner: Mrs Suzy Brown & Peter R Brown

runners.name: You Some Girl
runners.spotlight: Won a three-runner handicap at Newcastle in October (3m, good) but hasn't done quite as well since; back on her last winning mark.
runners.comment: Newcastle winner in October; hasn't done quite as well since; back on her winning mark

runners.name: Eaton Anne
runners.spotlight: Yet to win over fences, but creditable second at Wetherby in March (3m, good to soft; cheekpieces tried); has had wind surgery since fair fourth at Carlisle last month (3m, good; blinkers on); tongue-tie plus cheekpieces this time; chance.
runners.comment: Fair runs over fences on last two starts; had wind op since latest run; has a chance

runners.name: Court At Slip
runners.spotlight: Pulled up over 4m here last time (soft); plenty of decent placed efforts in 3m chases before that; has an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Pulled up over 4m here last time; several good placed efforts before that; each-way chance

runners.name: Betterforeveryone
runners.spotlight: Won over 4m here in March 2025 (good to soft); won again at Bangor in October (3m, good); pulled up at Bangor last time (3m5f, soft) but has since has 73 days off; chance.
runners.comment: 4m winner here in March 2025; Bangor win since; pulled up last time; could bounce back

runners.name: No Regrets
runners.spotlight: Veteran; has won on soft ground, but seems best on good ground; fair second over C&D in October (good) but has been beaten more than 30l on his two subsequent starts.
runners.comment: Veteran who is suited by good ground; 2nd over C&D in October, but well beaten since

runners.name: Hostile Hotelier
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner, including over 2m on heavy ground here; decent second over fences at Sedgefield in October (3m2f, good) but last two runs have been over hurdles (took a heavy fall when running well last time); a possible.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner here; good 2nd chasing in October; hurdling since (fell heavily latest)

runners.name: Gold Clermont
runners.spotlight: Ex-Olly Murphy; off the mark for present yard when winning five-runner handicap chase by a short head at Fontwell last time (3m2f, good); up 3lb; interesting.
runners.comment: Ex-Olly Murphy; Fontwell winner for this yard last time; up 3lb; interesting contender

races.race_name: Paxtons Heart Of All England Maiden Hunters' Chase (For The Heart Of All England Trophy)

runners.name: Peaceinthevalley
runners.spotlight: Ex-Henry de Bromhead; fair novice hurdler, but pulled up on only start over fences; fair third in a maiden point in April on stable debut; tongue-tie on; has a much stiffer task here.
runners.comment: Fair novice hurdler for Henry de Bromhead; ordinary point run on stable debut; stiff task

runners.name: Eagles Rock
runners.spotlight: Five-time point winner; runner-up in two hunter chases, both at Kelso, latest on Wednesday when beaten 7l (2m7f, good; 5-2); makes a quick return, but could go well.
runners.comment: Five-time point winner; 2nd twice under rules, latest on Wednesday; quick return; chance

runners.name: Line Em Up
runners.spotlight: Won four of his five starts pointing last season (going well when unseated three out in the other), the latest at Charm Park in April (3m, good); rules debut; contender.
runners.comment: Won four of his five starts pointing last term; contender on his rules debut

runners.name: Whatudoing
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden point in February last year (3m, soft) and then showed fair form in a couple of hunter chases; creditable third in a restricted point last month (3m, good to soft); improvement needed.
runners.comment: Maiden point winner in 2025; also fair form in two hunter chases; improvement needed

runners.name: Second Subaltern
runners.spotlight: Exposed; plenty of fair form in points, winning on three occasions, the latest at Charm Park last month (3m, good); didn't show a great deal on two previous attempts chasing, but has an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Exposed; three points wins, last on good ground last month; each-way chance

runners.name: Bentley Road
runners.spotlight: Maiden; has been placed on a few occasions pointing (also run out twice) but hasn't shown much in his two runs in hunter chases; not an obvious candidate.
runners.comment: Maiden; placed in three points but not shown much in two runs hunter chasing; best watched

runners.name: Sunset On Fire
runners.spotlight: Has won three of his ten starts pointing, the latest by 14l in March; suited by good/faster ground, tongue-tie tried and an each-way chance.
runners.comment: 3-10 in points, the latest in March; goes well on good/faster ground; each-way chance

runners.name: Imperial Imp
runners.spotlight: Has won two points on good ground, the latest in April; has a much stiffer task on his chasing debut.
runners.comment: Dual point winner on good ground but has a much stiffer task on his chase debut

runners.name: Eastern Storm
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a maiden hurdle on this card 12 months ago; has since won all four of his point-to-points, showing useful form last time when winning at Tranwell; has since been aimed at this race; bold bid expected.
runners.comment: 4-4 in points since last rules run; useful form; this race his target for chase debut.

runners.name: Pulse Of Camelot
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; third in a maiden hunter chase in January 2024 (2m5f, heavy); didn't show much in two chases for new yard in the winter, nor in two points in April; others stronger.
runners.comment: Third in a maiden hunter chase in Ireland; not shown much for present connections

runners.name: Economic Editor
runners.spotlight: 1-22 in points; has run a couple of fair races recently; has a much stiffer task here.
runners.comment: 1-22 in points; has run a couple of fair races recently; has a much stiffer task here.

races.race_name: Paxtons Number 1 For Case IH Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Gardener
runners.spotlight: Bidding for a C&D hat-trick having won here on last two starts (soft/good); has gone up 9lb but has run well off much higher marks in the past; could go well.
runners.comment: Bids for a hat-trick after two C&D wins on good/soft; up 9lb but could go well again
runners.owner: Mrs J M Plummer & M E Sowersby

runners.name: The Four Sixes
runners.spotlight: But disappointing in four runs over hurdles since a spell chasing; has dropped to a good mark as a result and better run last time; down in grade; could go well.
runners.comment: Below-par over hurdles since a spell chasing; on a good mark; better run latest; chance

runners.name: Johnson's Blue
runners.spotlight: Has won eight of his 21 races over hurdles; doesn't seem to be the force of old as was pulled up at Haydock last time having finished last of eight on previous hurdle run.
runners.comment: 8-21 over hurdles; not as good as he was and needs to improve on his last two hurdle runs

runners.name: Florida Dreams
runners.spotlight: Ex-Nicky Richards; smart bumper performer; has won two of his 17 races over hurdles, but disappointing on last two starts; makes debut for Sandy Thomson.
runners.comment: Ex-Nicky Richards; smart in bumpers; 2-17 hurdling (disappointing last twice); yard debut

runners.name: Lawrenny
runners.spotlight: Useful novice two seasons ago, last win over 2m here in March 2025; below his best on both starts last season; cheekpieces now tried; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Useful novice two seasons ago but below par last term; headgear now tried; bit to prove

runners.name: Saracen Beau
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; second hurdles win at Doncaster in March (second run at 2m5f, good to soft); stiffer task on this 7lb higher mark but a chance.
runners.comment: Lightly-raced; winner over 2m5f at Doncaster in March; stiffer task up 7lb; chance

runners.name: Imperial Data
runners.spotlight: Six hurdle wins, latest over 3m1f at Cartmel last August (good); high enough in the ratings at present, lacks a recent run and perhaps best watched this time.
runners.comment: Six hurdle wins, latest at Cartmel last August; lacks a recent run; perhaps best watched

runners.name: Perseus Way
runners.spotlight: Won a Hamilton handicap for Stuart Coltherd in September (1m5f, good to soft); subsequently changed yards and promising third over 2m here on stable debut after a break last week (good); should improve on that but yet to prove he is as effective at this trip.
runners.comment: Good 3rd after a break over 2m here last week; should improve on that; longer trip a worry
runners.owner: J Monkhouse R De Robeck A Miller

runners.name: Sunset Hill
runners.spotlight: Won a mares' novice at Kelso in December 2024 (2m5f, soft); back over hurdles after seven runs over fences, best form when third over C&D in November (good to soft); bit disappointing since.
runners.comment: Kelso winner in 2024; back over hurdles after seven runs over fences; bit to prove

runners.name: Shallow River
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m handicap hurdle on soft ground at Carlisle in December 2024 but hasn't been out since; looks his stable's second string.
runners.comment: Won 3m h'cap hurdle at Carlisle in December 2024; not run since; looks stable's 2nd string

runners.name: Monaco Rules
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles in France (2m3f, heavy); some fair form since joining Micky Hammond and was a close second at Sedgefield in November (2m4f, soft) but hasn't run since.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner in France; close second at Sedgefield in November but hasn't run since

runners.name: Dare To Shout
runners.spotlight: Pulled up over fences at Kelso when last seen out having won a Carlisle handicap chase (3m, good) in October; runs over hurdles for the first time for more than two years (C&D winner in October 2023); second run after wind op.
runners.comment: Having first hurdles run since 2024 (won over C&D in 2023); second run after wind op

races.race_name: Jenni's Justatenner 50th Celebrations Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Alvesta
runners.spotlight: 30-race maiden Flat and jumps; bought for 3,400gns in February; well beaten on stable debut at Kelso last month (2m2f, good); should improve for that run; on a good mark at present.
runners.comment: 0-30 Flat and jumps; 3,400gns buy; beaten 34l on stable debut; should improve; good mark

runners.name: Linden Lane
runners.spotlight: 0-11 over hurdles; third in handicaps at Windsor in January (2m, good) and Sedgefield in February (2m1f, good to soft); can be forgiven lesser effort on testing ground last time; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Creditable 3rd in two handicaps in the winter; lesser run on heavy latest; each-way chance

runners.name: Malangen
runners.spotlight: Has won ten of his 82 races, including over this C&D in June 2024 (good); should be all the better for last month's reappearance run at Perth; interesting.
runners.comment: 10-82, including C&D win in June 2024; should be all the better for last run; interesting
runners.owner: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Mr Globalist
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in 37 runs Flat and jumps; beaten a long way in a non-handicap C&D selling hurdle last week; has more of a chance here, but plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 37-race maiden Flat and jumps; well beaten in conditions seller last week; plenty to prove

runners.name: Gemini Man
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 6lb lower last August (good); some sound runs since, both over hurdles and on the Flat (9.4f Wolverhampton winner in February); chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner last August; some good runs over hurdles and on the Flat since; chance

runners.name: Clean Getaway
runners.spotlight: Seems best at Sedgefield and won over fences there last month (2m, good); well behind Scots Poet in a 2m1f handicap hurdle there last time; bit to find.
runners.comment: Sedgefield chase winner in April, but well behind Scots Poet in h'cap hurdle there latest

runners.name: Walk On Son
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in bumpers; has improved since going hurdling, best run when third in a Carlisle novice in October (2m1f, good); bit disappointing this year, including on handicap debut on first run after a second wind op last time (2m4f, good); bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Third in a 2m1f novice in October; below-par this year, including on h'cap debut (2m4f)

runners.name: Scots Poet
runners.spotlight: Mainly in good form this year, gaining a well-deserved win at Sedgefield last time (2m1f, good; blinkers on for fourth time); up 4lb; could go well again.
runners.comment: Off the mark for 2026 at Sedgefield last time; could go well off this 4lb higher mark

runners.name: Kellie's Dream
runners.spotlight: Two wins in Ireland, both with give in the ground; has become well handicapped after being dropped 10lb for three poor runs last season; interesting back after wind surgery.
runners.comment: Dual winner on softish going; down 10lb for three poor runs last term; back after wind op

runners.name: King Sacre
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; showed improved form on handicap debut when second at Sedgefield last time (2m1f, good); further progress likely; a possible.
runners.comment: Unexposed; improved form when second on h'cap debut at Sedgefield last time; a possible

runners.name: Ms Cindy Kate
runners.spotlight: Unexposed mare who shaped with promise when a running-on fourth in a Carlisle novice last time (2m1f, good to soft); improvement likely now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Unexposed; fair 4th in Carlisle novice last time and improvement expected now handicapping

races.race_name: Jenni's Justatenner 50th Celebrations Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Two Auld Pals
runners.spotlight: Not really firing on all cylinders when last seen in the winter but returns from 12-week break on a good mark and is versatile regarding ground; gained his only hurdle win over C&D; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: C&D winner who returns from 12-week break on a good mark; no surprise if he features

runners.name: My My My Delilah
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but ran well and suggested her turn might be near when close fourth behind Snapaudaciaheros over C&D (good) last month, and won't mind if the ground is slower today; could be the answer.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles but ran well and suggested her turn might be near over C&D last month

runners.name: Snapaudaciaheros
runners.spotlight: Seemed to benefit from combination of drop back in trip and first-time cheekpieces when finishing well to snatch victory over C&D (good to soft) last month; her overall profile remains unconvincing but she's effectively only 1lb higher today and features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Finished well to snatch C&D win last month; effectively only 1lb higher today

runners.name: National Question
runners.spotlight: Three wins last season, the latest at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) in March; managed only sixth off today's mark last month (2m4f) but wasn't beaten far and may yet have more improvement to come; respected.
runners.comment: Won three times last season and may yet have more improvement to come

runners.name: Breadalbane Lass
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over hurdles at 12th attempt, with clearcut success at Perth (2m, good) last month; 8lb rise demands more from her here but something may have clicked.
runners.comment: Clear winner at Perth last month, her 12th hurdle run; 8lb higher here; still considered

runners.name: Hourless
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two respectable efforts and arguably ran better than finishing position suggests when eighth at Carlisle (2m1f good to soft) in March but is yet to add to his C&D juvenile hurdle win in June 2023; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran better than finishing position suggests last time but remains on long losing run

runners.name: Mornington Beach
runners.spotlight: Displayed huge improvement when 80-1 second in C&D (good) novice in October, her third start over hurdles; raced too freely and was pulled up after weakening quickly in handicap here (2m4f, good to soft) the following month but significant positives can sill be taken from that run; drops back in trip today, having had wind surgery; a possible if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Pulled up on handicap debut (2m4f) but positives can still be taken from that run

runners.name: Justlikefire
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who rebounded from disappointing handicap debut when clear second to progressive rival at Uttoxeter (2m, soft) in November and was also running well before penultimate-flight fall at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in February; one to consider.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 7yo; best run when clear second to progressive rival in November

runners.name: The Best Way
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 8yo who returned from a layoff to open account at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in February 2025; not seen since but may still have potential and can evidently go well when fresh.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 8yo; won after layoff in February 2025; not seen since

runners.name: Mythical
runners.spotlight: Smart on the Flat in his prime but struggled off lowly mark on stable/handicap hurdle debut in March (3m1f); may benefit from this steep drop back in trip but still has too much to prove.
runners.comment: Struggled on stable/handicap hurdle debut; drops back from 3m1f today

races.race_name: British EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Thunda Struck
runners.spotlight: 10-1 and green, shaped well when a debut second of seven in novice at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago, nearest finish; this daughter of Bungle Inthejungle is a likely player with more to come.
runners.comment: Shaped well when a debut second at Ripon; a likely player with more to come

runners.name: Quantum Swift
runners.spotlight: Fair form shown when runner-up in 5f Wolverhampton maiden and fifth in 6f Nottingham novice this spring; she may do better still so not out of things.
runners.comment: Fair form shown on her first two runs; she may do better still so not out of things
runners.owner: C Munro, Mrs M Gander And Partner

runners.name: Alpaca's Bullet
runners.spotlight: 6,000 guineas yearling; Soldier's Call filly; half-sister to winners My Sea Cottage (US 7.5f-8.5f turf) and Holy Shambles (Qatari 6f); dam, 7f winner (RPR 84), is a sister to 7f/1m1f Group 3 winner Decado; the market can guide on her debut.
runners.comment: Soldier's Call filly; the market can guide on her debut

runners.name: Pertinent
runners.spotlight: 3,000 guineas yearling; Kameko filly; dam unraced half-sister to winners Kilmah (7f Group 3) and Tears Of The Sun (useful 1m-1m4f); the betting can prove an accurate indicator.
runners.comment: Kameko filly; the betting can prove an accurate indicator

runners.name: My Normandie
runners.spotlight: 15,500 guineas foal, 20,000 euros yearling; sister to 7f winner John Chard VC (RPR 85) and a half-sister to four other winners, notably Love Interest (7f; RPR 97); dam is a once-raced half-sister to two Group 1 winners; this Ardad filly can't be discounted for her in-form yard.
runners.comment: This Ardad filly can't be discounted on her first run for her in-form yard
runners.owner: Gerard A Normand, Anna & Moa Sundstrom

runners.name: Banter Inn Court
runners.spotlight: 12,000 guineas yearling; Inns Of Court colt; dam, Listed-placed 5f winner (RPR 91), is a sister to 1m winner Prima Diva; possibilities on his debut, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Inns Of Court colt; possibilities on his debut, especially if market vibes are positive

runners.name: Baldetti
runners.spotlight: 25,000 guineas yearling; half-brother to three winners, notably Alashar (French Listed 7f; RPR 101); dam, 6f-1m winner (Group 3; RPR 112), is out of a 1m Listed winner/1,000 Guineas third; this Minzaal colt needs considering.
runners.comment: A half-brother to three winners so this Minzaal colt needs considering
runners.owner: G H Thompson And Michael Dods

runners.name: Sea Mirage
runners.spotlight: Sent off 7-2 but very green when a never-dangerous sixth of eight in novice at Pontefract (5f, good) 18 days ago, running on late; this son of Naval Crown looks the sort to do a fair bit better now; not without interest.
runners.comment: Very green when debut Pontefract sixth; sort to do fair bit better; not without interest

runners.name: Sky Secret
runners.spotlight: 15-8, encouraging debut fourth of 12 in C&D maiden (good to firm) two weeks ago, leading long way; this son of A'ali seems sure to improve.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut C&D fourth; this son of A'ali seems sure to improve

runners.name: Postcard
runners.spotlight: Well held in 5f Bath novice and maiden over C&D last month; she needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Well held in 5f Bath novice and maiden over C&D last month

races.race_name: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Masked Warrior
runners.spotlight: Displayed plenty of promise when placed in 7f Newbury novice and 1m Nottingham maiden last autumn, travelling strongly long way when runner-up on latter occasion; gelded since; holds leading claims with better to come.
runners.comment: Plenty of promise when placed at Newbury and Nottingham at 2yrs; he holds leading claims

runners.name: King's Cavalry
runners.spotlight: Kingman colt who has shaped well on both his runs, runner-up in C&D novice last month; can do better still so he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Shaped well on both runs, runner-up in C&D novice last month; one to consider

runners.name: Galba
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when third in a 6f Newbury maiden on debut in October but he beat only one home on his return in a 7f Newmarket maiden last month; worth another chance.
runners.comment: Beat just one home on return in 7f Newmarket maiden; worth another chance

runners.name: Distant Moon
runners.spotlight: A half-brother to AW winners Florida Dawn (7f; RPR 65) and Venus Slipper (1m2f; RPR 60); dam, Listed-placed 7f/1m winner (RPR 100), is a half-sister to 1m Listed winner, out of sister to 10.5f Group 1 winner Johann Quatz; this Showcasing colt must enter calculations on his debut, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Showcasing colt must enter calculations on debut, especially if market vibes are positive

runners.name: Ashley Pool
runners.spotlight: 66-1, last of ten in maiden at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) on her debut two weeks ago; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Last of ten in maiden at Doncaster on her debut; significantly more is required

runners.name: Blazing Sam
runners.spotlight: 125-1, tenth of 13 in novice here (6f, good) on his debut 26 days ago; lots more is required.
runners.comment: Tenth in novice here on his debut; lots more is required

runners.name: Able Astra
runners.spotlight: 25-1, never-dangerous eighth of 11 in 7f Kempton novice on her debut in December; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Eighth of 11 in 7f Kempton novice on her debut; needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: Bridget's Baby
runners.spotlight: Well held in a pair of 6f maidens at Haydock and here last July; has plenty to prove after nine months off.
runners.comment: Well held in a pair of 6f maidens at Haydock and here last July

races.race_name: Smooth Radio Ladies' Day 4th July Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Invincible Speed
runners.spotlight: All four wins on AW; 0-7 on turf but his two best runs on grass have come over C&D, off 9lb higher last April and when close up back here last month; should be a goer with Jack Callan claiming a handy 3lb.
runners.comment: Four AW wins; 0-7 on turf but has twice run well over C&D; J Callan claims a handy 3lb

runners.name: Emperor Caradoc
runners.spotlight: Has won on a range of surfaces but two 6f wins in 2025 came on good to firm and he was racing on that surface for the first time since when winning at Thirsk last week; back up 3lb and has another good run in him.
runners.comment: Suited by good to firm; successful at Thirsk last week; back on career-high mark

runners.name: This Farh
runners.spotlight: Can lead; has been racing at about 7f for well over a year and he's run well in both attempts at that trip here, most recently when sticking his neck out for the win (good) last month; back up 3lb but on a fair mark still and used to have the speed for 6f; of interest.
runners.comment: Has 6f form but kept to 7f now; game winner here latest; fair mark still; of interest

runners.name: Mumayaz
runners.spotlight: Tough sort who last won on turf more than a year ago but he had a luckless run at Bath recently (extended 5f, good; no aids, as today); acts on this track and versatile regarding the ground; can't rule out.
runners.comment: Last won on turf more than a year ago but luckless at Bath latest and can't rule out

runners.name: Fiscal Policy
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins on AW and turf; has done well for this yard and his 6f turf form in 2025 certainly bears scrutiny; respectable efforts back on grass of late and while not at full strength in either, he'll be suited by this stiffer test at 6f providing there's a good pace; form chance.
runners.comment: Below level shown on turf in 2025 but still running well; in the thick of it again
runners.owner: S Thompson, Mrs R Carr & G Shields

runners.name: Dark Side Thunder
runners.spotlight: Eight AW wins but just one on turf, at Yarmouth (6f, good to soft) last May; no match for well-treated reviver there 18 days ago but he saw the race out nicely to take a late second; can play a part.
runners.comment: Eight AW wins, just one on turf; creditable 2nd to reviver back on grass latest; play part

races.race_name: Best Ticket Deals Online At leicester-racecourse.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Sword Of Wessex
runners.spotlight: Finally got off the mark (headgear change) in four-runner Yarmouth handicap (11.5f) 11 days ago; up 4lb but could have more to offer for his current yard.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Yarmouth latest; up 4lb but could have more to offer for current yard

runners.name: Iwantmytimewithyou
runners.spotlight: A dual 1m4f/1m6f winner last summer and back in decent form of late, fifth in 1m4f Beverley handicap 16 days ago; possibilities.
runners.comment: Back in decent form of late, fifth in 1m4f Beverley handicap 16 days ago; possibilities

runners.name: Trojan Truth
runners.spotlight: Bagged a hat-trick (including over C&D) last summer and he signed off for 2025 with a solid third in 1m6f Wolverhampton handicap in October; has won off a break too.
runners.comment: C&D winner; ended 2025 with solid Wolverhampton third in October; has won off a break too

runners.name: Kurios Lady
runners.spotlight: Reliable sort who comes here on the back of a good second of five in 1m2f handicap at Nottingham 13 days ago; this longer trip promises to suit so she's a player in her bid for a breakthrough success.
runners.comment: Good second of five at Nottingham latest; player with this longer trip promising to suit

runners.name: John Galt
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who posted his best effort when second of five in 1m6f handicap at Southwell 16 days ago; can't be ruled out on his turf debut nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Best effort when second at Southwell 16 days ago; can't be ruled out on his turf debut

runners.name: Johnny Boom
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in October and he got back on track when second of 12 in 1m4f Pontefract handicap last month; must enter calculations nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Back on track with Pontefract second last month; this C&D winner must enter calculations

races.race_name: Heather Small Plays Leicester Races 4th July Handicap

runners.name: Albeseeingyer
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but she got back on track when second of seven over C&D last month; this C&D winner can't be discounted nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: On losing run but back on song with C&D second latest; this C&D winner can't be discounted

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: Gained a deserved victory in 7f Musselburgh handicap ten days ago; up 2lb but this consistent front-runner ought to be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Deserved win at Musselburgh ten days ago; up 2lb but ought to be in the shake-up again

runners.name: Call Glory
runners.spotlight: A dual 6f/7f AW winner in January and he's held his form well since, beaten a head in 6f handicap here two weeks ago; he can go well again nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Holding his form well, beaten a head in 6f handicap here latest; can go well again up 1lb

runners.name: Fenlander
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden but not disgraced when third of six in 6f Yarmouth handicap 18 days ago; possibilities.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden but not disgraced when third of six in 6f Yarmouth handicap 18 days ago

runners.name: Zubaru
runners.spotlight: Confirmed earlier promise when readily getting off the mark in 7f Doncaster handicap a week ago; hiked up 9lb but remains low mileage and merits serious consideration for his in-form stable.
runners.comment: Readily off the mark at Doncaster week ago; up 9lb but still merits serious consideration

runners.name: Al Hofzan
runners.spotlight: Is 0-9 but he comes here in decent nick, fifth of nine in 6f handicap here 26 days ago; in the mix back up to 7f now.
runners.comment: Is 0-9 but he comes here in decent nick; in the mix back up to 7f now

runners.name: Enpassant
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways in 7f Southwell handicap in April but he beat only two home back there eight days later; this C&D scorer needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell but beat only 2 home there turned out 8 days later; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Cypriot Diaspora
runners.spotlight: A dual 7f winner last August and she made an encouraging return when fifth in 7f Kempton handicap in March; this course winner needs considering.
runners.comment: Encouraging return when fifth at Kempton in March; this course winner needs considering

runners.name: Tex
runners.spotlight: Proved reliable without winning in 2025, signing off with a good third of nine in 1m Nottingham handicap in August; remains low mileage so not out of things on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Proved reliable without winning in 2025; not out of things on his seasonal return

races.race_name: Go West Plays Leicester Races 4th July Handicap

runners.name: Versatile
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m2f winner for Michael Attwater; has shaped with promise on all three runs for his new yard, set a lot to do when third over hurdles at Plumpton 20 days ago; a slow starter but he still rates a player off a reduced mark here.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise for new yard, third at Plumpton latest; slow starter but he's a player

runners.name: Myna
runners.spotlight: Finally got off the mark in 1m2f classified event at Lingfield in January; has continued in good form and challenged wide when sixth in another classified event there in March; in the mix again.
runners.comment: In good form, challenged wide when sixth at Lingfield latest; in the mix again

runners.name: Clipsham Noble
runners.spotlight: Scored over 1m at Brighton last June for Alice Haynes; not disgraced all three runs for his current yard, very slowly away when fifth in 1m Bath handicap last time; no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Not disgraced all three runs for current yard; possibilities

runners.name: Pure Theory
runners.spotlight: Is 0-17 and she beat just one home in a 1m4f classified event at Lingfield last time; usual cheekpieces go back on (also tongue tied) with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Is 0-17 and she beat just one home in 1m4f classified event at Lingfield last time

runners.name: Mooretown Lad
runners.spotlight: Fair ex-Irish 1m1f winner; arrives in good nick for his current yard, fourth of ten in 1m Nottingham handicap three weeks ago; not dismissed from an easing mark.
runners.comment: In good nick for current yard, fourth at Nottingham latest; not dismissed from easing mark

runners.name: Oasis Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing run in emphatic style at Bath (1m) ten days ago; hiked up 9lb but not taken lightly on her first go over 1m2f.
runners.comment: Ended a losing run in emphatic style at Bath; up 9lb but not taken lightly

runners.name: Fillyfudge
runners.spotlight: Scored over hurdles at Stratford last May but below par on the AW over the winter, beating just one home in 1m2f handicap at Chelmsford in February; more is required after a break in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: Below par on the AW over the winter; more required after a break in a change of headgear

runners.name: Gert Lush
runners.spotlight: Without a victory since 2023; off 21 months before a never-dangerous seventh of ten in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap last month; she needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Off 21 months before seventh at Wolverhampton last month; needs to take major step forward
runners.owner: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward

runners.name: Eager Puccini
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much in his seven runs to date, sixth of nine in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap 30 days ago; not easy to warm to.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much in his seven runs to date; not easy to warm to

runners.name: City Escape
runners.spotlight: Consistent mare who signed off for Mark Loughnane with a solid third in 9.4f Wolverhampton handicap in October; has gone well fresh so not out of things for her new yard.
runners.comment: Consistent for Mark Loughnane in 2025; gone well fresh so not out of things for new yard

runners.name: Luminous Approach
runners.spotlight: Son of Teofilo who cut little ice in a trio of runs in 2025, seventh in 1m Lingfield maiden in September; lots more is needed on his return/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Cut little ice in a trio of runs in 2025; lots more is needed on his return/handicap debut
runners.owner: D Gilbert, A Bruce, G Wills

races.race_name: 'Picnic At The Races' Monday 25th May Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Sharma d'Amour
runners.spotlight: Back from five months off in good form, set a lot to do and nearest at the finish when second of 14 in 1m Bath handicap last month; she can go well again.
runners.comment: Set lot to do when good second in 1m Bath handicap last month; she can go well again

runners.name: Tonal
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who enjoyed a fine winter on the AW and he posted another good effort when fifth of 13 off a career-high mark at Southwell (1m) last month; must enter calculations back on turf.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a fine winter on AW and good Southwell fifth last month; must enter calculations

runners.name: Yokkell
runners.spotlight: On a losing sequence but she really caught the eye when a running-on third of 14 in 1m2f handicap here two weeks ago; up 2lb but very much one to consider.
runners.comment: On losing run but really caught eye when third here two weeks ago; very much considered

runners.name: Union Island
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways at Lingfield (1m) in February but his more recent form is not so encouraging, tenth in 1m Doncaster handicap two weeks ago; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Back to winning ways at Lingfield in February but more recent form is not so encouraging

runners.name: Star Start
runners.spotlight: A dual 1m2f scorer in 2025; back to form when third of nine in 1m Pontefract handicap ten days ago so ought to be thereabouts eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Back on song with Pontefract third ten days ago so ought to be thereabouts eased 1lb

runners.name: Mercurius Power
runners.spotlight: It's now 17 outings since his last success but he arrives on the back of a respectable sixth of 11 in 1m Newcastle handicap last month; easing in the weights and no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: It's now 17 outings since last success; easing in the weights though and no forlorn hope

runners.name: Super Hit
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence (for Darryll Holland) but he made a promising start for James Owen after a three-month layoff when second of 12 in 1m AW Lingfield handicap nine days ago; big shout nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Promising start for James Owen when second at Lingfield nine days ago; big shout up 1lb

runners.name: Up The Jazz
runners.spotlight: Without a win since 2024 but he got right back on track when second of ten in 1m Ripon handicap two weeks ago; up 2lb but well in the mix.
runners.comment: Without win since 2024 but back on track with Ripon second two weeks ago; well in the mix

runners.name: The Sweet Escape
runners.spotlight: Finally opened his account at Nottingham (1m) in April but failed to back it up when sixth at Ripon (1m) two weeks ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won at Nottingham but failed to back it up when sixth at Ripon since; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Study Up
runners.spotlight: Winless since his debut but he comes here in decent nick, third of nine in 1m Bath handicap 22 days ago; not out of things from a slipping mark.
runners.comment: In decent nick, third in Bath handicap 22 days ago; not out of things from a slipping mark

races.race_name: SVE Services Ltd Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Carpe Diem
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when well clear at Huntingdon (2m4f, soft) in February but paid the handicapping price after; it's been harder since but he was unfortunate to run up against one who relished the new 3m3f test at Stratford (good) in April, although he still ran well; major player in first 3m run.
runners.comment: Wide-margin 2m4f winner in February; up weights; creditable 3m3f run latest; good claims

runners.name: Bahtiyar
runners.spotlight: 0-30 over hurdles and fences but in the frame on last five starts, two of them here (3m1f/2m4f); deserves to find a race and he should be involved again providing this doesn't come too soon after his run on Tuesday.
runners.comment: 0-30 over hurdles and fences but in the frame on last 5 starts, two of them here

runners.name: School For Scandal
runners.spotlight: Two more chase wins in 2025, the latest at Windsor (3m, good to soft; suited by good) in November; often battled with unsuitable ground over the winter but 4lb lower than at Windsor so has a weights chance.
runners.comment: Mixed over the winter but 4lb lower than November win (3m) so has a weights chance
runners.owner: K Johns & Joe Tickle Racing Club

runners.name: Redbridge Rambler
runners.spotlight: Two good runs behind subsequent winners at Hereford and Catterick (both 3m1f, good) in late 2025; always struggling over an inadequate 1m7f at Wincanton (no headgear; cheekpieces return) in April and perhaps fortunate to get dropped 2lb; fair chance of another win.
runners.comment: Two good runs over 3m1f in late 2025; inadequate trip latest; solid chance

runners.name: Sweet Nightingale
runners.spotlight: Went chasing late in her career but she's making a good fist of it and was going best from some way out when winning at Exeter (3m, good) in March; no blame attached for defeat next time and she didn't appreciate the 3m2f trip at Fontwell recently; a sharp 3m should suit; close up.
runners.comment: Won 3m chase at Exeter in March; had excuses since; this sharp 3m can suit; close up

runners.name: Chief Black Robe
runners.spotlight: Difficult times since back from absence last June but he's dropped a long way in the weights and there have been some signs of life; non-stayer over the extended 3m3f at Stratford recently (Carpe Diem runner-up) but he didn't run badly and better is expected.
runners.comment: Difficult times since back from absence; dropped right down weights; not disgraced latest

runners.name: Black Occ
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in two of three chase starts, the latest at Bangor after some guessy jumps; had run well when runner-up at Hereford (2m5f, good) in March, however, and should do better at some stage; tongue tied this time.
runners.comment: Pulled up in 2 of 3 chase starts but creditable second in March (2m5f); should do better
runners.owner: P Conway, T Lewis & G Wilson

runners.name: Stonegate
runners.spotlight: Modest form over jumps so far and has been pulled up on last three chase starts; can only watch from 9lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Modest form over jumps and pulled up on last three chase starts; can only watch

races.race_name: Mike Mohan's 60th Birthday 'National Hunt' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: So Proud
runners.spotlight: Attracted plenty of support when winning a Haydock bumper (good) one year ago; subsequently changed hands for £100,000 but weakened on stable/hurdling debut at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in January; disappointing if he's not capable of better.
runners.comment: £100,000 buy after bumper win; weakened on January hurdles debut; should do better

runners.name: Kap De Cerisy
runners.spotlight: Two starts in six-runner Irish points; 6-4 when close second in April 2024 then scored by 11l at 1-2 eleven months later; sold for £75,000 four days on and has joined leading yard; hooded and tongue tied for this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: £75,000 buy after Irish point win in March 2025; hood/tongue-tie on debut for top yard
runners.owner: Mrs Kym Glennon & Mrs Edelle Logan

runners.name: Wings Of Ayrton
runners.spotlight: Beaten 1l when second of 12 in Irish point (good to yielding) in January; bought for £50,000 the following month; pedigree suggests staying chases will be his game but still of interest on hurdles debut for respected yard.
runners.comment: £50,000 buy after second in January Irish point; has joined respected yard

runners.name: Taranis Dubh
runners.spotlight: Won 11-runner Irish 4yo point in October on sole start in that sphere; backed into odds-on when only third of eight in Newton Abbot bumper (good) last month; that still represents the best bumper form in this field and he's respected on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Leading contender on RPRs on Irish point and bumper form; player on hurdles debut

runners.name: Dunstall Star
runners.spotlight: From the family of these connections' high-class staying chaser Nacarat; still green but off the mark at the second attempt when getting up late in five-runner Exeter bumper (good) three weeks ago; doubtful that was a strong event and he'll surely want further in time but attitude was pleasing; hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Won moderate bumper (good) three weeks ago but attitude was pleasing; can improve

runners.name: Sky Grove
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when second to a good one in Irish point at the start of the year and offered something to work on in a hood (retained) when third of 12 in Hereford bumper (good) on stable debut three weeks ago; might have a say on his introduction to hurdles.
runners.comment: Chased home a good prospect in second Irish point; bumper third encouraging 23 days ago

runners.name: Fine Fellow
runners.spotlight: Staying chasers in the family and he was outpaced before sticking on for third in good-ground bumper at Uttoxeter last May; didn't build on that when well held in course bumper (good to soft) in December; hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Fair third on debut but didn't build on it when well held in course bumper in December

runners.name: Peter's Last Deal
runners.spotlight: Third in a Hereford bumper (good to soft) and maiden hurdle (about 2m4f, good; kept on) last year; didn't build on latter effort when sixth in novice (2m, soft) back at that track in January; still early days.
runners.comment: Didn't build on hurdle debut when sixth (2m, soft) in January; still early days.

races.race_name: Subscribe To Head In Front Betting Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Kantagua Du Large
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare; followed up a couple of fair efforts at Exeter and Taunton with a below-par run at Ffos Las last month; has slipped to a career-low mark and Harry Cobden rides for the first time but she's still looking for her first win in Britain and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Fourth at Exeter and Taunton in March but below par at Ffos Las last month.

runners.name: Silver Gale
runners.spotlight: Has shown improvement since stepping up in trip and switching to handicaps, latest when runner-up at Fakenham (2m7f, good) last month; should go close here.
runners.comment: Has shown improvement since stepping up in trip and switching to handicaps.

runners.name: Broadway Honey
