runners.spotlight: Best effort over hurdles when third in a Hereford novice last November; below that form in a couple of handicaps since but she's still lightly raced and she could show more upped in trip off a sliding mark.
runners.comment: Best effort when third in a Hereford novice last November; below that form since.

runners.name: Mexican Stan
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed from seven runs but shapes as though this step up in trip will suit; has dropped another 4lb and should do better.
runners.comment: Yet to be placed from seven runs but shapes as though this step up in trip will suit.

runners.name: Blue In The West
runners.spotlight: Scored at the 25th attempt at Exeter (2m5f, good) last month and ran well off 6lb higher when runner-up back there under identical conditions ten days later; this hold-up performer could go well again but his overall record makes him a risky proposition for win purposes.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Exeter last month and second back there ten days later.

runners.name: Danger Nap
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start in bumpers but has shown little in three runs over hurdles; still early days and wears first-time cheekpieces on handicap debut up in trip; may well show improvement.
runners.comment: Made a promising start in bumpers but has shown little in three runs over hurdles.

runners.name: Kingston Kid
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on old Irish form but lightly raced and disappointing in recent years; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on old Irish form but lightly raced and disappointing in recent years.

races.race_name: Lewis Badges 1832 Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Three Pikes
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in June last year and got off the mark over hurdles at Stratford (2m2f, good) last month; looks sure to be suited by this step up in trip and holds claims.
runners.comment: Bumper winner in June last year and got off the mark over hurdles at Stratford last month.

runners.name: Camarrate
runners.spotlight: Scored for the first time in two years at Newcastle (2m4f, good) four weeks ago; up 4lb and should not be far away in this follow-up bid.
runners.comment: Scored for the first time in two years at Newcastle four weeks ago.

runners.name: Mammies Boy
runners.spotlight: Won three times for the O'Neill's, including over C&D; ran his best race for current trainer when third of four finishers at Exeter (2m5f, good) last month when clouting the second-last; knocking on the door and should run well.
runners.comment: Ran his best race for current trainer when third at Exeter last month.

runners.name: Urban Isle
runners.spotlight: 0-7 under rules and a beaten co-favourite on his handicap debut when sixth of 12 finishers at Exeter (2m5f, good) last month; first-time cheekpieces now on and still lightly raced so cannot dismiss.
runners.comment: 0-7 and a beaten co-favourite on his handicap debut when sixth at Exeter last month.

runners.name: Impecunious
runners.spotlight: Won twice when trained by Chris Down last year; resumes following 293 days off for new trainer; betting will be best guide to his chance.
runners.comment: Won twice when trained by Chris Down last year; back after 293 days off for new trainer.

runners.name: Gwash
runners.spotlight: Sole success over hurdles came here over two years ago and his best effort came when runner-up at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) last July; well beaten in three runs since, the latest on chase debut at Taunton in November; cheekpieces worn last time are left off and doesn't appeal for win purposes.
runners.comment: Best effort came when runner-up at Uttoxeter last July; well beaten in three runs since.

runners.name: Kom Tu Voudras
runners.spotlight: Former Willie Mullins inmate who got off the mark on her stable debut in a Huntingdon maiden (2m4f, heavy) in February; makes handicap debut on contrasting ground and a fascinating contender here.
runners.comment: Got off the mark on her stable debut in a Huntingdon maiden in February.

runners.name: Il Va De Soi
runners.spotlight: His two wins came on soft in April 2024; out of sorts over fences and hurdles this term; cheekpieces return and has dropped to a career-low mark but doesn't look ready to take advantage.
runners.comment: His two wins came on soft in April 2024; out of sorts over fences and hurdles this term.

races.race_name: Jockey Club 0-110 Rated Series Final Open Hunters' Chase (A Grassroots Hunters' Chase Series Final)

runners.name: Walkin Out
runners.spotlight: Landed maiden hunter chase at Cartmel in May 2023 and a prolific winner in points with 12 wins in that sphere; beat two rivals in a point at Stafford Cross 13 days ago and she should go close.
runners.comment: A prolific winner in points with 12 wins in that sphere.

runners.name: Good Source
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on his rules debut in a Fakenham maiden hunter chase (3m, good) in April 2025 and has performed with credit in points this year, winning at Higham last month; carries a 3lb penalty but should run well.
runners.comment: Has performed with credit in points this year, winning at Higham last month.

runners.name: Best Mate Dave
runners.spotlight: Sole success came over hurdles in June 2023; last of three finishers at Leicester (2m6f, heavy) in February and beaten three times in points since, including in a match; has his work cut out here.
runners.comment: Last of three finishers at Leicester in February and beaten three times in points since.

runners.name: Malinas Glory
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; has made a bright start in points for his current trainer, winning twice last month under Heidi Palin; races prominently and should give a good account.
runners.comment: Has made a bright start in points for his current trainer, winning twice last month.

races.race_name: Claverdon FC Have Done The Double Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Legend D'Airy
runners.spotlight: Made a pleasing debut when second of nine in a Hereford bumper (2m, good) in March; normal progress should see him go close.
runners.comment: Made a pleasing debut when second of nine in a Hereford bumper in March.

runners.name: Dennis's Honor
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 2m4f chase winner, half-sister to 2m7f hurdle winner So Ladylike, out of point winning half-sister to useful 2m jumper Blueberry Boy; yard not renowned for their exploits in bumpers and may need more time.
runners.comment: Yard not renowned for their exploits in bumpers and may need more time.

runners.name: Nate Of Spades
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros foal, 40,000euros 3yo; seventh foal; closely related to point winner Fortunate Soldier, half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Sporty Jim; dam 2m2f hurdle winner, closely related to useful bumper/2m-2m3f hurdle winner Royal Guardsman; in good hands and he needs watching in market on debut.
runners.comment: 40,000euros 3yo; has good pedigree and needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Roll A Dollar
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point in March; beaten 18l on his rules debut when third of seven at Taunton (2m, good) last month and needs to show more.
runners.comment: Beaten 18l on his rules debut when third at Taunton last month.

runners.name: Barascaria Ten
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to useful chaser Escaria Ten, but he was tailed off on his rules debut at Chepstow (2m, soft) in February and will need to have improved significantly to figure here.
runners.comment: Tailed off on his rules debut in a bumper at Chepstow in February.

runners.name: Midnight Escape
runners.spotlight: Shaped with some promise at Market Rasen (2m, good to soft) in December but isn't sure to progress much in bumpers, being first foal of a 2m4f-3m hurdle winner for his owner.
runners.comment: Shaped with some promise at Market Rasen in December.

races.race_name: Butterfly Bay Griffin Plate (Turf)

runners.name: Secret Ingredient
runners.spotlight: Made the running and beat three of today's rivals when second over C&D on last month's debut; sets the form standard.
runners.comment: Made the running and beat three of today's rivals when second over C&D on debut

runners.name: Talents Champion
runners.spotlight: Out of a dual Group 3 sprint winner; only just behind Secret Ingredient when third of six over C&D on last month's debut and has obvious claims if the addition of cheekpieces has any positive effect today.
runners.comment: Third over C&D on debut; obvious claims if new cheekpieces have any positive effect

runners.name: Silvery Knight
runners.spotlight: Out of a three-time winner (6.5f-1m) who has produced a German 2,000gns winner; behind two of these when fourth of six over C&D last month but wasn't beaten far and has obvious scope to build on that effort.
runners.comment: Behind two of these when fourth over C&D on debut but wasn't beaten far

runners.name: Almighty Warrior
runners.spotlight: Out of a 7f winner; didn't look anything out of the ordinary in his trials but the booking of Zac Purton suggests a big run is expected on this official debut.
runners.comment: Didn't look anything special in trials but Zac Purton rides on debut

runners.name: Sharp Planet
runners.spotlight: Won a 4f trial last month and has Joao Moreira enlisted for this official debut; tongue-tie fitted; likely contender.
runners.comment: Won a 4f trial last month and has Joao Moreira enlisted for this official debut
runners.owner: Yiu King Chuen, Chino Yiu Chin Hang & Chris Yiu Chun Hang

runners.name: Show Me Your Love
runners.spotlight: Fluffed the start in his first trial, which is a slight concern, but created pretty good impression when winning one over 5f last month; shortlisted on this official debut.
runners.comment: Created pretty good impression when winning 6f trial last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Spice Bag
runners.spotlight: Has not shown enough in trials to warrant serious interest on this official debut.
runners.comment: Has not shown enough in trials to warrant serious interest on this official debut
runners.owner: Lai Wai Chi & Adrian Michael Bell

runners.name: Ever Wealth
runners.spotlight: Out of a 6f Listed winner; won a 4f trial in March and evidently has ability but has since finished behind Sharp Planet in another; makes official debut today.
runners.comment: Won a 4f trial in March but has since finished behind Sharp Planet in another

runners.name: Gaudium Magnum
runners.spotlight: Out of a three-time winner over about 6.5f; drawn on the wing for this official debut, which is not ideal, but has finished second in his last two trials (4f/5f); wears blinkers.
runners.comment: Drawn on the wing for this official debut but came second in last two trials

runners.name: Glorious Hero
runners.spotlight: Behind three of today's rivals and beaten over 7l when fifth of six over C&D on last month's debut.
runners.comment: Behind three of today's rivals and beaten over 7l when fifth of six over C&D on debut

races.race_name: Tuen Mun Public Riding School Hcp (C4) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Russet Glow
runners.spotlight: Belied 76-1 odds when keeping-on third over C&D in February, his second start, but two lesser efforts have followed (6f/7f); not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Second at big odds over C&D in February but two lesser efforts have followed

runners.name: Bright Day
runners.spotlight: Close second at Happy Valley when dropped back to 6f last month and also ran well from a wide stall there three weeks later; remains on a workable mark and has much better draw here; high on the list.
runners.comment: Two good runs since dropped back to 6f last month; well drawn; high on the list

runners.name: Northern Fire Ball
runners.spotlight: Disappointed when tried in cheekpieces (now removed) over C&D in March and remains 8lb higher than when making virtually all at Happy Valley in February but benefits from combination of favourable draw and Nichola Yuen's useful 10lb claim today.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time but has good draw and Nichola Yuen claims useful 10lb

runners.name: Ryui Kokoroe
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all seven Hong Kong starts; ran on well to take fourth from outside stall at Happy Valley last month but is drawn wide again here; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Did well to finish fourth from stall 12 at Happy Valley last month but is drawn wide again

runners.name: Matzden
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Hong Kong but was denied a clear run before keeping on for fourth over C&D last month and is another 1lb lower today; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: 0-5 in Hong Kong but was denied clear run before keeping on for fourth over C&D last month

runners.name: Quartz Legend
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over C&D on Sunday and now 0-9 in Hong Kong but ran well three consecutive times over 6f beforehand; drawn on the inside and reunited with Joao Moreira today; could have a say.
runners.comment: Well beaten over C&D on Sunday but was running well beforehand; a possible

runners.name: Warriors Dream
runners.spotlight: Opened Hong Kong account with ready C&D win in early March, his 11th attempt, but managed only fifth off new mark later that month and ran poorly (albeit from a wide stall) over 7f last time; needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Opened HK account over C&D in March but needs to kick on again
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung

runners.name: Whowrotethatsong
runners.spotlight: Reserve; took step in right direction when midfield at Happy Valley (1m) last month, his fourth start, but needs more here and the drop back to 6f is not an obvious move.
runners.comment: Not disgraced over 1m last month but needs more again here

runners.name: Rising Elite
runners.spotlight: Reserve; safely held all six starts (6f-1m; mainly on dirt); needs only to drop another 2lb to be eligible for Class 5.
runners.comment: Safely held all six starts (6f-1m; mainly on dirt); needs a drop in grade

runners.name: Lightning Ace
runners.spotlight: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after three starts (all 6f) and probably needs to drop further down the weights.
runners.comment: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after three starts (all 6f)

runners.name: Aca Fast
runners.spotlight: Out of a 7f winner; makes debut after some underwhelming recent trials; best watched.
runners.comment: Makes debut after some underwhelming recent trials; best watched

runners.name: Spirited Steed
runners.spotlight: First foal from a 5f/5.5f winner; displayed clear promise under Zac Purton in a trial last month and the champion jockey is booked again for this official debut; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Showed promise in a trial last month; Purton rides again on this official debut

runners.name: The Cosmic Power
runners.spotlight: Always in rear when a big-priced outsider for debut over C&D in December.
runners.comment: Always in rear when a big-priced outsider for debut over C&D in December
runners.owner: Lam King Wai, Dr Lam Kai Cheong Et Al

runners.name: Extraordinary Gall
runners.spotlight: A down-the-field outsider on both starts (turf/dirt) and has tough draw today; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on both starts (turf/dirt) and has tough draw today

races.race_name: Pok Fu Lam Public Riding School Hcp (C5) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Team Happy
runners.spotlight: Both wins at 6f, over which he was a running-on second at Happy Valley 17 days ago; that was under Zac Purton who has turned elsewhere but from a good draw and over a trip he stays, he's on the shortlist; has form here.
runners.comment: Both wins at 6f but stays this far and latest effort was encouraging

runners.name: Jolly Jumper
runners.spotlight: Only effort of note in his last nine races was the close fifth over 1m here on New Year's Day; up against it from a bad draw; visor back on for blinkers.
runners.comment: Only effort of note in last nine races was the close fifth over 1m here on New Year's Day
runners.owner: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate (T1)

runners.name: Verbier
runners.spotlight: He seems fine from 6f to 1m1f but he ran a bit flat under Joao Moreira at Happy Valley last time and stall 12 is another negative.
runners.comment: He ran a bit flat under Moreira at Happy Valley last time and has wide draw today

runners.name: Speedy Trident
runners.spotlight: He's still a maiden, isn't in great form and he could be better at 6f; therefore, opposable on a few counts.
runners.comment: He's still a maiden, isn't in great form and he could be better at 6f

runners.name: Supreme Winner
runners.spotlight: Reserve; out of the weights and had overweight when winning here over 6f in April; off this mark he was an eyecatching fifth here last week (again over 6f) after a troubled run; stays this far and would have a chance if getting in.
runners.comment: Reserve; has been running well over 6f and stays this far; chance if getting in

runners.name: Run Yes Run
runners.spotlight: Placed early in his career but has essentially become disappointing and now 0-15.
runners.comment: Placed early in his career but has essentially become disappointing and now 0-15
runners.owner: Kong Wing Ming & Edmond Kong Chun Man

runners.name: Sparkle And Gold
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all 13 starts and his best distance remains something of a mystery.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all 13 starts and his best distance remains something of a mystery

runners.name: Only U
runners.spotlight: 1-33 but had been running well in defeat until a very disappointing favourite last time under Zac Purton who now looks elsewhere; possibilities if back on song but perhaps not the percentage play.
runners.comment: Possibilities if back on song but perhaps not the percentage play with him 1-33

runners.name: General Smart
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a recent trial but Zac Purton was aboard when he narrowly missed out over C&D in March and he was drawn out of contention last time over 1m; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Definite chance on his March run and was drawn out of contention last time
runners.owner: Alex Chau Man Lok, Jack Chan Hoi Et Al

runners.name: Winning Cigar
runners.spotlight: Slow start was no help here last time but he didn't head into that race in great form; best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Slow start was no help here last time but he didn't head into that race in great form

runners.name: Superb Guy
runners.spotlight: Has a much better draw than in his recent races but modest runs outweigh the good ones and he's still a maiden.
runners.comment: Well drawn but modest runs outweigh the good ones and he's still a maiden

runners.name: Fight Time
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate of 1-27 and was down the field dropped into a Class 5 last time when tried in cheekpieces; he has since won a trial, which rates a positive.
runners.comment: Modest last run on the track but has the form to feature and has since won a trial
runners.owner: Daren Wong Wai Chow, Lau Kay Jing & Eric Liu Kar Hang

runners.name: Wave Garden
runners.spotlight: Won in New Zealand but his Hong Kong mark has spiralled and he wouldn't be the obvious answer despite having first attempt at a Class 5.
runners.comment: He wouldn't be the obvious answer despite having first attempt at a Class 5

runners.name: The All Rounder
runners.spotlight: No wins in ten but went close over C&D in a Class 4 in March and that was when 3lb higher; he did disappoint from a good draw last time but needs respecting now down in grade.
runners.comment: No wins in ten but went close over C&D in a Class 4 in March; his first run at this level

runners.name: Power Summit
runners.spotlight: Reserve; still a maiden and there would appear to be no light at the end of the tunnel.
runners.comment: Reserve; still a maiden and there would appear to be no light at the end of the tunnel

runners.name: Golden Fortune
runners.spotlight: Has yet to get remotely competitive in seven Hong Kong starts and surprising should he fare any better this time from a wretched draw.
runners.comment: Has yet to get remotely competitive in seven HK starts and poorly drawn

races.race_name: Lei Yue Mun Public Riding School Hcp (C4) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Cap Liner
runners.spotlight: Hasn't built on a really encouraging debut here but recent trial wins suggest he might be back on track; well drawn under Joao Moreira and seriously considered.
runners.comment: Form dipped since debut but recent trial wins suggest he might be back on track

runners.name: Day Day Victory
runners.spotlight: Reserve; effective over 5f and 6f, here and at Happy Valley; fair mark and hasn't been running at all badly.
runners.comment: Reserve; proven winner on a fair mark and hasn't been running at all badly
runners.owner: Alan Kiang Ping Fai & Joyce Lee

runners.name: Grand Nova
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in late 2024 which was his last win; did have a favourable draw when runner-up at Happy Valley last time but that was a good run after quite an absence; enters the equation.
runners.comment: C&D winner in late 2024 which was his last win; positive run at Happy Valley last time

runners.name: Comet Radiance
runners.spotlight: Improved form when going down by half a length here in March and didn't get the rub of the green last time; still lightly raced and has races in him off this mark.
runners.comment: Near miss here in March and didn't get the rub of the green last time

runners.name: Alsonso
runners.spotlight: Blinkers returned when a 2.5l fourth over C&D last month and now cheekpieces are back with a tongue-tie added; has yet to win in Hong Kong but endured some near misses over C&D off higher marks; considered.
runners.comment: No wins in Hong Kong but has endured some near misses over C&D off higher marks

runners.name: Rapid Phantom
runners.spotlight: Placed in a few trials, the latest after his debut third here a month ago; had a favourable high draw that day and yet he's now in stall 2, but should have more to offer; Nichola Yuen also rode last time.
runners.comment: Placed in trials and a fine debut third here a month ago, albeit from a better draw
runners.owner: Karson Choi Ka Tsan & Donnie Yen Chi Tan

runners.name: Beauty Thunder
runners.spotlight: Sole Hong Kong win came at Happy Valley (5f) where he's been a model of consistency in recent weeks; 0-7 at Sha Tin and no better than fourth on those visits, so that's the concern.
runners.comment: Major player on his Happy Valley form but no better than fourth in seven visits here

runners.name: Casa Primo
runners.spotlight: Fifth in two C&D starts and, only 2lb lower, he'll need extra to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Fifth in two C&D starts and, only 2lb lower, he'll need extra to get off the mark

runners.name: Master Payment
runners.spotlight: Competitive in trials before finishing second on his official debut over C&D three weeks ago; the winner was a big price but this gelding went down narrowly despite being stopped in his run; not ideally drawn but Zac Purton takes over.
runners.comment: Second on official debut here; not ideally drawn but Purton takes over

runners.name: Tactical Victory
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner in Australia but well down the field in all his Hong Kong runs, in Class 4s the last twice; opposable.
runners.comment: Well down the field in all his Hong Kong runs, in Class 4s the last twice; opposable

runners.name: E Ho Ho
runners.spotlight: Won in Australia but it's been a quiet start in Hong Kong and he remains best watched, despite a favourable draw.
runners.comment: Won in Australia but it's been a quiet start in Hong Kong and he remains best watched

runners.name: Oneshot
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well when behind Master Payment and Alsonso here last month but he finished last six days ago.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on his debut here but he finished last six days ago
runners.owner: Anthony Chan Wai Choi, Wong Tsz Wa & Kwok Ying Lung

runners.name: Bustling City
runners.spotlight: Managed only ninth when odds-on for C&D debut and again disappointed over 6f here next time; however, better was clearly expected of him in both those races and he's since gone 3-3 in trials; considered.
runners.comment: Twice well beaten when seemingly fancied but has since gone 3-3 in trials

runners.name: Flowing
runners.spotlight: Reserve; hasn't been showing enough in his trials to expect too much on official debut.
runners.comment: Reserve; hasn't been showing enough in his trials to expect too much on official debut

runners.name: Storm Mirror
runners.spotlight: Accrued plenty of experience in trials, winning twice; clearly has ability and drawn well for debut.
runners.comment: Plenty of experience in trials, winning twice; has ability and drawn well for debut

runners.name: Winalot
runners.spotlight: Took a few goes to win a trial which he did last month; likely best watched on official debut.
runners.comment: Took a few goes to win a trial which he did last month; likely best watched on debut

races.race_name: The Hong Kong Riding For The Disabled Association Cup (C4) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Brownneedsfurther
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of his last three races off the same mark, though never troubled the winner last time at Happy Valley; well drawn for one who tends to race prominently and certainly has a chance.
runners.comment: Well drawn for one who tends to race prominently and certainly has a chance

runners.name: Amazing Fun
runners.spotlight: Just missed out over 7f here in January but has achieved very little since.
runners.comment: Just missed out over 7f here in January but has achieved very little since

runners.name: Positive Smile
runners.spotlight: Won twice at this level in January; struggled off his revised mark in better races, though latest effort from well off the pace was encouraging; back in a Class 4 and with a handy draw, he looks a player.
runners.comment: Latest Class 3 effort respectable; this is more his level and he's well drawn

runners.name: Romantic Laos
runners.spotlight: Reserve; not as good as he was but some signs of returning to form off his reduced mark; each-way claims if getting a run.
runners.comment: Reserve; not as good as he was but some signs of returning to form off his reduced mark

runners.name: Flashing Fighter
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 5228 in this year's trials before narrowly holding on after showing early speed over C&D a month ago; Nichola Yuen again claims her 10lb off a 6lb higher mark; respcted.
runners.comment: Narrowly held on after showing early speed over C&D a month ago; 6lb higher

runners.name: Heroic Vanguard
runners.spotlight: Reserve; fifth of 12 on debut but beat one home a month ago when a big price.
runners.comment: Reserve; fifth of 12 on debut but beat one home a month ago when a big price

runners.name: Conrad Patch
runners.spotlight: A 6lb rise for his C&D win in March has surely been a factor in subsequent defeats, though was only 2l away the last time and Britney Wong now takes away 7lb; could be a factor.
runners.comment: Beaten off this mark the last twice but Britney Wong now takes away 7lb

runners.name: Emerging Star
runners.spotlight: Modest form in trials, two novices and on handicap debut; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Modest form in trials, two novices and on handicap debut; look elsewhere

runners.name: Real Gentleman
runners.spotlight: A winner in Ireland but precious little to shout about thus far in Hong Kong, from 6f to 1m.
runners.comment: A winner in Ireland but precious little to shout about in Hong Kong, from 6f to 1m
runners.owner: Wong Chung Yin, Wong Lai Ngor & Tracy Wong Chui

runners.name: E Hopeful
runners.spotlight: Ran better on his second start here 20 days ago but still only ninth of 14; steps up to 6f.
runners.comment: Ran better on his second start here 20 days ago but still only ninth of 14; steps up to 6f

runners.name: Trendy Rush
runners.spotlight: Insufficient promise in his two 6f runs at Happy Valley to merit a second look; has since finished third of the four runners in a trial.
runners.comment: Insufficient promise in his two 6f runs at Happy Valley to merit a second look

runners.name: Graceful Heart
runners.spotlight: Figures of 92125 in his trials, the latest after finishing only ninth on official debut here over 5f; has ability for sure but bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Figures 92125 in his trials, the latest after finishing only ninth here over 5f on debut

runners.name: Impending Legacy
runners.spotlight: Debut run was okay but no more than that and he has twice disappointed since (blinkered last time), and again in subsequent trials.
runners.comment: Debut run was okay but disappointing since on the track and in trials

runners.name: Grouper
runners.spotlight: Mixed bag in a number of trials and a watching brief seems best on official debut.
runners.comment: Mixed bag in a number of trials and a watching brief seems best on official debut

races.race_name: Castle Peak Hcp (C4) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Decision Link
runners.spotlight: First visit to Happy Valley when winning over 6f there last month and, although beaten 3l into second off this 6lb higher mark over C&D 20 days ago, that was still a commendable show from a wide draw; he's burdened with another but still respected.
runners.comment: Burdened with another high draw, as when runner-up over C&D 20 days ago
runners.owner: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen

runners.name: Super Dragon
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and by no means his first encouraging run when a closing fifth from well off the pace here last time, having exited from stall 14; very much enters the reckoning.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and an excellent fifth from the pace last time, given his draw in 14

runners.name: Star Satyr
runners.spotlight: Twice placed over C&D through the winter but his form has cooled markedly; opposable at the minute.
runners.comment: Twice placed over C&D through the winter but his form has cooled markedly

runners.name: Aquaman
runners.spotlight: Reserve; the third over C&D in February is his standout piece of form; opposable on balance of exploits.
runners.comment: Reserve; the third over C&D in February is his standout piece of form
runners.owner: Steve Chan Chi Fung & Terry Ng Ting Chi

runners.name: Lucky Man
runners.spotlight: Signed off for last yard on a downer but pretty much bolted up on debut for this one over C&D a month ago, with Harry Bentley doing the steering; 7lb higher but looks to have turned a corner.
runners.comment: 7lb higher than for last month's won but that was an impressive stable debut

runners.name: The Concentration
runners.spotlight: Did really well in Class 5s but his mark took a hit and he was only midfield in a Class 4 here last time; what will help is Nichola Yuen's useful 10lb claim.
runners.comment: Did well in Class 5s but mark took a hit and only midfield in a Class 4 here last time.

runners.name: Solid Car
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 12-race maiden with a race in him off this mark but perhaps at 6f.
runners.comment: Reserve; 12-race maiden with a race in him off this mark but perhaps at 6f

runners.name: Invictus Dragon
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; placed behind in-form opposition (6f/7f) before another fair effort when fifth last month, for all that he was a beaten favourite; Zac Purton, aboard for those last three races, keeps the faith.
runners.comment: Respectable in defeat under Purton in his last three races; solid, though drawn wide

runners.name: Leather Goods
runners.spotlight: Best run was his first and hasn't progressed at all; also well held in a recent trial.
runners.comment: Best run was his first and hasn't progressed at all; also well held in a recent trial

runners.name: Cheerful Wongchoy
runners.spotlight: Promising runs over 6f at Happy Valley and wasn't beaten a million miles when eighth upped to 7f here a month ago, finishing alongside The Concentration.
runners.comment: Wasn't beaten a million miles when eighth upped to 7f here a month ago

runners.name: Money Tycoon
runners.spotlight: Latest run here was a small step in the right direction but that was over 6f; unconvincing.
runners.comment: Latest run here was a small step in the right direction but that was over 6f; unconvincing

runners.name: Celestial Strider
runners.spotlight: A well-beaten outsider on all four starts and lower-grade races probably await.
runners.comment: A well-beaten outsider on all four starts and lower-grade races probably await

runners.name: Flying Fortune
runners.spotlight: Struggled initially but showed a tad more relegated into a Class 4 last time over C&D, finishing just under 4l off the winner; still doesn't look on that good a mark, down only 2lb.
runners.comment: Bit better here last time but doesn't look on that good a mark, down only 2lb

runners.name: Invictus
runners.spotlight: Has competitive form in trials, including a second since his midfield finish on debut here over 5f; raised 2f in trip here and could have a bigger effort in him.
runners.comment: Debut here was over 5f; now upped to 7f and his trial efforts read well

runners.name: Lucky Bid
runners.spotlight: Without in a win in trials, before or since finishing sixth here over 6f last month; tricky draw as well.
runners.comment: Without in a win in trials, before or since finishing sixth here over 6f last month
runners.owner: Adrian Kwan Chi Keung, Hui Wing Ka & Ho Siu Kong

runners.name: Best World
runners.spotlight: Figures in this year's trials read 36634 and he looks one to watch on official debut.
runners.comment: Figures in this year's trials read 36634 and he looks one to watch on official debut

races.race_name: The St George's Challenge Cup (C4) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Fighting Machine
runners.spotlight: Regressive 6yo who never really looked dangerous when eighth here last time and losing run is mounting up.
runners.comment: Regressive 6yo who never really looked dangerous when eighth here last time

runners.name: Illuminous
runners.spotlight: Stuck on a losing run since his winning debut but has returned with encouraging runs off his much reduced mark, beaten 4l in handicaps here over 6f and 7f; this should be more his trip and he's one to consider despite his wide draw.
runners.comment: Better the last twice off his reduced mark and returning to further should be a plus
runners.owner: Christina Y C Ting, Chen Wen Bin Et Al

runners.name: Superb Kid
runners.spotlight: Regular here; should appreciate this drop in grade but he's not been in the best of form and has dropped unlucky with the draw.
runners.comment: He's not been in the best of form and has dropped unlucky with the draw

runners.name: Amazing Duck
runners.spotlight: Losing run stretches back to early 2024; there were definite signs of him returning to form but his last run needs forgiving.
runners.comment: There were definite signs of him returning to form but his last run needs forgiving

runners.name: Red Brick Warrior
runners.spotlight: Made significant ground from the rear when fourth over 1m at Happy Valley on penultimate start and again he got behind back there last time; can be slowly away but still lightly raced and could go well if giving himself a chance.
runners.comment: Can be slowly away but lightly raced and could go well if giving himself a chance

runners.name: Bling Bling Genius
runners.spotlight: Finally came good over C&D last month but that was a Class 5 he won comfortably and he has gone up 6lb.
runners.comment: Finally came good over C&D last month but that was a Class 5; up 6lb

runners.name: Incanto Star
runners.spotlight: Reserve; runner-up at Happy Valley in January but that was in no headgear and he's struggled since in blinkers.
runners.comment: Reserve; best last run was in no headgear and he's since struggled in blinkers

runners.name: Lucky Year
runners.spotlight: A close sixth over C&D two runs back despite hitting traffic and again not a lot went right on last month's return visit; lightly raced and remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; close up two runs back and nothing went to plan last time

runners.name: Circuit Marshal
runners.spotlight: Looked progressive when runner-up over C&D two runs ago and wouldn't read too much into latest defeat (behind Turin Champions) as he couldn't get involved from off the pace; not ruled out.
runners.comment: An improved second in March and wouldn't read too much into latest defeat

runners.name: Victor Supreme
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and has been closing on the principals in his last three races, all over C&D (last time under Zac Purton); he's drawn wider than ideal but turn looks near.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and has been closing on the principals in his last three races, all over C&D

runners.name: Celtic Times
runners.spotlight: Reserve; knows how to win but he's another who has dropped his guard of late.
runners.comment: Reserve; knows how to win but he's another who has dropped his guard of late

runners.name: Gallant Epoch
runners.spotlight: A good run of form has triggered this career-high mark off which he was only sixth last time, and that was with Nichola Yuen claiming 10lb; might be vulnerable.
runners.comment: A good run of form triggered this career-high mark off which he was only sixth last time
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Simon Suen Siu Man And Chloe Suen

runners.name: Ka Ying Superb
runners.spotlight: Lost his way after nearly winning a Class 3 over 7f here last September but got back on track in winning a recent trial and he's now downgraded with Nichola Yuen taking off 10lb; from stall 2 he's in the mix.
runners.comment: Got back on track in winning recent trial and he's now downgraded with good claimer booked

runners.name: Beauty Viva
runners.spotlight: Opened his Hong Kong account at the 20th attempt over Happy Valley's 1m in March but hasn't kicked on from there; off this mark, others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won in March but hasn't kicked on from there and off this mark others are preferred

runners.name: Audacious Pursuit
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in November and even better when twice runner-up at 1m1f this year; latest effort was a big backward step but he's a lot better than that run in early March; has since finished third in a recent trial.
runners.comment: Latest run was a big backward step but this C&D winner is a lot better than that

runners.name: Turin Champions
runners.spotlight: Has had some bad luck with draws and in running, so it was no great surprise to see him run well back in a Class 4 two weeks ago when he only faded late on; wouldn't be exposed just yet and he enters calculations.
runners.comment: Finally had some luck here last time and only faded late on, finishing third
runners.owner: Dr Sheldon Tse Hiu Tung, Tsoi Huk Yam & Ken Huang Jian Hua

races.race_name: Lei Yue Mun Park Hcp (C3) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Kansas
runners.spotlight: Smart and speedy 2yo when based in Ireland but launched Hong Kong career with two down-the-field runs last month (5f/6f).
runners.comment: Launched Hong Kong career with two down-the-field runs last month (5f/6f)

runners.name: Spicy Standard
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who caused a 34-1 surprise over C&D on seasonal debut in September and has gone close on two of his three starts since, latterly from stall 11 here last month; recent 2lb nudge enforces this rise in grade; still considered.
runners.comment: Went close from wide stall here last month; forced up in grade but still considered

runners.name: Thriving Brothers
runners.spotlight: Consistent 5yo who won over 7f in this grade in November and kept on well for second when dropped back to 6f at Happy Valley last month; well drawn here and current mark is workable; can make presence felt.
runners.comment: Consistent 5yo; on workable mark and has good draw; can make presence felt
runners.owner: Yeung Chun Wang, Yeung Ho Wang & Yeung Chung Wang

runners.name: Motor
runners.spotlight: Reserve; led close home at Happy Valley in March and was placed off today's mark there last month; evidently in good heart but will be moving up in grade if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark since winning in March but moves up in grade today

runners.name: Superb Capitalist
runners.spotlight: Ought to be happier back on turf, following last month's down-the-field dirt run, and has the inside draw but has lost his way since 5f win on seasonal debut in September; others have less to prove.
runners.comment: Ought to be happier back on turf but has lost his way since 5f win in September
runners.owner: Li Cham Kuen & Li Ming Choi

runners.name: Rising From Ashes
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D on seasonal debut in September but out of form in recent months and now eligible for a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Out of form in recent months and now eligible for a drop in grade

runners.name: Ace Champion
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; went close from the inside stall at Happy Valley in March, his fourth Hong Kong start, and had the outside stall when only seventh there last month; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: 0-5 in HK but went very close at Happy Valley two starts ago; each-way claims at least

runners.name: Chill Buddy
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent 4yo who came good in Hong Kong at the eighth attempt, over C&D in February, and posted another good effort when fourth off new mark last month; well drawn again and Nichola Yuen claims a useful 10lb this time; ought to give another good account.
runners.comment: In-form; won over C&D in February and benefits from Nichols Yuen's 10lb claim today

runners.name: Thunder Action
runners.spotlight: Signed off in Australia with 7f maiden win last August, his third run; drops in trip for Hong Kong debut; interesting to see how he fares.
runners.comment: Signed off in Australia with 7f maiden win last August; down in trip for Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Jubilant Winner
runners.spotlight: Dominated from the front at Happy Valley 17 days ago and has now won four times this season; could still have more improvement to come but outside draw dents his appeal today.
runners.comment: Made all at Happy Valley last month, his fourth win this season; drawn wide today, though

runners.name: Sovereign Fund
runners.spotlight: Reserve; returned to form with win at Happy Valley on stable debut in February and put two lesser efforts behind him when placed there last month but has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: Kept on for third at Happy Valley last month but has become hard to predict
runners.owner: Glenn Chan Wai Cheung & Raymond Chan Ka Shun

runners.name: Lucky My Way
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner towards end of last season but out of form over 7f this term (four runs); drops back in trip for stable debut; needs to have been revitalised by his new regime.
runners.comment: Out of form over 7f this season; drops back in trip for stable debut
runners.owner: Patrick Wong Ho Yin, Ricky Choy Wing Kay Et Al

runners.name: Effortless Win
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut in 6f maiden in Australia last July; unraced since but market support will make him of interest on this Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: Absent since winning 6f maiden on debut last July; market may guide
runners.owner: 25/26 Frankie Lor Fu Chuen Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Gold Patch
runners.spotlight: Did not need to be asked for maximum effort to win over C&D last month and is now 3-4; wide draw makes things awkward today but it's hard to tell when his progress will level off.
runners.comment: Won fairly comfortably here last month and is now 3-4; commands respect, despite wide draw

races.race_name: Pok Fu Lam Country Park Hcp (C3) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Parents' Love
runners.spotlight: Returned to form over C&D last month, keeping on well to cause 21-1 surprise; nicely drawn against the nearside rail today but likely to find things tougher in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Returned to form over C&D last month, keeping on well to cause 21-1 surprise; up in grade
runners.owner: Edmund Ho Kwok Shing, Joe Fong Fu Yuen Et Al

runners.name: Sparkling Fellow
runners.spotlight: Not at best on last two appearances but had to wait for room before going very close over C&D (albeit in lower-grade company) in January and remains on a good mark; can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Not at best lately but went close over C&D in January and is on a good mark

runners.name: Looking Bright
runners.spotlight: Disappointing favourite over C&D last month and now 0-7 in Hong Kong but finished close up here on his two previous appearances and has good claims if judged on that form.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite here last time but previous two C&D runs give him a good shout

runners.name: Mickley
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over 6f this year but his only Hong Kong win was gained over 1m and he's drawn on the wing for this first attempt at 5f.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice over 6f this year but drawn on the wing for first 5f attempt

runners.name: Master Of All
runners.spotlight: Began season in good form over 7f but has looked badly out of sorts in recent months; makes very limited appeal on first 5f start since 2023.
runners.comment: Began season in good form over 7f but has looked badly out of sorts in recent months

runners.name: Lightness Of Music
runners.spotlight: Impressed when winning over C&D on debut last June but returned with two disappointing efforts at start of this season (5f/6f); switched stables during subsequent 160-day break; still has plenty of time to get career back on track but is not easy to predict.
runners.comment: Impressed on debut last June; twice well held at start of this season; now with new stable

runners.name: Lucky Candy
runners.spotlight: Began Hong Kong career with some inauspicious runs at start of this year but was placed here twice last month; denied clear run over C&D on first occasion and had wide draw over 6f last time; 1lb lower and sports new cheekpieces today; interesting.
runners.comment: Denied clear run when third over C&D last month; also third from wide stall over 6f since
runners.owner: Yeung Wai Kwong & Lee Sheung Ping

runners.name: Alpha Strike
runners.spotlight: Suffered dip in form after winning over C&D on Hong Kong debut in October but has finished fourth over C&D on his last two appearances; 1lb lower today and Joao Moreira takes the ride; one to consider.
runners.comment: In the frame over C&D on last two outings; 1lb lower today and Joao Moreira rides today

runners.name: Fiery Steed
runners.spotlight: Struggled to get competitive on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/6f).
runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/6f)

runners.name: Straight To Glory
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f Class 4 winner at Happy Valley in January; runner-up in this higher grade since and should be okay back at 5f but his last run was disappointing and, on balance, others look stronger.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner this year; should be okay back at 5f but others look stronger
runners.owner: Chan Tim Shing & Pang Wai Hak

runners.name: Together We Value
runners.spotlight: Reserve; Ex-Australian 4yo; not beaten far after finishing well over this trip at Happy Valley on last two starts and will have possibilities if he gets a good pace to aim at here.
runners.comment: Not beaten far after finishing well over this trip at Happy Valley on last two starts
runners.owner: 25/26 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Metro Power
runners.spotlight: Just did enough to make all at Happy Valley two starts ago and can cope with this drop back in trip but his latest run was disappointing and his current mark probably demands a career-best effort.
runners.comment: Just held on to make all over 6f two runs ago; 5f also suits but career best needed today

runners.name: Ever Luck
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who made all over C&D when dropped in trip last month and is now 2-5; moves up in grade today but could still have untapped potential over 5f; respected.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D when dropped in trip last month and is now 2-5; up in grade; respected

runners.name: Fast Responder
runners.spotlight: Doubled Hong Kong tally over C&D last month, holding on by a neck under 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen; evidently in good nick but looks vulnerable after recent 7lb rise.
runners.comment: Held on well by a neck over C&D last month but looks vulnerable after 7lb rise
runners.owner: Hong Kong St John Presidents Club Syndicate

runners.name: Watch Legend
runners.spotlight: Ended three-race career in Australia with maiden handicap win over about 6.5f in September (first-time blinkers); drops back in trip for Hong Kong debut; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Won 6.5f maiden handicap in Australia in September; makes HK debut today; best watched

runners.name: Master Champion
runners.spotlight: Reserve; began season with two good 6f runs at Happy Valley, the latter a win, but finished last in this higher grade there on next two outings; makes stable debut after 199-day break.
runners.comment: Began season in good form but absent since finishing last twice in October; new stable

races.race_name: Pok Fu Lam Reservoir Hcp (C3) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Hymnbook
runners.spotlight: Quite useful when trained in Britain and has showed promise on two of his three Hong Kong starts, more notably when keeping on well for close fifth in C&D contest won by Amazing Partners four weeks ago; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Kept on for close fifth in C&D contest won by Amazing Partners last month; still unexposed
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung

runners.name: Legend Winner
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong with a rather mixed record but was placed over C&D in January and is now 7lb lower; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-6 in Hong Kong with mixed record but was placed off 7lb higher over C&D in January

runners.name: Withallmyfaith
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; placed over 1m on four of his seven starts since blinkers were added in November and still on a workable mark but stall 14 makes things awkward today.
runners.comment: Placed on four of seven starts since blinkers were added but has awkward draw today

runners.name: M M Concord
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all three Hong Kong starts; put in fairly good front-running shift before dropping to fifth here (7f) three weeks ago but needs another step forward today.
runners.comment: Ran quite well over 7f last month but needs another step forward today

runners.name: Master Trillion
runners.spotlight: First past the post (subsequently demoted to second) in Class 4 C&D contest in March but well beaten in this higher grade twice since.
runners.comment: Finished first in C&D Class 4 in March but well beaten in this higher grade twice since

runners.name: Family Jewel
runners.spotlight: Built upon satisfactory seasonal debut when fourth over C&D in March but has disappointed twice since (1m2f/1m; blinkers added last time); drawn wide today; others have more compelling claims.
runners.comment: Ran well when fourth over C&D in March but has disappointed twice since
runners.owner: Tony Stradmoor, Billy Li Sze Kuen Et Al

runners.name: Rising Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f Class 4 winner this season; has held his own over C&D since upped to this grade but managed only eighth behind Blazing Wukong a fortnight ago; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Holding his own since upped to this grade but others appeal more all the same
runners.owner: James Kwan Wai Ming & Leung Kam Chuen

runners.name: Golden Champ
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner when based in New Zealand but launched Hong Kong career with three low-key runs (6f-7f); upped in trip today; may need more time to acclimatise.
runners.comment: Four-time winner in New Zealand but launched Hong Kong career with three low-key runs

runners.name: Amazing Partners
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who scored over C&D when upped in trip last month and is now 3-6; still unexposed as a miler and it's hard to tell when his improvement will level off; makes obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Progressive 4yo who scored over C&D when upped in trip last month and is now 3-6
runners.owner: Thomas Liang Cheung Biu & Elizabeth Lee Wong Sing Fung

runners.name: Smart Avenue
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f here in December and went close on consecutive appearances here (1m/1m1f) in March; needs to rebound from a disappointing 1m2f run last month but Zac Purton takes the ride; contender if perked up by change of headgear (swaps visor for blinkers).
runners.comment: Disappointed last time but generally in good form this season; headgear switched
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Chan Chung Yin & Mr & Mrs Clevin Got Chong Key

runners.name: Mister Dapper
runners.spotlight: Late-maturing 6yo who prevailed by a nose at Happy Valley in February and probably posted another career-best performance when close second to Amazing Partners over C&D last month; drawn wide today but, with Nichola Yuen claiming 10lb, must be considered.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D last month and Nichola Yuen claims 10lb today; must be considered
runners.owner: P Chan Pak Hou, J Chan Chi Kwong & J Chan Chon San

runners.name: Make You Smile
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 7f Group 3 winner as a 2yo in Britain but, following a long absence, has shown only minor promise over that trip on his two Hong Kong starts this year; upped in trip with blinkers added today.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise on first two HK starts (7f); blinkers added today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Tony Tang Wai Bun And Vicky Tang

runners.name: Call Me Magnifique
runners.spotlight: 3-9 when trained in Australia, signing off with 1m2f handicap win in November; worth a glance in the betting before this Hong Kong debut; cheekpieces removed.
runners.comment: 3-9 when trained in Australia, signing off with 1m2f handicap win in November; HK debut
runners.owner: 25/26 Danny Shum Chap Shing Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Blazing Wukong
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season, latterly from off the pace in first-time blinkers a fortnight ago; latest 7lb rise asks a lot of him but the new headgear may yet enable further progress.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season, latterly in first-time blinkers last month; up another 7lb

runners.name: Endued
runners.spotlight: Just held on to pip Smart Avenue over C&D in March and has been placed over C&D twice since, latterly when third behind Blazing Wukong a fortnight ago; ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Placed twice since C&D win in March; ought to be in the thick of things again

races.race_name: Sai Wan Shan Hcp (C2) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Victor The Winner
runners.spotlight: Group 1 C&D winner in his prime but badly out of form this season, albeit not beaten far when seventh behind Crimson Flash last time.
runners.comment: Group 1 C&D winner in his prime but badly out of form this season

runners.name: Lady's Choice
runners.spotlight: Now 13lb lower than when going close over C&D on seasonal debut in October but this year's form has been underwhelming and he's drawn on the outside today; needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: This year's form has been underwhelming and he's drawn on the outside today
runners.owner: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate

runners.name: Public Attention
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on first three Hong Kong starts (6f-7f) and both subsequent defeats can probably be overlooked (tough task/dirt debut); back on turf and well drawn for this stable debut; a possible.
runners.comment: Made the frame on first three HK starts and both subsequent defeats were excusable

runners.name: Bulb General
runners.spotlight: Lighty raced and progressive 4yo who won three in a row over C&D last year; returned from six-month break with keeping-on third behind Young Champion and Patch Of Starts here six weeks ago; will be dangerous if that outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins last year; returned from break with pleasing C&D third in March; big player

runners.name: Young Champion
runners.spotlight: Placed three consecutive times before fending off Patch Of Stars to score over C&D in March, and again ran well when close third behind Crimson Flash here last month; likely contender again.
runners.comment: Narrow C&D winner in March and continued good run of form with close third here last month

runners.name: Pakistan Legacy
runners.spotlight: Reserve; made all over C&D in March and ran well in defeat three weeks later but has a point to prove after duff effort in April.
runners.comment: Struck form in March but needs to rebound from last month's duff effort

runners.name: Self Improvement
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won dirt Group 3 in South Korea in September but not much has gone to plan since and he was well beaten over C&D last month.
runners.comment: Won dirt Group 3 in South Korea in September but not much has gone to plan since
runners.owner: Wong Chung Yin, Wong Lai Ngor & Wilson Wong Chun Wing

runners.name: Invincible Sage
runners.spotlight: Won C&D Group 1 in 2024 but has regressed and become inconsistent; too well handicapped to rule out but finished last over C&D on latest outing two months ago and can't be supported with much confidence.
runners.comment: Too well handicapped to rule out but finished last over C&D on latest outing; risky

runners.name: Gustosisimo
runners.spotlight: Below form at Happy Valley last time (raced wide from outside stall) but made the frame four consecutive times over 6f/7f beforehand and has each-way claims if judged on those efforts.
runners.comment: Didn't fire from wide stall last time but earlier form gives him good each-way claims

runners.name: Akashvani
runners.spotlight: Dominated from the front over 7f here in January; not in same form over trips ranging from 5f to 1m1f since but is well drawn to attack today and Britney Wong claims 7lb.
runners.comment: Mixed form over variety of trips this season but well drawn to attack from the front

runners.name: Patch Of Stars
runners.spotlight: In the frame all three starts since his consecutive C&D wins in October/November, losing out only by a neck to Young Champion on latest appearance six weeks ago; well drawn; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: In the frame all three starts since his consecutive C&D wins in October/November

runners.name: Hot Delight
runners.spotlight: Very promising 3yo who is unbeaten after three C&D races this year; up in grade today and has awkward draw for a prominent racer but it's hard to gauge the limit of his ability.
runners.comment: Very promising 3yo who is unbeaten after three C&D races this year; up in grade

runners.name: Crimson Flash
runners.spotlight: Bang-in-form 4yo who led close him over C&D last month and now bids to complete a hat-trick; combination of double-digit draw and latest 7lb rise demands more improvement, though.
runners.comment: Led close home over C&D last month and now bids for a hat-trick; up 7lb; drawn wide

runners.name: Lucky With You
runners.spotlight: Drops in grade after contesting a Group 2 last month but this regressive 8yo has been well below his best this season; revival needed.
runners.comment: Drops in grade today but this regressive 8yo has been out of form this season

races.race_name: The Sportsbet Goodwood (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Flying For Fun
runners.spotlight: Really good run coming from well back in the Robert Sangster Stakes this venue at last start this journey. Can challenge.
runners.owner: M W O'Keeffe & Ms V J King

runners.name: Watchme Win
runners.spotlight: In the thick of it at Morphettville two back 11 Apr. Put in some big strides late this venue second-up. Hard to warm to.
runners.owner: A J Hehir, D W Rasheed Et Al

runners.name: Super Smink
runners.spotlight: Last start this trip ran on okay without troubling this venue. Has a tricky draw. Others with greater appeal.
runners.owner: Mrs C L Morton, McHams Et Al

runners.name: New York Lustre
runners.spotlight: Issued decent challenge John Hawkes Stakes this venue at first run off a long break. Strong wet stats. Fitter second-up. At optimum distance.

runners.name: Midtown Boss
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Last at turn, ran on late here at first run off a long break. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: Eales Racing Pty Ltd, Ballcourt Dream Et Al

runners.name: Desert Lightning
runners.spotlight: Won the 3yo+ Open Moonee Valley 6 Sep. Ran on late from the back at Mornington when off an extended spell. Strips fitter second run back. First time around Morphettville.

runners.name: Tycoon Star
runners.spotlight: Sat behind speed and was strongest late here over this distance. Has an adverse draw. Harder now.
runners.owner: Lindsay Park Bloodstock, Yu Long Investments Et Al

runners.name: Extragalactic
runners.spotlight: Made late and solid headway into the placings this track. Untested in wet. Shapes as a rough place prospect.
runners.owner: T W Hennessy, Choochoo Racing Et Al

runners.name: Recon
runners.spotlight: Stormed home from the tail to an impressive victory at Mornington at last start this journey. Could give some cheek at odds.

runners.name: Meridius
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Annexed the (Bm96) at Caulfield 21 Mar. Reserved at back and gave late chase into third at Caulfield. Nice gate.
runners.owner: Mrs M L Kennewell, D Rogers Et Al

runners.name: Power Beau
runners.spotlight: On pace but conceded late this track. Adverse barrier tests. Leaning towards others.

runners.name: Grand Larceny
runners.spotlight: Determined effort to secure victory this track at resumption. Awkwardly drawn. Tackling stronger company.
runners.owner: Werrett Bloodstock, Madden Bloodstock Et Al

runners.name: Enriched
runners.spotlight: Let-up (49-days). Not disgraced in soft going at Rosehill. Drawn well. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: T M Board, Mrs M Board Et Al

runners.name: Talkanco
runners.spotlight: Picked up intently to just miss in the 2yo+ WFA at Ascot at last start this journey. Drawn favourably and under less weight. Worth a look.
runners.owner: Woodbridge Thoroughbreds, Ms R Huckson Et Al

races.race_name: Calvin Houghland Iroquois Stakes (Grade 1) (Hurdle) (Turf)

runners.name: Abaan
runners.owner: Eclipse Thoroughbred Partners & Daigneault Thoroughbreds Llc

races.race_name: Royal Palm Juvenile Stakes (Conditions) (Turf)
Royal Palm Juvenile Fillies Stakes (Conditions) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Easy Life
runners.owner: R A Hill Stable, BlacRridge Stables LLC A& SGV Thoroughbreds

races.race_name: William Hill Epic Boosts Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Twisting Physics
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced, progressive 5yo; made all for narrow win at Newbury (7f) last June and shaped encouragingly upped to 1m2f back there (also good), racing freely but keeping on well from a poor draw on reappearance three weeks ago; retains plenty of potential.
runners.comment: Lightly raced, progressive 5yo who made eye-catching return three weeks ago; big player

runners.name: Double Red
runners.spotlight: Hit the ground running last year, winning twice at Nottingham in April, over 8.5f (good) and 1m2f (good to firm); also scored at Epsom (good to soft) in September and once again made a winning return when picking up an AW handicap here (1m2f) 19 days ago; up 5lb and this is tougher but he's progressive and can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Fourth win when scoring on return; up 5lb and this is tougher but he's progressive

runners.name: Morcar
runners.spotlight: Close third in this in 2024 and he enhanced a good C&D record when winning last year's edition (good to firm) from this mark; placed on his next four starts and had a soft-ground excuse on final start that term; had wind surgery ahead of a quiet comeback at Newbury three weeks ago but no surprise if this day has been ringed in his calendar.
runners.comment: Good C&D record; won this last year; looks sure to be primed for this race again

runners.name: Sweet Reward
runners.spotlight: Registered his seventh win when scoring on reappearance at Newbury (1m2f, good) last year and he attracted support before finishing second to an unexposed sort at Epsom (8.5f, good) on recent comeback this time; might not come on a bundle for that but this trip is more suitable and he's in with an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Might not improve for comeback second (8.5f) but this trip is more suitable; each-way shot

runners.name: Salamanca City
runners.spotlight: 3-12 on turf (all 1m2f), the third of those wins coming when she scraped home at Yarmouth (good) on her reappearance four weeks ago; 3lb nudge puts her on a career-high mark.
runners.comment: 3-12 on turf (all 1m2f); scraped home on return and needs more from career-high mark

runners.name: Warning Sign
runners.spotlight: Won two amateur riders' races last year but not competitive in either start this term; suspicion he'll come back to life returned to those earlier events when owner David Dunsdon is back in the saddle.
runners.comment: Won two amateur riders' races last year; of more interest when returned to that sphere

runners.name: Noble Horizon
runners.spotlight: Consistent for William Haggas and he made all to land Leicester novice (1m2f, good to firm) in July; signed off for that yard with creditable third of 19 at York (10.5f, good; not clear run) off this mark in October; secured for 140,000gns a fortnight later and subsequently gelded; a player on stable debut/reappearance.
runners.comment: Bought for 140,000gns after good 3rd of 19 (10.5f, good; not clear run) in October; player

runners.name: Towerlands
runners.spotlight: Improved once returned to AW and bagged a hat-trick across October and December (1m); on the scoresheet again upped to 9.5f at Wolverhampton (career-best form) in February; form took a slight dip on last two starts, while his earlier turf form isn't comparable so interesting to see how he fares back on grass.
runners.comment: Hat-trick once back on AW; chance depends on what he makes of this return to turf

runners.name: Cristo
runners.spotlight: Went back-to-back over 1m2f on Chelmsford's AW around this time last year; winless since and latest eighth of ten at Ripon (1m2f, good to soft) didn't suggest he's about to capitalise on a declining mark; a handicapping case can be made, though.
runners.comment: Handicapping case can be made but latest effort didn't suggest he's about to capitalise

runners.name: Youthful King
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m2f (good to firm) although that was back in 2022/23 and his last success came over 1m6f at Sandown in July; tailed off on final start last term and two runs (1m4f) this season contained little encouragement.
runners.comment: Tailed off on final start last term and two runs (1m4f) this season weren't encouraging

races.race_name: Pertemps Network Swinton Handicap Hurdle (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tellherthename
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced for his age; caught the eye in no uncertain terms when twelfth of 23 in the County at Cheltenham on first start for current yard, travelling smoothly but short of room in straight and finishing full of running; very best efforts for previous stables came on good ground and he missed the Scottish Champion when the rain came; still unexposed and a fascinating contender in a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Caught eye in County at Cheltenham on first start for current yard; unexposed; big chance

runners.name: Magna Victor
runners.spotlight: Trained in Shropshire but has raced mainly in Scotland; followed two wins at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) over the winter with creditable third of 15 at Ayr (2m, soft) three weeks ago; more needed off unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Creditable third at Ayr three weeks ago but needs a personal best off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Made U Blush
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly who gets weight all round; made it 2-3 over hurdles with a Listed juvenile win at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in January and she improved further when a close second at Sandown (2m, good) last month; open to further progress and she's a second plausible candidate for Dan Skelton.
runners.comment: 4yo filly who gets weight all round; close second at Sandown last time; respected

runners.name: Indemnity
runners.spotlight: 4-6 on good ground over hurdles, notching another personal best when returning from a break to win readily at Plumpton (2m; 15-2) last month; a 10lb rise demands further improvement but he's thriving and should encounter suitable conditions again.
runners.comment: 4-6 on good ground over hurdles; ready winner at Plumpton; up 10lb but he's thriving

runners.name: Star Of Guiting
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in the winter and has had excuses since, making late headway after a slow start when eighth of 21 at Aintree four weeks ago; probably in much better heart than recent form-figures suggest, and he's an interesting each-way proposition.
runners.comment: Won twice at Musselburgh in the winter and has had excuses since; interesting each-way

runners.name: Hamlet's Night
runners.spotlight: 0-14 on Flat turf and a beaten favourite at Musselburgh two weeks ago; his hurdles record (2-7) is better but he had no obvious excuse when fifth to Indemnity at Plumpton (33-1) last month; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Had no obvious excuse when fifth to Indemnity at Plumpton last month; others appeal more

runners.name: Mr McLoughlan
runners.spotlight: Free-goer who was on a roll last autumn, winning three times; well held behind Star Of Guiting at Musselburgh on New Year's Day and couldn't land a blow behind Dance And Glance over C&D last month, but he should get plenty of cover in this big field and could outrun his likely odds.
runners.comment: Again too free here last time; should get cover in a big field and could outrun his odds

runners.name: Helnwein
runners.spotlight: Often gives his running in strong handicaps (second in this event 12 months ago) but losing sequence is up to 11 and he looked held when falling two out in the Plumpton race won by Indemnity last time; cheekpieces left off today; opposable off unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Held when falling in the Plumpton race won by Indemnity; opposable off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Bubble Dubi
runners.spotlight: Has run just four times since 2023; returned from long absence to win at Aintree (2m1f, good to soft) last November but failed to repeat the form in prestigious handicaps at Newbury and Sandown; something to prove.
runners.comment: Returned from long break to win at Aintree but has failed to repeat the form; bit to prove

runners.name: Dance And Glance
runners.spotlight: 5-14 strike-rate and steadily progressive form, gaining his latest success over this C&D (good ground; three of today's rivals behind) in convincing fashion; a 6lb rise does not look harsh, and with further improvement on the cards he has solid claims.
runners.comment: Progressive; had two of these behind when winning over C&D last month; solid claims

runners.name: Frontier Prince
runners.spotlight: Has proved a model of consistency in the last 12 months and he rallied for fourth behind Dance And Glance over C&D last time; however, that run underlined that the handicapper appears to have his measure for now; needs to be boosted by the fitting of cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Another creditable run when fourth over C&D last time but the handicapper seems in charge

runners.name: Bucephalus
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who regularly gives his all and was beaten off only on the run-in when third to Dance And Glance over C&D (good) five weeks ago; should make another bold bid but several of these look less exposed.
runners.comment: Front-runner; third over C&D last time; in the mix but several of these look less exposed

runners.name: No Ordinary Joe
runners.spotlight: Finally took advantage of some leniency from the handicapper when winning at Sandown (2m4f, good) two weeks ago, always in the firing line; still well treated on old form but he's not sure to be so effective back over this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Ended long losing run over 2m4f at Sandown; not sure to be so effective back in trip

runners.name: Laafi
runners.spotlight: Useful on Flat and has quickly reached a similar level over hurdles, posting a career best when first past the post in a big field at Aintree's Grand National meeting (2m, good to soft) only to be disqualified for a whip offence; seeks compensation but a 6lb higher mark will make things tougher.
runners.comment: First past post at Aintree last month only to be disqualified for whip offence; 6lb higher

runners.name: Gibbs Island
runners.spotlight: Easy winner at Chepstow (2m, good) last October and he took a big step back in right direction when seventh of 23 in the County at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft); missed the Scottish Champion Hurdle last month due to heavy ground; still only five and he's 1lb lower than in the County; major player.
runners.comment: Shaped well when seventh of 23 in the County at Cheltenham; still only five; major player

runners.name: Moon Chime
runners.spotlight: In good form over hurdles last spring, winning at Stratford and Hexham (both 2m, good to soft); he's only 2lb higher than for the second of those wins, and a recent run on the Flat after six months off should have blown away the cobwebs; impossible to discount in first-time tongue-strap.
runners.comment: Recent Flat run should have blown away the cobwebs; possibilities on last spring's form
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

races.race_name: William Hill Oaks Trial Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Romantic Symphony
runners.spotlight: Sister to winners including Yibir (7f-2m including US Grade 1; RPR 123) and Wild Illusion (1m/1m2f including Group 1, also Oaks second; 116); 10-11 when getting the better of a pretty useful rival at Newmarket (1m, good) last September, preceding another novice win at much shorter odds in good style for six-runner race at Kempton (1m3f, AW) one month ago; lots of potential.
runners.comment: 2-2 in novice events at Newmarket (1m, good) and Kempton (1m3f, AW); lots of potential

runners.name: Amora Queen
runners.spotlight: Won last summer on her first two starts, before failing to beat a rival in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket (33-1); second of six when favourite for a handicap at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) two weeks ago put her back on the right track and she's likely to stay; it's also the best RPR in this line-up but it would still be surprising if she's not vulnerable.
runners.comment: Last in Group 1 Fillies' Mile; 2nd in 1m2f handicap at Doncaster is the best RPR in this

runners.name: Bloom
runners.spotlight: By Wootton Bassett out of a 9.5f Listed winner who's sister to Irish Guineas winner Roderic O'Connor; won a maiden at Cork (1m, yielding) last September and probably improved again when front-running third of seven in Group 3 at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding) two weeks ago; top trainer so often gets these 3yo middle-distance fillies to improve.
runners.comment: Front-running third of seven in Group 3 at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding) two weeks ago
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Prizeland
runners.spotlight: Sister to 2022 Derby second Hoo Ya Mal; made an eye-catching debut (good to soft) before her novice win at Kempton (1m, AW) later in October; 4-7 for another novice here (1m4f, AW) last month and easily dismissed her only three rivals in slowly run race; faces radically different opposition today and returns to turf but she's promising, for sure.
runners.comment: Won AW novice events over 1m and 1m4f; radically different opposition but she's promising

runners.name: Cameo
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett half-sister to a 1m4f Listed winner; won 7f maiden at the Curragh before below-form favourite in a 1m Group 3 later in October; fifth of ten in another 1m Group 3 there on reappearance was her best form and suggested she's a middle-distance filly; those last three runs were in the mud, which is most unlikely here, but another step forward otherwise looks assured; Ryan Moore rides her for the first time.
runners.comment: Reappearance fifth in 1m Curragh Group 3 was her best form; looks a middle-distance filly
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% British EBF Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Zgharta
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in 1m2f Newmarket maiden last May; the Sandringham over C&D last June was a brutal start to her handicap career but a strong-finishing fifth of 12 at Glorious Goodwood (1m, good) next time, when blocked in her run, suggested this mark was within reach; had excuses on last two starts and has a big run in her under favourable conditions.
runners.comment: Has won at 1m2f but a stiff 1m can suit and this mark isn't beyond her; can go well

runners.name: Song N Dance
runners.spotlight: Limited action so far but she ended 2025 in fine fettle, finding plenty to win a 7f Leicester novice (good) and was not at all hard pressed to win on handicap debut at Newcastle (1m, AW); useful comeback run when runner-up on Southwell AW in April and should come on for the outing; further improvement likely.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; progressive at 7f and 1m on turf and AW; has more to come; major player

runners.name: Jannas Journey
runners.spotlight: Scottish yard won a Group 1 at Royal Ascot from just two course runners in 2025; this one had a season to dream of last year, winning seven at 6f-1m, but only once at the latter trip; this stiff track will test her and she's 4lb higher than the latest win in September; tall order.
runners.comment: Had a season to dream of in 2025, winning 7; still 4lb higher than the latest in September

runners.name: Deedaydiva
runners.spotlight: Just three runs but her Sandown win (7f, soft) on second start turned out very useful form with the second doing really well after and the next three also successful since; her comeback third in a Nottingham novice (about 1m, good) was perfectly satisfactory; interesting newcomer to handicaps.
runners.comment: Just 3 runs; Sandown 7f win was good form; satisfactory return in April; handicap debut

runners.name: Radiant Beauty
runners.spotlight: Progressive in 2025, coming right away for a 1m Redcar win on yard debut before following up on Kempton AW (7f); races freely and saw too much daylight at Newmarket (1m, good) in October but, hanging on well for third, she lost nothing in defeat; up against it in a Listed AW race at Kempton in March but it was a good return; has another handicap in her.
runners.comment: Progressive in 2025, latterly for this yard; pleasing return in AW Listed race; solid

runners.name: Politely
runners.spotlight: Complete turnaround on two modest turf runs when upped to about 1m on AW, well clear at Lingfield then winning with something to spare on handicap debut at Wolverhampton recently; up 6lb but she can rise further.
runners.comment: Progressive since upped to 1m on AW; up 6lb for ready handicap debut win; can rise further

runners.name: Electrifarhh
