runners.spotlight: Won Southwell maiden (1m4f, AW) on debut last autumn and best of the rest behind superstar hurdler Constitution Hill in Kempton novice (1m4f again) on his return in March; on the back of that he was sent off 11-10 favourite for his 2m Newbury handicap and turf debut three weeks ago but pulled too hard; retains potential back down in trip, but the outside stall has the potential to make life awkward.
runners.comment: Promise over 1m4f on AW first two starts; too keen over 2m since; retains potential

runners.name: Opportunity
runners.spotlight: Edged out St Leger runner-up Rahiebb in Haydock novice (11.6f, good) last spring; badly hampered when well-held fourth to Amiloc in a Listed race at Goodwood (1m3f) next time and not too hard to forgive a heavy defeat behind that same rival in the King Edward VII over C&D at the Royal meeting (good to firm); off since (gelded); a very interesting handicap newcomer, particularly if the betting speaks in his favour.
runners.comment: Unexposed handicap newcomer from top yard; market confidence could prove significant

runners.name: Whatsgoingonmarvin
runners.spotlight: A useful 1m5f Flat winner for Sir Mark Prescott last year; only fair form at best in four hurdle outings for this yard since, including when well-held sixth on Stratford handicap debut in that sphere four weeks ago; returns to the Flat with something to prove; first-time cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Bit disappointing over hurdles for this yard ahead of Flat return in headgear

runners.name: Serenity Blue
runners.spotlight: Pair of 1m2f novice wins on good to firm at the start of last summer; disappointed in handicaps on final two starts last year but got his career back on track when third of nine in 1m4f Kempton handicap on reappearance 33 days ago; subsequently gelded; surprise if he's reached his limit after six starts.
runners.comment: Two 1m2f turf novice wins at 3; solid third in 1m4f AW handicap on return; gelded after

runners.name: Too Soon
runners.spotlight: Largely in good nick last season but winless since 2024 and well held on 1m2f Doncaster reappearance (good to soft) six weeks ago.
runners.comment: No win since 2024 and well beaten on reappearance

runners.name: Hermetic
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground winner (1m4f/2m) in France for Christophe Ferland last autumn; new connections picked him up for 105,000euros in November; no surprise were he to develop into another very useful handicapper for this yard but perhaps best watched this time unless the betting speaks in his favour.
runners.comment: Dual soft-ground winner in France; joined good yard but might be best watched this time

runners.name: In The Breeze
runners.spotlight: Won at Pontefract (1m4f, good to soft) for Mick Appleby last September and bounced back from below-par efforts to end 2025 when making a successful start for the Henrietta Knight stable over 1m4f at Thirsk (good) four weeks ago; did that readily but this looks significantly tougher than that five-runner affair.
runners.comment: Winning start for Henrietta Knight at Thirsk last month but this is a much stronger race

runners.name: Siempre Arturo
runners.spotlight: The winner of eight of his 22 starts, the latest success coming at Kempton (1m4f) off a 2lb lower mark; ended 2025 with a couple of lesser runs and might be best watched on return.
runners.comment: Has healthy 8-22 record but below par at end of 2025 and might be best watched on return

runners.name: Wine Dark Sea
runners.spotlight: Winning handicap debut at Kempton (1m4f, AW) last August; didn't fire in the same way back at Kempton (1m3f) next time but resumed his progression in no uncertain terms when close second in valuable 3yo handicap at Newmarket (1m4f, good) on final start, form which was strongly boosted last weekend by the winner (Yabher) and third (Daiquiri Bay); could have a big say if the betting suggests he's ready to roll after 211 days off.
runners.comment: Form of Newmarket second on final 3yo start was strongly boosted last weekend; player

runners.name: Al Wasl Storm
runners.spotlight: Came up well short at Group level last summer but resumed his progression back in calmer waters with a 1m4f Newbury handicap debut success (good to firm) last August; this low-mileage sort has the potential for better again in 2026.
runners.comment: Resumed progression with 1m4f Newbury handicap win last August; more to come in 2026

runners.name: Boatswain
runners.spotlight: His progress stalled at the end of last season but returns as a still relatively unexposed 4yo who could kick on again in 2026; betting informative.
runners.comment: Returns as a still relatively unexposed 4yo who could kick on again in 2026
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Kirchner
runners.spotlight: Showed useful form across five starts in France for Jean-Claude Rouget but not seen since finishing fourth over 1m4f just under a year ago; changed hands for 72,000euros last July; has joined an excellent stable for his British career but an opening mark of 92 isn't obviously generous; has been gelded and now equipped with first-time cheekpieces; the betting should provide more clues.
runners.comment: Useful French recruit for James Owen but off for nearly a year; the betting should guide

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Handicap

runners.name: Dragon Leader
runners.spotlight: Made all in very valuable auction race as a juvenile but winless since taking Redcar's Two-Year-Old Trophy six weeks later; looked to be rediscovering his level when third on AW in March and it was a solid fifth of 18 behind Royal Velvet (declared for preceding Group 3) at Newmarket (7f, good) last time; if the stands' rail proves the place to be this could be the day to revert to forcing tactics; interesting.
runners.comment: No win since 2023 but ran well latest and drawn to bag the near rail; interesting

runners.name: Back In Black
runners.spotlight: Scored on his reappearance at Newbury (7f) last April; string of good efforts followed from August onwards and he posted a career best when fourth of 24 in Newbury's Spring Cup (1m, also good) on his return three weeks ago; stall 1 might not be ideal but otherwise holds strong claims.
runners.comment: Career best when fourth on his return three weeks ago; doesn't know how to run a bad race

runners.name: Foreseen
runners.spotlight: The winner of three of his last six starts (1m, heavy and AW) and ran another solid race when close fourth of ten here (AW) in March; also acts on good ground but this is hotter than the races he usually contests.
runners.comment: The winner of three of his last six starts; this is hotter than his normal patch, though

runners.name: Christian David
runners.spotlight: Novice and handicap (7f) winner on AW here, latterly in December when sporting first-time blinkers (retained); respectable ninth of 21 when 66-1 for the Lincoln in March and it was another solid effort when third in Class 2 handicap (1m, good) at Newmarket's Craven meeting; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Respectable ninth in Lincoln and third at Newmarket's Craven meeting; can't be ruled out

runners.name: The Dragon King
runners.spotlight: Spectacular late run to land valuable auction race (6f, good to soft) in October 2024 is starting to fade from the memory but he won his first race since when taking a Wolverhampton handicap in November; narrow defeat at Newcastle (also 7f, AW) in February was up with his best form and respectable efforts on last two starts; not dismissed back on turf.
runners.comment: Narrow defeat (7f, AW) in February was up with his best form; not dismissed back on turf

runners.name: Mr Swivell
runners.spotlight: Good second of 16 (AW) on his stable debut/reappearance last April and went on to land similar events in June and July; turf no issue and was second of 17 at Ascot (also 1m, soft; acts on good to firm) in September; career-high mark but still respected on this return.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner last year; career-high mark but acts on turf and respected on this return

runners.name: Apotheosis
runners.spotlight: Defied penalty in 3yo novice here (good) in 2024; back from a 145-day break and wind surgery when landing Class 2 at Newcastle (also 7f, AW) in December and beaten a neck at Abu Dhabi (1m, good) in March; career-high mark but still considered.
runners.comment: 7f 3yo winner here; beaten neck at Abu Dhabi latest; career-high mark but still considered

runners.name: Sword
runners.spotlight: Patchy form for David O'Meara last term but won in June (good to firm; first-time hood) and ran a corker when third of 21 in valuable Ascot handicap (good) in July; back in form when slightly unlucky second at Newcastle (also 7f, AW) in October and sold for 40,000gns later that month; hood (absent when disappointing on final start) missing again; tongue tied for stable debut and warrants a market check.
runners.comment: Capable in a hood when on a going day last term; makes yard debut in tongue-tie this time
